Educn.—Education. Uncov.—Uncovenanted.
Engr.—Engineer. Univ.—University.
Engrg.—Engineering. Vaccn.—Vaccination.
Estabt.—Establishment.
Exam.—Examination.

RECORD OF SERVICES.

ABBOTT, EVELYN ROBINS, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Bath Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 30th Oct., 1893, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

ABBOTT, HENRY BYAM, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., June, 1861; asst. rest., Aden, 1865; from Oct., 1869, served as asst. agent, Rajputana; pol. asst., Manpura, and dep. Bhil agent, 1875-76; supt. of Jhalawar, March, 1876; pol. agent, Alwar, Jan., 1891; rest., W. states of Rajputana, April, 1892; offg. gov.-gen. agent in Rajputana, March, 1895.

ABBOTT, HERBERT EDWARD STACY, D.S.O., Major, R.E., India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 16th Sept., 1878, and was posted to the Punjab; on field service during Afghan war, 1880-81 (medal); exec. engr., Jan., 1885; field engr. in Hazara expedn., 1888 (medal and clasp); and in Miranzai expedn., 1891 (clasp); under sec. to Punjab govt., public works dept., May, 1893; D.S.O., Jan., 1896.

ABDUL GHAFUR KHAN OF ZAIDA, Punjab Commn.—Apptd. prob. under native civil service rules, 14th Feb., 1889; asst. commr., Dec., 1891.

ABRAHAMS, BARNETT LIONEL, B.A., Finl. Dept., India Office.—Educ. at the City of London Sch. and Balliol Coll., Oxford (scholar, 1888-92; Jenkyns exhibr., 1892; Arnold prize, 1894); junr. clerk, 21st Feb., 1893; précis writer, Dec., 1894.

ABU TURAB KHAN, Agha, Statutory C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Apptd. prob. asst. mag. and collr. under the statutory civil service rules, 16th April, 1889; confd., 8th Aug., 1892.

ACKLOM, ROBERT EVATT, Bombay Police Dept.—Joined the service, 18th April, 1867, as asst. district supt. of police; supt., Rajputana-Malwa railway police, May, 1883.

ACWORTH, HARRY ARBUTHNOT, C.I.E., B.L., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Brighton Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 31st Oct., 1870, and served as asst. collr. and mag., dep. municipal commr., and dep. collr. of salt rev.; member of mixed commn. at Goa, 1884-85; municipal commr. for city of Bombay, April, 1890; C.I.E., May, 1895; editor of the Bombay Rev. Handbook, 3rd edn., and author of 'Ballads of the Marathas,' 1894.

ADAMS, GEORGE, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Christ's Hosp.; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 30th Dec., 1861, and

served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., dep. commr., and mag. and collr.; commr. in Oudh, Nov., 1889; commr., Benares, Aug., 1890; Lucknow, April, 1893; junr. member, board of rev., April, 1894; also member of provl. legisl. council, June, 1894.

ADAMS, JOHN, Bombay Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 13th May, 1869; asst. engr., Nov., 1871; exec. engr., Sept., 1881; architectural exec. engr. and surveyor, Jan., 1882; is a fellow of Bombay univ.

ADAMSON, CHARLES HENRY ELLISON, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Burma Commn.—First commn., royal artillery, Jan., 1868; joined the Burma commn., Dec., 1871, and served as asst. mag., asst. commr., and prest. of municipal committee, Rangoon; dep. commr., June, 1885; C.I.E., May, 1889; commr., Jan., 1892.

ADAMSON, HARVEY, M.A., Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at the Gymnasium, Old Aberdeen, and King's Coll., Aberdeen; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 17th Dec., 1877, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and settl. officer; dep. commr., April, 1888; acted as commr. in 1888, 1894, and from June, 1895.

ADDIS, DAVID FOULIS, M.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at the Univs. of Glasgow and Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 14th Nov., 1872, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag., asst. settl. officer, and dep. commr.; mag. and collr., July, 1890; district and sessions judge, March, 1895.

ADDIS, HENRY BAWN, late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 25th Jan., 1871, as asst. engr., and was employed on the Nimach, W. Rajputana and other railways; exec. engr., April, 1878; ret'd., Aug., 1894.

AGNEW, PATRICK DALREAGLE, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 1st Dec., 1889, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and asst. settl. officer.

AGNEW, WILLIAM FISCHER, B.L., Jud. Dept., Burma.—Reporter for Indian law reports, high court, Calcutta, from Feb., 1877; law lecturer, presidency coll., 1879; advocate, high court, Calcutta, 1879; recorder of Rangoon, March, 1884; offd. as judge, high court, Calcutta, in 1885 and 1886; author of 'The Law of Patent,' 'The Statute of Frauds,' 'The Law of Trusts in India,' 'The Criminal Procedure Code,' &c.

AHSANUDDIN AHMAD, B.L., Statutory C.S. (Bengal).—Apptd. asst. mag. and collr. under the native civil service rules, 3rd Jan., 1882; confd., June, 1887; joint mag. and dep. collr., March, 1893.

AIKMAN, ROBERT SMITH, M.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Madras Coll., St. Andrew's, and St. Andrew's Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1867, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; on famine relief duty in Bengal, 1874; from March, 1884, served in the jud. dept.; offd. as puisne judge, high court, in 1892, and from Dec., 1894.

AINSLIE, WILLIAM, late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury Coll., 1845; arrived, 7th Nov., 1845; served in Bengal as asst. and joint mag. and dep. collr. in Cuttack; specially employed in 1850-51 and 1851-52 in the suppression of Meriah sacrifices in the Kandh districts; mag. of Rangpur, July, 1852; railway commr., E. Indian railway, 1856-57; mag. and collr., Bhagalpur, March, 1857; railway commr., E. Bengal railway, 1858, and subsequently of the S.E. railway also; from Aug., 1863, served in the jud. dept., and was apptd. civil and sessions judge of Patna, Jan., 1864; prest., tea commn., 1867-68; judge of the high court, Calcutta, May, 1873; ret'd., April, 1880.

AITCHISON, SIR CHARLES UMPHERSTONE, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., LL.D., D.C.L., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at the Univ. of Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1855; arrived, 24th Sept., 1856, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. commr. and mag.; transf'd. to the Punjab, May, 1857; personal asst. to jud. commr., July, 1858; offg. under sec., govt. of India, foreign dept., Nov., 1859; dep. commr., Punjab, Jan., 1865; offg. commr., Lahore, Aug., 1867; offg. sec. to govt., Punjab, Nov., 1867; from April, 1868, served in the foreign dept., govt. of India, as offg. sec., and was confd. in this appt., Aug., 1870; on special duty in thagi and dakaiti dept., Oct., 1877; chief commr., British Burma, March, 1878; provisional member, govr.-gen.'s council, July, 1880; lieut.-govr., Punjab, April, 1882; member, govr.-gen.'s council, April, 1887, to Nov., 1888; M.A. (hon.), Oxford, June, 1895; has publ. 'Manual of the Criminal Law of the Punjab,' 1860; 'Treaties, Engagements, and Sanads relating to India and the Neighbouring Countries; and 'The Native States of India.'

ALCOCK, JOHN BACKHOUSE, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 10th Nov., 1876, and served as asst. collr. and mag., and railway mag.; from Jan., 1884, served as asst. and joint judge and sessions judge; judge and sessions judge, March, 1894.

ALEXANDER, ERNEST BRUCE, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Repton; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 1st Nov., 1871, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., asst. settl. officer, junr. sec. to the board of rev., joint mag. and dep. collr., and dep. commr.; mag. and collr., Oct., 1889; in 1894 acted as sec. to govt.; jud. dept.; acting commr., May, 1895; author of 'Gazetteer of Gorakhpur.'

ALEXANDER, JAMES WILLIAM, India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, June, 1865; asst. engr., Dec., 1866; asst. sec. to govt. of India, public works dept., Jan., 1868; exec. engr., March, 1870; suptg. engr., Feb., 1890.

ALEXANDER, Moses, Store Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 3rd March, 1888.

ALEXANDER, NATHANIEL STUART, late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury Coll.; arrived, 10th July, 1858; from Oct., 1859, served in the Bengal rev. and jud. depts., and was apptd. mag. and collr., 1st grade, Jan., 1879; commr., Dacca, Aug., 1884; Bhagalpur, Dec., 1885; and Bardwan, Aug., 1887; ret'd., March, 1890.

ALGIE, WILLIAM, B.A., B.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. apprentice engr., 19th April, 1875, and posted to Burma; asst. engr., April, 1876; exec. engr., May, 1885; employed on irrign. works in Egypt, 1885-86.

ALISON, SIR ARCHIBALD, Bart., G.C.B., Gen.—First commn., ensign, 72nd foot, 3rd Nov., 1846; served in the Crimean campaign of 1855, and was present at siege of Sevastopol (medal and clasp and Turkish medal); served in the Indian Mutiny, and was mil. sec. to the commander-in-chief, Aug., 1857, to March, 1858; was present at relief of Lucknow (arm lost, medal and clasp, and C.B.); in staff employment, adjutant-gen.'s dept. at headquarters, and in the south western district, and at Aldershot, 1862-73; served in the Ashanti war of 1873-74 as brigadier-gen. of European brigade (thanks of both houses of parliament, medal with clasp, and K.C.B.); dep. adjutant-gen., headquarters, Ireland, 1874-77; commandant, staff college, 1878; dep. quartermaster-gen., intelligence branch, 1878-82; served in the Egyptian expdn. of 1882 as major-gen. in command of troops at Alexandria, and on the Mahmoudiyeh canal, and, in command of 3rd brigade, 2nd divn., at battle of Tel-el-Kebir (thanks of both houses of parliament, medal with clasp, bronze star, and 2nd class Osmanieh); promoted lieut.-gen. for distinguished service, Nov., 1882; commanding divn., Aldershot, Aug., 1883, to Feb., 1885; and again, Oct., 1885; adjutant-gen. to the forces, Feb.-Sept., 1885; G.C.B., June, 1887; member of the council of India, Jan., 1889.

ALLEN, BASIL CPLESTON, B.A., Indian C.S. (Assam).—Educ. at Haileybury and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 14th Dec., 1893, and served in Assam as asst. commr. and personal asst. to chief commr.

ALLEN, CHARLES FRANCIS EGERTON, B.A., B.L., late Jud. Dept., Burma.—Called to the bar (Inner Temple), 1871; admitted advocate of high court, Calcutta, Nov., 1873; law examr., Calcutta univ., Dec., 1874, and again in 1875 and 1876; acting judge of the Calcutta court of small causes, 1875-76; lecturer on English law, presidency coll., Calcutta, Feb., 1876, and profir. of law (temporary), May, 1877; served as govt. advocate, Rangoon, and acted as recorder of Rangoon for various periods; M.P. for Pembroke district, 1892-95.

ALLEN, CHARLES GEORGE HILLERSDEN, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Haileybury and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 16th Dec., 1885, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and settl. officer; joint mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1893.

ALLEN, DAVID BIRD, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Eton, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 5th Nov., 1873, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., asst. to dir.-gen. of statistics, and under the dir. of the agricl. dept., May, 1885; joint

mag. and dep. collr., Sept., 1887; mag. and collr., Aug., 1890.

ALLEN, JEFFERY JULIAN, M.I.E.E., India Telegraph Dept.—Joined the service as asst. supt., 26th Nov., 1868; supt., Aug., 1881; dir., traffic branch, May, 1895.

ALLEN, PERCY UNDERHILL, B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch., Cheltenham Coll., and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1835; arrived, 21st Nov., 1888, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., April, 1892.

ALLEN, THOMAS TAYLOR, late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Queen's Coll., Cork; apptd. after exam. of 1859; arrived, 7th Feb., 1861, and served in the Bengal rev. and jud. depts.; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1866; district and sessions judge, March, 1875; supt. and remembrancer of legal affairs, Feb., 1883; served for various periods as member of legisl. council; ret'd., Nov., 1894.

ALLEN, WALTER GRIFFITH, India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1874, and employed on state railways; exec. engr., May, 1883; in charge of Kalka-Simla railway survey, 1894; offg. dep. consulting engr. for railways, Lucknow, 1894.

ALLEN, WILLIAM, late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch., and St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 10th Oct., 1868, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; junr. collr. and mag., Nov., 1889; senr. collr. and mag., Nov., 1891; ret'd., Sept., 1893.

ALLISON, HAZLETT, M.D., Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., Madras Med. Dept.—Joined the service as surgn., Sept., 1873; arrived, 29th April, 1874, and served on mil. duty; fort surgn., Fort St. George, prof'r. of anatomy in the med. coll., and med. inspr. of seamen, Jan., 1888; fellow of Madras univ., Aug., 1892.

AMBICA CHARANSEN, M.A., Statutory C.S. (Bengal).—Apptd. under the native civil service rules asst. mag. and collr., 21st Jan., 1884; confd., 19th Feb., 1886; joint mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1893.

AMEER ALI SYED, C.I.E., M.A., B.L., Bengal Jud. Dept.—Educ. at Calcutta Univ.; called to the bar (Inner Temple), 1873; presidency and chief presidency mag., 1878-81; member of the Bengal legisl. council, 1878-79, and 1881-83; member of the supreme legisl. council, 1884-85; Tagore law prof'r., 1884; C.I.E., 1887; judge of the high court at Calcutta, April, 1890; author of the 'Tagore Law Lectures for 1884.'

AMEER HOSSEIN, SYED, C.I.E., Nawab, Bengal Jud. Dept.—Joined the service as offg. dep. mag. and dep. collr., 29th Aug., 1864; confd., 5th Feb., 1867; member of Bengal legisl. council, April, 1878; member of central exam. committee, April, 1886; addl. member of govr.-gen.'s council, Dec., 1886, and again Dec., 1888; C.I.E., Jan., 1888; presidency mag., Calcutta, Nov., 1888; acted as coroner of Calcutta in 1888-89; and as inspr.-gen. of registrn. in 1893 and 1894.

AMIRTA LAL CHATTERJEE, B.L., Bengal Jud. Dept.—From 19th May, 1862, served in the subord. jud. service, Bengal, and was apptd. subord. judge, April, 1873.

AMOS, ROBERT, Customs Dept., Bengal.—

Served in the dept. from 4th Dec., 1876, and was apptd. asst. collr. and head appraiser, Calcutta, July, 1882.

ANDERSON, ALEXANDER, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Gordon's Hosp., New Gr. Sch., and King's Coll., Aberdeen; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 4th Nov., 1872, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and forest settlt. officer; dep. commr., March, 1889.

ANDERSON, FRANCIS CHARLES, Indian C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at Edinburgh Acad. and Repton Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 31st Oct., 1871, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr., personal asst., and asst. sec. to the chief commr.; commr. of excise, supt. of stamps, and inspr.-gen. of registrn., Feb., 1882; sec. to chief commr., Jan., 1891; commr., Jabalpur, April, 1894; prepared an excise manual for the Central Prov., 1885.

ANDERSON, FRANCIS GEOFFREY HARTNELL, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Bath Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1893, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

ANDERSON, GEORGE ARTHUR, India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1875, and posted to Central India; transf. to state railways, Jan., 1878; exec. engr., Oct., 1885; offg. dep. consulting engr. for railways, Bombay, June, 1886; services lent to Bengal-Nagpur railway co., Nov., 1887.

ANDERSON, HENRY AIKEN, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Edinburgh Acad. and Repton Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 13th Nov., 1873, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; asst. pol. officer, Kuram, Aug., 1880; dep. commr., May, 1889; acted as commr. and supt., Derajat, in 1892; on special duty with Waziristan delimitation commn. in 1894, and from Feb. 1895.

ANDERSON, JAMES, M.A., B.L., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 14th Nov., 1861; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag., dep. commr., and mag. and collr.; on special duty connected with the Wahabi case, June, 1870; ret'd., 1888.

ANDERSON, JAMES ARTHUR, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Joined the dept. as asst. engr., 13th Dec., 1871; exec. engr., Dec., 1876; employed on the Sind-Pishin, Indus valley and E. Bengal railways; engr.-in-chief, Kachmir railway survey, Oct., 1890; supt., way and works, E. Bengal railway, July, 1892; suptg. engr., Oct., 1892.

ANDERSON, JAMES DRUMMOND, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Rugby; apptd. after exam. of 1873; arrived, 14th Nov., 1875, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. transf. to Assam, Jan., 1880, and served as asst. commr., and asst. sec. to chief commr.; dep. commr., April, 1889; returned to Bengal as mag. and collr., April, 1894; author of vocabularies of the Tippera-Deori-Chutia and Aka languages, and a collection of Kachari folk stories.

ANDERSON, JOHN ALEXANDER, B.L., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at the Gr. Sch. and Univ. of Aberdeen, and was also student of the Inner Temple; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1874, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; district judge, April, 1889; divnl. judge, June, 1894.

ANDERSON, JOHN W. M., Col., late Rev. Survey Dept., Bombay.—First commn., Jan., 1855; from Nov., 1863, served in the rev. survey dept., Mysore, as asst. supt. and dep. supt.; from April, 1881, served as supt. of the Sind settlt. survey; ret'd., March, 1888.

ANDERSON, ROBERT HILDEBRAND, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 7th Dec., 1876, and served as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; district and sessions judge, Oct., 1893; acted as jud. commr., Chota Nagpur, in 1895.

ANDREW, JAMES, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at the Royal Sch., Armagh, and Kings-town Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 17th Nov., 1879, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settlt. officer; head asst. to collr. and mag., N. Arcot, April, 1888; India principal asst., Ganjam, Dec., 1891.

ANDREWS, WILLIAM, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 30th March, 1876.

ANLEY, GEORGE AUGUSTUS D'AUVERGNE, late Public Works Dept., Bengal.—Apptd. exec. engr., 25th Jan., 1861; served in the Bengal public works dept., and temporarily under the municipality of Calcutta; offg. asst. to chief engr. and asst. sec. to the govt. of Bengal, April, 1876; suptg. engr., March, 1882; ret'd., Nov., 1888.

ANNESLEY, ROBERT, Major, late Jud. Dept., N.W. Prov.—Entered the army in 1855, and served in the Crimean and Abyssinian campaigns; ret'd. in 1869; joined the service, 4th Jan., 1871, and served as asst. district supt. of police; cantonmt. mag., June, 1877; judge, small cause court, June, 1880; 1st class cantonmt. mag., Oct., 1886; ret'd., April, 1895.

ANSON, GEORGE WEMYSS, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Oudh Commn.—First commn., 11th Jan., 1867; private sec. and A.D.C. to lieut.-govr., N.W. Prov., 1871, and 1875-78; from April, 1872, served as offg. cantonmt. mag. and offg. asst. commr.; confd. in latter appt., Sept., 1875, and in the former, Sept., 1877; agent, Balrampur estate, June, 1886; in addn. cantonmt. mag., Aug., 1887; agent and dep. commr., Nov., 1890.

APJOHN, JAMES HENRY, M.A., M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 22nd Dec., 1868, as asst. engr., and was employed on irrign. works in Bengal; exec. engr., Sept., 1873; services lent to Calcutta port commrs., March, 1887.

ARBUTHNOT, SIR ALEXANDER JOHN, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., late Madras C.S., Member of the Council of India.—Educ. at Rugby and Haileybury Coll., and apptd. to the Madras civil service in 1842; arrived, Sept., 1842; served as asst. collr. and mag., Malayalam translr., sec. to the coll. and univ. boards, and dep. and acting registrar to the sadar court; dir. of public instrn., March, 1855; chief sec. to govt. and member of legisl. council, Oct., 1862; member of Madras council, Oct., 1867, to Oct., 1872; temporary govr. of Madras in the absence of Lord Napier, Feb.-May, 1872; ret'd. from the Madras civil service, Oct., 1874; member of govr.-gen.'s council, June, 1875, to April, 1880, and acted as prest. in 1878 and 1879; vice-chancellor of the univ. of Madras, Oct., 1871, and of the univ. of Calcutta, Oct., 1878; counsellor of the Empress of India, Jan., 1877; apptd. member of the council of India, 1st Nov., 1887; vice-prest., 1893-94; author of various publns. on educ. and other subjects in connection with India, among which

are 'Reports of Selected Cases determined in the Court of Foujdari Adawlut,' 1851; 'Papers relating to Public Instruction in Madras,' 1855; 'A Memoir of Sir Thomas Munro,' 1881; &c.

ARBUTHNOT, SIR CHARLES GEORGE, G.C.B., Gen., R.A.—First commn., royal artillery, June, 1843; served in the Crimean campaign, 1855 (medal and clasp, 5th class Medjidie, Turkish medal); dep. adjutant-gen., royal artillery, in Bengal, 1873-77; acted as inspr.-gen. of ordnance, 1877-78; inspr.-gen. of artillery in India, Sept., 1878, to July, 1880, during which period he served as brigadier-gen. in the operations in Afghanistan (medal, K.C.B.); with the headquarters of the army in England, 1880-83; inspr.-gen. of artillery in England, 1883-85; prest., ordnance committee, 1885-86; commander-in-chief, and member of council, Bombay, Feb. to Dec., 1886; commander-in-chief, and member of council, Madras, Dec., 1886, to March, 1891; G.C.B., May, 1894.

ARBUTHNOT, ROBERT EDWARD VAUGHAN, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at the Merchant Taylors' Sch., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 15th Nov., 1892, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

ARBUTHNOTT, JOHN CAMPBELL, Indian C.S. (Assam).—Educ. at Fettes Coll., Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 21st Dec., 1879, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to Assam, Feb., 1883, and served as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Nov., 1890.

ARCHER, CHARLES, Capt., I.S.C., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., 9th Sept., 1882; entered civil employ, 8th Aug., 1887, as acting asst. agent, Baluchistan; confd., May, 1889; asst. sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., April, 1891; pol. agent in Zhib, Jan., 1894.

ARDAGH, SIR JOHN CHARLES, K.C.I.E., C.B., Col., R.E.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; entered royal engrs. as lieut., 1st April, 1859; attached to the special embassy during the congress at Berlin, June, 1878, and was made a C.B. (civil), July, 1878; apptd. asst. to commr. for the delimitation of the frontier of Bulgaria, Sept., 1878; commr. for the demarcation of the Greek frontier, June, 1881; served in the Egyptian campaign of 1882-84, including the operations at Alexandria, and the battle of Tel-el-Kebir (medal with clasp, bronze star, and 4th class of Osmanich); served in the Soudan in 1884, commanding royal engrs. and chief of the intelligence dept. (battles of Teb and Tamai; 2 clasps); C.B. (mil.), May, 1884; served in Soudan expdn., 1884-85; commandant of base at Cairo, and in the expdn. of 1885-86 with the frontier field force; received the 3rd class of the Medjidie; private sec. to the Marquess of Lansdowne, govr.-gen. of India, from Dec., 1888, to Jan., 1894; C.I.E., Jan., 1892; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1894; commandant of sch. of mil. engrg., Chat-ham, April, 1895.

ARGYLL (DUKE OF), HIS GRACE GEORGE DOUGLAS CAMPBELL, K.G., K.T., P.C.—Born in 1823; succeeded to the dukedom in 1847; chancellor of the univ. of St. Andrew's, 1851; lord privy seal, 1852; postmr.-gen., 1855; K.T., 1856; lord privy seal, 1859; in 1860 he was again apptd. postmr.-gen., and subsequently lord privy seal; sec. of state for India, Dec., 1868, to Feb., 1874; lord privy seal, May, 1880; resigned in April, 1881; K.G., 1884; elected rector of the

univ. of Glasgow, Nov., 1854; presided over the Glasgow meeting of the British association, 1855; prest. of the royal society of Edinburgh, 1861; is hereditary master of the Queen's household in Scotland, a trustee of the British museum, and hereditary sheriff and lord lieut. of Argyllshire; has publ. 'The Reign of Law,' 'Primeval Man,' 'The Eastern Question from the Treaty of Paris to the Treaty of Berlin, and to the second Afghan War,' &c.

ARMSTRONG, FRANCIS BUSFEILD, Finl. Dept., India Office.—Educ. at Cheltenham Proprietary Coll., and King Edward's Sch., Birmingham; junr. clerk, finl. dept., 30th March, 1869; senr. clerk, Sept., 1883.

ARMSTRONG, JOHN ARTHUR, Lieut.-Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., June, 1860; joined the dept., Nov., 1865, and served in the Punjab as asst. engr., and in the Hazara campaign, 1868, in charge of field telegraph; exec. engr., Sept., 1869; asst. to chief engr., and asst. sec. to govt., April, 1877; served in Kashmir on special road work, 1881-82; suptg. engr., March, 1886; ret'd., March, 1887.

ARMSTRONG, JOHN SCOBELL, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Marlborough Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 3rd March, 1866, and served in the rev. and jud. depts. in Bengal; dep. collr. of customs, Calcutta, Dec., 1877; mag. and collr., and collr. of customs, Calcutta, April, 1883; port commr., Calcutta, Nov., 1887; ret'd., July, 1892.

ARNOLD, GEORGE FREDERICK, B.A., Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 1st Jan., 1894, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

ARNOTT, JAMES M.D., C.M., Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., Med. Dept., Bombay.—Joined the Indian med. service, 1st April, 1867; arrived, 25th Oct., 1867, and was employed under the mil. dept. till Aug., 1868, including service in the Abyssinian expedn. (medal); and subsequently as asst.-surgn. in the David Sassoon hosp., Poona, and in the European gen. hosp., Bombay, and as prof. of pathology in the Grant med. coll.; surgn., European gen. hosp., Bombay, March-April, 1882; prof. of midwifery, Grant med. coll. and obstetric physician, Jamsetji Jijibhoy hosp., April, 1882; physician, European gen. hosp., May, 1891.

ARNOTT, NAPOLEON, Lieut.-Col., R.E., Mil. Works Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., 22nd Dec., 1865; arrived, 13th Dec., 1868, and served in Bengal and the Punjab on irrign. duty; exec. engr., Jan., 1879; offd. as sec. to agent to govr.-gen., Baluchistan, and as sec. to defence committee in 1882; employed on special defence works, Aug., 1887; suptg. engr., Dec., 1889; dep. dir.-gen., mil. works, Sept., 1890; transf. to Madras command as chief engr., March, 1894.

ARTHUR, OSWALD GEORGE, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Winchester and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 8th Nov., 1890, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.

ARTHUR, SIGISMUND RAYNOR, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Charterhouse, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 19th Nov., 1888, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settlt. officer.

ARUNDEL, ARUNDEL TAGG, B.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 18th Nov., 1865, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; private sec. to govr. of Madras, July, 1875; prest. of the municipal commn., Madras, June, 1881; fellow of the Madras univ., March, 1882; principal asst. to collr. and mag., Vizagapatam, Oct., 1884; district and sessions judge, Kistna, Dec., 1884; collr. and mag., Kistna, Aug., 1887; member, board of rev., and member of legisl. council, Jan., 1894.

ARUNDELL, EDWARD WILLIAM, B.A., F.C.H., India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1874, and employed on surveys and other railway work in the Punjab; exec. engr., May, 1883; dep. consulting engr. to govt. of India for railways, central divn., May, 1887; Lucknow, Nov., 1894.

ASHBURNER, LIONEL R., C.S.I., late Bombay C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1848; served in the rev. and jud. depts. in Broach, Surat, and Kaira; on the outbreak of the Mutiny was apptd. supt. of police, Kaira, and organised a body of horse and foot for the protection of the eastern frontier; special commr. for trial of rebels, 1858; collr. and mag., Khandesh, 1861; rev. and police commr., N. divn., 1872; member of council, Bombay, Aug., 1877; acted as govr., 13th March to 28th April, 1880; ret'd., 1883.

ASHTON, GEORGE, Finl. Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 7th April, 1884.

ASHWORTH, ERNEST HORATIO, B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch. and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 11th Dec., 1894, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.

ASTON, HENRY FAURE, B.L., Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at the S. African Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 9th Oct., 1869, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., railway mag., and cantonmt. mag.; 1st grade asst. judge and sessions judge, Aug., 1883; jud. asst., Kathiawar, March, 1885; judge and sessions judge, Nov., 1886; on special duty in connection with settlt. cases, Baroda, in 1888; offg. jud. commr., Lower Burma, April, 1894.

ATA-ULLAH KHAN, MIRZA, Sardar Bahadur, Hon. Lieut.-Col.—British agent at Kabul, June, 1885; ret'd., June, 1891.

ATKINS, CHARLES HERBERT, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at King Henry VIII's Sch., Coventry, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 30th Nov., 1891, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

ATKINS, JOHN DE COURCY, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch., London Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1878; arrived, 9th Nov., 1880, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settlt. officer; junr. sec. to govt., educ., &c., depts., April, 1895.

ATKINSON, FREDERIC JAMES, Finl. Dept., Govt. of India.—Joined the service as prob., 8th July, 1873, and served as district grain acctnt. in Bengal, and as asst. acctnt.-gen., in Bombay, Bengal, and N.W. Prov.; asst. comptr., Burma, Dec., 1883; dep. acctnt.-gen., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, June, 1891.

ATKINSON, JOHN NATHANIEL, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll.; apptd.

after exam. of 1876; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1878, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and asst. sessions judge; on special duty under the commr. of salt rev., April, 1885; head asst. to collr. and mag., Sept., 1886; sub-collr. and joint mag., Jan., 1891.

ATLAY, Ven. B. T., M.A., late Ecclesi. Dept., Bengal.—Arrived, 17th Oct., 1867; chaplain of St. Paul's Cathedral and of the gen. hosp., Dec., 1867; senr. chaplain, Oct., 1877; senr. chaplain, St. Paul's Cathedral, and chaplain of the gen. hosp. and presidency jail, Jan., 1882; commissary to the lord bishop, and offg. chaplain in charge of the diocese, March, 1883; archdeacon of Calcutta, Sept., 1883; ret'd., 1889, and was apptd. vicar of Willesden.

ATUL CHUNDER DUTT, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at the Free Church Instn., Calcutta, and at Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1837; arrived, 14th Jan., 1890, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

AUCOTT, ELLIOTT, Funds Dept., India Office.—Clerk, and class, 27th Feb., 1886.

AULAD HUSSAIN, C.I.E., Khan Bahadur, Central Prov. Commn.—Joined the service, 29th July, 1844; served in various subord. posts till Dec., 1864, when he was apptd. extra asst. commr.; C.I.E., Jan., 1882; asst. commr., Sept., 1883; settlt. officer, Jan., 1885; also asst. commr., June, 1890.

AUSTIN, ROBERT FREDERICK, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch. and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 23rd Dec., 1894, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

AUSTIN, WARE PLUMPTRE, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Gloucester Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 28th Dec., 1864, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr., and sub-collr. and joint mag.; district and sessions judge, Oct., 1884, and again in 1888; collr. and mag., Nov., 1886; collr. of sea customs, and of the Madras district, protector of emigrants, supt. of stamps and stationery, and chairman of the harbour trust board, Nov., 1891.

AYLING, WILLIAM BOCK, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Weymouth Coll. and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 30th Jan., 1839, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

BADCOCK, FRANCIS WILLIAM, late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Harrow Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 24th Nov., 1869, and served in Bengal as asst. mag., joint mag., and dep. collr., and settlt. officer; offg. inspr.-gen. of registrn., March, 1879; joint mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1881; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1883; ret'd., March, 1895.

BADDELEY, WILLIAM LEWIS CLINTON, Major, R.E., Madras Public Works Dept.—Joined the dept., 23rd July, 1874; asst. engr., Aug., 1875; exec. engr., May, 1883; dep. chief engr. and under sec., public works dept., Nov., 1886, to March, 1891.

BADEN-POWELL, BADEN HENRY, C.I.E., late Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 31st Oct., 1861; asst. commr., May, 1862; from July, 1866, to April, 1869, served as small cause court judge, Lahore, and subsequently in the forest dept.; addl. commr., Lahore and Rawalpindi, Oct., 1881; dep. commr., Dec., 1882;

judge, chief court, Punjab, March, 1889; ret'd., May, 1889, but employed for a time under govt. of India, rev. and agricl. dept.; has publ'd. 'Punjab Products,' 'Punjab Manufactures,' 'Manual of the Land Revenue System and Land Tenures of British India,' and 'Manual of Jurisprudence for Forest Officers.'

BADGLEY, WILLIAM FRANCIS, Col., I.S.C., late Survey of India.—First commn., April, 1857; arrived, May, 1857; served during the Mutiny in Central India and Oudh, and afterwards with the Lushai expdn. in charge of a survey party (medals and clasp); apptd. to the topogr. survey, May, 1865, and was employed in Rewa and Bundelkhand, and in the Khasi, Garo, and Naga Hills; dep. supt., Dec., 1880; in charge of Gujarat survey, March, 1884; offg. asst. surveyor-gen. in charge of photo. and litho. offices, March, 1886; ret'd., June, 1889.

BADOCK, HENRY WALTER, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office.—Junr. clerk, 11th Feb., 1876; senr. clerk, June, 1893.

BAGLEY, FREDERICK ROBERT, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd., 16th Sept., 1871, and was employed on road works in the Central Prov., and on famine relief works in Bengal; transf'd. to state railways, and employed in Burma on railway survey and construction; exec. engr., May, 1883.

BAGNELL, HENRY WILLIAM JAMES, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Eastbourne Coll., Keble Coll., Oxford, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 1st Nov., 1882, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settlt. officer.

BAGSHAW, CECIL, Forest Dept., Haidarabad Administration.—Joined the service as temporary sub-assst. conservator, 26th Oct., 1869; asst. conservator, 14th Aug., 1871; dep. conservator, Oct., 1876; employed in the mil. dept. in the Kuram valley, Aug., 1879, and at the forest sch., Dehra Dun, Dec., 1879; conservator, Jan., 1889.

BAILEY, FREDERICK, Lieut.-Col., R.E., late Forest Dept., N.W. Prov.—Arrived as a lieut., 12th Nov., 1864; served under the mil. dept. till July, 1867, when he was apptd. asst. engr.; exec. engr., June, 1871; from Aug., 1871, served in the forest dept. as dep. conservator and supt. of forest surveys; conservator of forests, and dir. of Indian forest sch., in addn. to his duties as supt. of forest surveys, Sept., 1878; ret'd., April, 1892; is lecturer on forestry at Edinburgh univ., and sec. to royal Scottish geographical society.

BAILEY, RICHARD JONES, India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd., 25th Oct., 1872, and posted to the mil. works branch in the Punjab; transf'd. to Burma, Jan., 1881; exec. engr., May, 1883.

BAILLIE, DUNCAN COLVIN, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Inverness Acad., Glasgow Acad., and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 10th Nov., 1879, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., asst. commr., and asst. settlt. officer; joint mag., Sept., 1880; provl. supt. of census operations, April, 1890, and prepared the report; dep. commr., April, 1894.

BAINBRIDGE, GEORGE, M.D., Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., Bombay Med. Dept.—Joined the service, 1st April, 1867; arrived, 25th Oct.,

1867, and served in Bombay as civil surgn. and in various acting appts.; civil surgn., Karachi, Dec., 1891.

BAINES, JERVOISE ATHELSTANE, C.S.I.; late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Rugby, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 12th Nov., 1870, and served as asst. collr. and mag., asst. pol. agent, and cantonmt. and railway mag.; dep. supt. of census operations in Bombay presidency, and under sec. to govt., gen. dept. (census), April, 1880; 2nd asst. collr. and forest settl. officer, Dec., 1882; on special duty to supervise the work under the income tax act in the Bombay presidency, May, 1886; also acting as dir. of agric. from Dec., 1886; 1st asst. collr. and mag., Oct.–Nov., 1886; served under the govt. of India as census commr. for India from Aug., 1889; on depn. to England to complete census report, May, 1892; on depn. at India office, July, 1893, and prepared decennial report on moral and material progress of India, 1891–92; C.S.I., Jan., 1894; sec. to royal commn. on opium, June, 1894, to March, 1895; retd., Sept., 1895.

BAIRD, ANDREW WILSON, F.R.S., Lieut.-Col., R.E., Mint Dept., Calcutta.—Lieut., royal engrs., Dec., 1861; arrived, March, 1863, and served in the Bombay public works dept. as asst. engr., and was employed on the harbour defences and reclamations; exec. engr., Oct., 1867; served in the Abyssinian expdn. as asst. field engr., 1868 (medal); from Dec., 1868, served in the trigon. survey as asst. surveyor, and was employed in Kathiawar and Gujarat; asst. supt., Oct., 1871; in July, 1877, was apptd. to the general superintendence and control of tidal observations upon the Indian coasts; on depn. as commr., Venice geographical congress and exhibn., Aug., 1881; dep. supt., July, 1882; mint master, Calcutta, Aug., 1889; author of various papers and reports on tidal observations, and other works; and joint author of the annual tide tables for Indian ports.

BAKER, EDWARD NORMAN, Indian C.S.—Educ. at Christ's Coll., Finchley; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 15th Nov., 1878, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec., govt. of Bengal, finl. dept., May, 1885; acted as under sec., govt. of India, fin. and commerce dept., in 1885; dep. commr., June, 1890; dep. sec. to govt. of India, fin. and commerce dept., Sept., 1892.

BAKER, FRANCIS, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Berkhamstead Gr. Sch. and Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 13th Nov., 1869, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and first asst. sec. to govt.; private sec. to lieut.-govr., N.W. Prov., April, 1874; junr. sec., board of rev., May, 1878; under sec. to govt., May, 1881; supt. of Dehra Dun, March, 1884; joint mag., Aug., 1886; dep. commr., March, 1888; mag. and collr., March, 1889; offg. commr., Allahabad, April, 1895.

BAKER, HARRY VICTOR SAMPSON, India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1875, and employed on irrign. works in the Punjab; exec. engr., May, 1883; under sec. to govt., Punjab, public works dept., 1892–94.

BAKER, MONTAGU BERNARD, late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at Winchester, and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived,

29th Nov., 1864, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and in Sind as acting jud. commr. and judge of the sadar court; judge and sessions judge, May, 1882; retd., June, 1891.

BAKER, OSWALD, Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., Med. Dept., Burma.—Joined the Bengal med. service, 1873; was employed under the mil. dept. up to May, 1874, and served subsequently in Burma as civil surgn. and supt. of jail; med. officer to the mission to Siam under Major C. W. Street, Feb., 1879; offg. inspr.-gen. of jails for Burma, May, 1887; joint med. officer, Simla, Sept., 1889; junr. civil surgeon, Rangoon, Nov., 1890.

BALMOKAND, A.M.I.C.E., M.I.M.E., Rai Bahadur, India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. apprentice engr., Oct., 1873, and posted to the Punjab; asst. engr., Feb., 1875; exec. engr., Dec., 1884.

BALTHASAR, GUSTAVE, Indian C.S. (Assam).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch. and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 29th Nov., 1893, and served in Assam as asst. commr.

BAMBER, HENRY WILLIAM JOHN, late Police Dept., Bengal.—From 1st Oct., 1857, to May, 1862, served as asst. supt., and supt. of salt chowkies; from May, 1862, served in the Bengal police dept., and was promoted to district supt., 1st grade, Aug., 1885; retd., July, 1891.

BANERJEE, DURGAGATI, C.I.E., Rai Bahadur, Bengal Administration.—Joined the service, Aug., 1865, and held various subord. posts till April, 1889, when he was apptd. dep. collr. of Calcutta, supt. of excise rev., &c.; C.I.E., Jan., 1895; member of provl. legisl. council, March, 1895.

BANERJEE, GOOROO DASS, M.A., D.L., Jud. Dept., Bengal.—Educ. at the Presidency Coll., Calcutta, and Calcutta Univ.; asst. lecturer on mathematics at that coll., 1865; law lecturer, Berhampur coll., 1866–72; Tagore law prof., 1878; judge, high court, Calcutta, Jan., 1889; member of Bengal legisl. council, 1888; vice-chancellor of Calcutta univ., 1890–92; author of 'The Hindu Law of Marriage and Stridhan.'

BANKS, SAMUEL HENRY O'BRIEN, F.R.C.S., Surgn.-Col., Bombay Med. Dept.—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1866, and arrived, 17th April, 1867; served in the Abyssinian campaign, and afterwards on civil, mil., and jail duty; presidency surgn., Nov., 1888; acted as principal med. officer, Karachi, 1893.

BANNERMAN, PATRICK WILSON, Col., I.S.C., late Pol. Dept., Govt. of India.—Ensign, Bombay army, June, 1852; asst. to the supt., Nimach, July, 1859; asst. to govr.-gen.'s agent, Central India, 1860; pol. asst., Bhopawar, Aug., 1865; also Bhil agent and commandant, Malwa Bhil corps; pol. agent, Rewa, March, 1871; pol. agent, Bhopal, 1880; rest., E. states, Rajputana, Oct., 1881; rest., Gwalior, March, 1882; acted as agent to govr.-gen. for Central India in 1883–84, and again in 1887; reverted to mil. dept., 1888.

BANTON, WILLIAM HERBERT PHILIPPE, Registry and Record Dept., India Office.—Writer, India office, 5th Aug., 1875; clerk specially attached, 26th March, 1893.

BARADA CHARAN MITRA, M.A., Statutory C.S. (Bengal).—Apptd. under native civil service rules asst. mag. and collr., 30th March, 1886; confd., 23rd March, 1888; asst. settl. officer, Orissa, March, 1891; joint mag. and dep. collr., Tippera, April, 1894.

BARBOUR, SIR DAVID MILLER, K.C.S.I., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Queen's Coll., Belfast; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 20th Dec., 1863, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec., govt. of India, finl. dept., Aug., 1873; acctnt.-gen., Punjab, and dep. commr., paper currency, Lahore circle, June, 1877; offg. sec., govt. of Bengal, finl. dept., April, 1878; acctnt.-gen., Madras, and commr. of paper currency, Jan., 1880; acctnt.-gen., Bengal, Sept., 1881; offg. sec., govt. of Bengal, rev. and gen. depts., Jan., 1882; Bengal legisl. council, Jan. to Oct., 1882; sec., govt. of India, dept. of fin. and commerce, July, 1882; C.S.I., Feb., 1887; on special duty in England as member of the royal commn. on currency in 1886; finl. member of council of govr.-gen., Nov., 1888, to Nov., 1893; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1880; member of royal commn. on finl. relations between Great Britain and Ireland, May, 1894; author of 'The Theory of Bimetallism.'

BARCLAY, ERNEST, JUD. Dept., Madras.—Served from 18th Sept., 1861, as French interpreter to the supreme court, Madras, acting dep. clerk of the crown and acting asst. registrar; apptd. govt. solicitor, Feb., 1876.

BARCLAY, PRINGLE DAVID, India Public Works Dept.—Joined the dept., March, 1875, as asst. traffic supt., Rajputana state railway; traffic supt., Punjab N. railway, March, 1881; E. Bengal railway, June, 1884.

BARKLEY, DAVID GRAHAM, M.A., B.L., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Queen's Coll., Belfast; apptd. after exam. of 1857; arrived, 29th Dec., 1857, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Punjab as asst. mag. and collr., asst. commr., personal asst. to commr., Ambala, and asst. sessions judge; dep. commr., Oct., 1874; addl. commr., Delhi, May, 1876, and subsequently at Jalandhar and Lahore; offd. as judge, chief court, Punjab, 1878 and 1880; civil and sessions judge, Nov., 1880; commr. and supt., Multan, Sept., 1881; addl. judge, chief court, Jan., 1882; addl. member of govr.-gen.'s council, 1883 and 1884; judge of the chief court, Punjab, Feb., 1886; ret'd., Jan., 1887; has publ'd. 'Directions to Rev. and Settlt. Officers, Non-Regulation Law.'

BARLOW, SIR RICHARD WELLESLEY, Bart., late Madras C.S.—Apptd. from Hailebury, 1855, and served in the rev. and jud. depts., Madras; from 1871 to 1878 served as collr. and mag. of Chengalpat, and subsequently as commr., Nilgiris, 1878-80, and 1881; major commandant Nilgiri volunteer rifles, 1878-79; acted as member of the board of rev. in 1880, and again in 1881 and 1886; collr. and mag., and addl. sessions judge, Nilgiris, Feb., 1882; collr. of sea customs, &c., Madras, Jan., 1883; acting rest., Travancore and Cochin, Feb. to Aug., 1884; member of the legisl. council, Madras, Sept., 1885; chairman of the harbour trust board, May, 1886; supt. of stamps and stationery, April, 1887, in addn.; ret'd., Nov., 1889.

BARNES, FRANCIS CARNAC, Stamps and Stationery Dept., Bengal.—Served in the finl. dept., govt. of India, from 9th Nov., 1872; asst. to acctnt.-gen., N.W. Prov., Jan., 1877; asst. compr.-gen., Calcutta, Nov., 1878; private sec. to lieut.-govr. of Bengal, April, 1882; supt. of stamps and stationery, Bengal, April, 1887.

BARNES, HERBERT CHARLES, B.A., Indian C.S. (Assam).—Educ. at Westminster Sch. and

Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 13th Dec., 1894, and served in Assam as asst. commr.

BARNES, HUGH SHAKESPEAR, Indian C.S., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—Educ. at Malvern Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 30th Oct., 1874, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; transf'd. to finl. dept. of govt. of India, Dec., 1876, and apptd. asst. acctnt.-gen. in the N.W. Prov.; private sec. to the finl. member of the govr.-gen.'s council, Dec., 1876; acted as under sec. in the home dept. in 1878; on special duty under the foreign dept., Nov., 1879; asst. to rest., Kandahar, Nov., 1880; served in the Afghan campaign, 1881, as pol. officer with Gen. Wilkinson's brigade; asst. to the govr.-gen.'s agent in Baluchistan, 1881-83; pol. agent in Quetta and Pishin, June, 1883; in charge of arrangements, supplies, &c., for passage of Afghan boundary commn. across Baluch desert, Aug., 1884; under sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., May, 1890; rev. commr., Baluchistan, Dec., 1890; offd. as govr.-gen.'s agent and chief commr. in 1891 and 1892; rest., Kashmir, March, 1895.

BARR, DAVID WILLIAM KEITH, C.S.I., Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., Sept., 1864; served in Abyssinian expdn. (medal); boundary commr., Rewa Kantha, and Malwa Bhil agency, 1869; pol. asst. and boundary officer, Bhopal, July, 1869; asst. to govr.-gen.'s agent, Central India, 1870; offg. pol. agent, Jodhpur, 1878-79; pol. agent, Bhagelkhand, July, 1881; also supt., Rewa state; rest. at Gwalior, May, 1887; rest. in Kashmir, Nov., 1893; govr.-gen.'s agent in Central India, March, 1895; C.S.I., Jan., 1896.

BARRETT, EDWIN CLIFFORD, Survey of India Dept.—Apptd. proby. asst. surveyor, Indus-Sind rev. survey, 18th Nov., 1862; asst. supt., Dec., 1868; accompanied Lushai expdn., 1871-72 (medal and clasp); dep. supt., Oct., 1884; supt. (new scale), Aug., 1892.

BARRON, ALBERT HENRY, India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 27th May, 1868, as asst. engr., and was employed on irrig. work in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., Sept., 1875.

BARRON, CLAUD ALEXANDER, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at the Aberdeen Gr. Sch., Aberdeen Univ., and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 12th Nov., 1892, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

BARROW, FRANK, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Oudh Commn.—Educ. at Bruton Gr. Sch., Victoria Coll., Jersey, and Cheltenham Coll.; first commn., 6th Nov., 1863; from Aug., 1869, served as asst. commr. in Oudh, offg. junr. sec. to the chief commr., and offg. cantonmt. mag.; dep. commr., June, 1880.

BARROW, FRANK HUNTER, late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Bruton and Felsted Gr. Schs., and Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 17th Jan., 1870, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1881; mag. and collr., March, 1887; ret'd., Aug., 1894.

BARROW, OSCAR THEODORE, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Gloucester Coll. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1877, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf'd. to finl. dept., govt. of India, Dec., 1882; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bombay,

March, 1887; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, April, 1887; offg. acctnt.-gen. and dep. commr., paper currency, N.W. Prov., May, 1888; acctnt.-gen., Bengal, April, 1891; acctnt.-gen., Bombay, March, 1894.

BARROW, REGINALD POCOCK, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Oxford Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 13th Nov., 1885, and served in Sind as asst. collr. and mag., 2nd asst., and forest settlt. officer.

BARRY, THOMAS DAVID COLLIS, Surgn.-Capt., Bombay Med. Dept.—Joined the dept., 27th May, 1889, as acting chemical analyser, profs. of chemistry and med. jurisprudence, Grant med. coll., and profs. of chemistry, Elphinstone coll.; confd. in these posts, April, 1892.

BARRY, WILLIAM, M.R.C.S., late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at the High Sch. and Univ. of Glasgow, and Univ. of Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 14th Feb., 1863, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; employed on famine duty in Bengal, 1873-75; joint mag. and dep. collr., Banda, Aug., 1877; from May, 1881, served in the jud. dept. and was apptd. district and sessions judge, Nov., 1882; ret'd., March, 1890.

BARSTOW, HENRY CLEMENTS, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 31st Oct., 1861, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; served under the govt. of India, foreign dept., Jan., 1874, to Oct., 1875, as offg. pol. agent, Bhopal; offg. supt., Dehra Dun, Feb., 1876; mag. and collr., Cawnpore, April, 1880; ret'd., Nov., 1886.

BARTER, JOSEPH FRANCIS, Brigade-Surgn., late Madras Med. Dept.—Joined the service, July, 1859; from April, 1862, served in the Central Prov. as civil surgn. and supt. of jail, and acted for various periods as inspr.-gen. of jails; sanitary commr., supt. of vaccn., and inspr.-gen. of dispensaries, Dec., 1878; civil surgn. and supt. of lunatic asylum, Nagpur, July, 1883; ret'd., April, 1890.

BARTHOLOMEUSZ, MATHEW LORENZ, M.B., C.M., Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., Bombay Med. Dept.—Joined the service, 30th Sept., 1873, and served on mil. duty, and as med. officer at Zanzibar; civil surgn., Sukkur, April, 1884; transfed. to Nasik, June, 1888; civil surgn., supt. of lunatic asylum, &c., Ahmadabad, Nov., 1889.

BARTHOLOMEW, ROBERT, Lieut.-Col., Bengal Cavalry, late Punjab Commn.—Arrived 2nd April, 1862; from 1st Aug., 1866, served in the Punjab commn. as asst. commr.; jud. asst., Sept., 1881; district judge, 1st grade, Delhi, offg. as divl. judge, Nov., 1884; dep. commr., July, 1886; ret'd., April, 1894.

BARTLETT, HUBERT FREDERICK, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 25th Oct., 1871, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; transfed. to the N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Jan., 1880, and served as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; mag. and collr., July, 1889.

BARTLETT, THOMAS WALTER, M.I.C.E.,

India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 31st Jan., 1871, as asst. engr., and was employed on Rajputana state railway; exec. engr., Jan., 1880; supt. of works, Bolan railway, April, 1885; services lent to Bengal-Nagpur railway co., Jan., 1888; transfed. to Madras, Nov., 1889, and employed on Villupuram-Guntakal and E. Bengal state railways.

BARTON, WILLIAM PELL, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Bedford Modern Sch. and Uniy. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 17th Dec., 1894, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

BASANTA KUMAR MULLICK, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch., and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 13th Jan., 1890, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

BATCHELOR, STANLEY LOCKHART, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at St. Edmund's Coll., Ware, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 1st Dec., 1889, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

BATE, THOMAS ELWOOD LINDSAY, Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., Jail Dept., Punjab.—Joined the service, 31st March, 1875; arrived, 14th Nov., 1875; served in mil. dept. to Nov., 1880, when he was apptd. civil surgn., Gujarat; inspr.-gen. of prisons, Punjab, April, 1891.

BATTEN, HALLET GEORGE, Burma Commn.—Joined the service, 17th Oct., 1869, and on 13th Feb., 1871, was apptd. extra asst. commr., Burma; asst. commr., Nov., 1877, and dep. commr., May, 1888; served also as cantonmt. mag. and judge of small cause court in 1886-87.

BATTEN, JOHN KAYE, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Haileybury Coll., and at Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 29th Nov., 1887, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; special asst., Malabar, Nov., 1893; head asst., Coimbatore, Jan., 1895.

BATTY, HERBERT, M.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 31st Oct., 1870, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., profs. of logic and moral philosophy in the Deccan coll., Poona (1872-75), and asst. judge and sessions judge; under sec. to govt. of Bombay, pol., &c., depts., and sec. to the legisl. council, Nov., 1885; acting chief presidency mag., Bombay, April, 1888; remembrancer of legal affairs, June, 1892; member of legisl. council, June, 1893, and again, July, 1895.

BAXTER, BERNARD, India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd., 1st Nov., 1871, and employed on state railways in Sind and Baluchistan; transfed. to Bengal, April, 1881; exec. engr., May, 1883; employed in Burma, April, 1888; and in Madras, on E. Coast railway, from Nov., 1889.

BAYLAY, CHARLES ALLEN, Col., I.S.C., late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—Lieut., Bengal artillery, Dec., 1857; asst. commr., Berar, Jan., 1866, and subsequently in Ludhiana; pol. asst., Rajputana, 1868-70; asst. gen. supt. for thagi and dakaiti, Dec., 1870; pol. agent, Haraoti and Tonk, 1876; pol. agent, Kotah, Dec., 1879; acted as rest., W. states of Rajputana, in 1884; ret'd., May, 1890.

BAYLEY, CHARLES STUART, B.L., Indian

C.S.—Educ. at Harrow and Heidelberg; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 31st Dec., 1877, and served as asst. mag. and collr. in Bengal, offg. asst. sec. to chief commr. of Assam, July, 1880, and offg. under sec. to govt. of Bengal; member of exec. committee, Calcutta international exhibn. of 1883-84; offg. registrar, high court, March, 1884; under sec., govt. of India, rev. and agricl. dept., April, 1885; asst. commr., Ajmir, Sept., 1886; pol. agent, Bikanir, June, 1888; joint mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1889; pol. agent, Bikanir, Sept., 1891; offg. gen. supt., thagi and dakaiti dept., April, 1894; addl. private sec. to viceroy, Dec., 1894.

BAYLEY, SIR LYTTELTON HOLYOAKE, Kt., B.L., late Jud. Dept. Bombay.—Called to the bar (Middle Temple), May, 1850; apptd. advocate-gen., Bombay, 17th March, 1866; judge of the high court, Bombay, June, 1869; acted as chief justice for various periods; ret'd., June, 1895; Kt., Jan., 1896.

BAYLEY, SIR STEUART COLVIN, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., late Bengal C.S., Council of India.—Apptd. from Haileybury; arrived, 4th March, 1856, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., junr. sec. to govt., and mag. and collr.; commr., Dacca divn., July, 1873, and Patna divn., Sept., 1873; sec., govt. of Bengal, May, 1877; addl. sec., govt. of India, finl. dept., Aug., 1877; personal asst. to the viceroy, for famine affairs, Sept., 1877; addl. sec., govt. of India, public works dept., famine branch, in addn., Dec., 1877; sec., govt. of India, home dept., June, 1878; offg. chief commr., Assam, June, 1878; offg. lieut.-govr. of Bengal, July-Nov., 1879; chief commr. of Assam, June, 1880; ret., Haidarabad, March, 1881; member of govr.-gen.'s council, May, 1882; lieut.-govr. of Bengal, April, 1887; ret'd., Dec., 1890; apptd. sec., pol. and secret dept., India office, Dec., 1890; member of council of India, Sept., 1895.

BAYLY, WILLIAM GOODENOUGH, B.A., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. to the accts. branch as asst. controller, 28th July, 1868; dep. controller, Nov., 1871; asst. examr., April, 1872; dep. examr., July, 1877; examr., Jan., 1882; examr., public works accts., Burma, Feb., 1889; ret'd., Aug., 1893.

BAYNE, CHARLES GERWIEN, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at Uppingham, and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1878; arrived, 27th Dec., 1880, and served in British Burma as asst. commr.; junr. sec. to chief commr., April, 1886; under sec., April, 1887; dep. commr., Nov., 1888; junr. sec. to chief commr., Dec., 1890; sec., March, 1891; offg. chief sec., March-July, 1892; rev. sec., March, 1893; resumed his appt. as sec., Jan., 1895.

BEACHCROFT, CHARLES PORTER, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Rugby, and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 29th Nov., 1892, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

BEACHCROFT, FRANCIS PORTEN, B.A., B.L., late Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Rugby, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 12th Nov., 1864, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; jud. asst., April, 1878; dep. commr., Dec., 1883; divl. judge, Nov., 1884; offd. as judge of chief court, Punjab, in 1890 and 1891; ret'd., Nov., 1891.

BEADON, CECIL, Lieut.-Col., Madras Cavalry, late Punjab Commn.—Arrived, 24th March, 1858;

from May, 1862, served in the Punjab commn. as asst. commr., cantonmt. mag., and jud. asst.; dep. commr., March, 1877; offd. as commr. and supt. at various periods; reverted to mil. dept., Oct., 1893.

BEAMAN, FRANK CLEMENT OFFLEY, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch., and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 22nd Dec., 1879, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; from April, 1883, served in the jud. dept.; special settlt. officer, Baroda, 1886 and 1887; asst. judge, April, 1890; jud. asst. to the pol. agent in Kathiawar, May, 1891.

BEAMES, JOHN, late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury; arrived, 16th March, 1858; from March, 1859, served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and from Dec., 1861, in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; mag. and collr., July, 1867; commr., Bardwan, Nov., 1881; offg. member of board of rev., 1887 and 1889; commr., Bhagalpur, Aug., 1887; prest. of the police revision committee, Aug., 1890; commr., Presidency divn., Nov., 1891; ret'd., March, 1893; author of 'Outlines of Indian Philology,' 1867, 'Comparative Grammar of the Modern Aryan Languages of India,' 1872-79, and 'Grammar of the Bengali Language,' 1891; edited Sir H. M. Elliot's 'Races of the N.W. Provinces of India,' 1869.

BEAN, MAURICE, Capt., R.I.M., Port Officer, Bombay.—Joined the service as asst. port officer, Bombay; offg. port officer, Madras, April, 1891; port officer, Bombay, Oct., 1891.

BEARD, HENRY GOODLAKE, Store Dept., India Office.—Educ. at the City of London Sch.; writer, India store depôt, 5th June, 1868; clerk, 2nd class, Aug., 1878.

BEATSON, JOHN FULLARTON, C.I.E., B.A., M.D., Surgn.-Gen., late Bengal Med. Service.—Educ. at Glasgow Univ.; asst. surgn., Bengal, 16th June, 1843; served with the army of the Sutlej, 1845-46 (medal and clasp), and in the Punjab campaign (medal); in charge of Allahabad gen. field hosp. during the Mutiny; garrison surgn., Allahabad, 1865-68; med. storekeeper, Ambala, 1868-69; dep. inspr.-gen., Meerut, May, 1869; surgn.-gen., Nov., 1875; C.I.E., Jan., 1878; ret'd., March, 1880.

BEATSON-BELL, NICHOLAS DODD, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Clifton Bank, St. Andrews, Edinburgh Acad., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 16th Dec., 1889, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

BEATTY, JOSEPH, Col., R.E., late Public Works Dept., Madras.—First commn., Dec., 1852; arrived, 6th Jan., 1855, and served in the Madras public works dept. as asst. engr. and exec. engr.; supervisor of the canals, Godavari, May, 1874; suptg. engr., Aug., 1877; ret'd., May, 1880.

BEATTY, THOMAS BERKELEY, B.A., M.D., Surgn.-Gen., late Bombay Med. Service.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the Bombay med. service, May, 1851; civil surgn., Karachi, July, 1853; supt. of vaccn., April, 1856; supt., Matheran, Dec., 1867; dep. surgn.-gen., Poona, Oct., 1876; principal med. officer, Malta expednry. force, 1878; surgn.-gen. with the govt. of Bombay, April, 1880; ret'd., March, 1885; author of various contributions on med. subjects in the transactions of the med. and physical society, Bombay.

BEAUMONT, THOMAS, M.D., F.R.C.S.I., Dep. Surgn.-Gen., late Madras Med. Dept.—Arrived, 13th Sept., 1857; served under the mil. dept. till Feb., 1864, when he was apptd. residency surgn., Indore; residency surgn., Haidarabad, June, 1881; also supt. of vaccn. and supt. of med. sch.; ret'd., Oct., 1885.

BEAUMONT, WILLIAM, late Rev. Survey Dept., Madras.—Joined the dept., 27th May, 1858, and served as asst. supt. and dep. supt.; member of reorganisation committee, Madras, March, 1871, and of board of examrs., Nov., 1882; ret'd., Nov., 1887.

BEAVAN, REGINALD, Col., I.S.C., late Survey of India Dept.—Ensign, 4th May, 1860; arrived, 11th June, 1860; in July, 1868, joined the topogr. survey dept. as asst. surveyor; served with various survey parties, and in July, 1880, organised and took charge of the Baluchistan topogr. survey; dep. supt., Nov., 1884; ret'd., June, 1892; served in S. Afghanistan, 1878 (medal); accompanied the Marri field force and the Bozdar field force in 1880 and 1881.

BECHER, ALFRED READ, India Public Works Dept.—Joined the dept., 23rd July, 1868; served in the Punjab as asst. engr. on the Sirhind canal; from April, 1873, served in the accts. branch, Punjab, as asst. controller, and dep. controller; transf'd. to Bombay, Nov., 1875; served subsequently in Haidarabad, Oudh, Rajputana, and Mysore; examr. of accts., Calcutta, June, 1886; dep. acctnt.-gen. and under sec. to govt. of India, July, 1893.

BECKETT, HENRY BARRON, Punjab Commn.—Served from 10th May, 1860, as asst. commr. and jud. asst.; dep. commr., Peshawar, Aug., 1884; district judge, Oct., 1885; dep. commr., Aug., 1886.

BEDFORD, CHARLES AUGUSTUS S., Bengal Non-Regulation Commn.—Joined the service, 25th July, 1864, as asst. commr.; civil asst. pol. officer, Lushai expedn., Feb., 1889; dep. commr., April, 1891.

BEDFORD, JAMES PERCH, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Clifton Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 14th Dec., 1889, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. acctnt.-gen., Nov., 1895.

BEESTON, ROBERT CHAPMAN, India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 30th Aug., 1873, and was employed on state railways in Central India and Burma; exec. engr., Feb., 1881; on special duty in Upper Burma in 1885-86; employed on Kashmir railway survey, Feb., 1891; and on N.W. line, Jan., 1893.

BEEBIE, ALFRED GLYNN, Lieut.-Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., July, 1861; arrived, Nov., 1870; joined the dept., Feb., 1871, as asst. engr. in the railway dept., Central India; exec. engr., Oct., 1871; Agra-Gwalior railway survey, Nov., 1872; offg. dep. controller of accts., Bengal, Aug., 1874; controller of accts., Mysore, Aug., 1875; examr., guaranteed railway accts., Calcutta, 1878; Punjab N. railway accts., 1880; dep. acctnt.-gen., and under sec. to govt. of India, public works dept., Jan., 1886; acctnt.-gen. and dep. sec., Oct., 1880; ret'd., July, 1893.

BEHARI LAL GUPTA, B.L., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Colootola Branch Sch., Calcutta, Calcutta Univ., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 29th Sept., 1871, and served in Bengal as asst. mag.

and collr.; on famine relief duty, Sept., 1874; presidency mag. and coroner, Calcutta, June, 1881; district and sessions judge, Nov., 1888.

BEIGHTON, THOMAS DURANT, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at King Edward's Sch., Birmingham; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 4th Dec., 1867; and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; on special duty in connection with boundary disputes on the Bhutan frontier, Jan., 1878; district and sessions judge, May, 1885; acted as supt. and remembrancer of legal affairs in 1894; member of provl. legis. council, March, 1895; author of 'The Ethnography of the Bhutan Frontier,' and two pamphlets.

BELCHAMBERS, ROBERT, Jud. Dept., Bengal.—Joined the service, 15th Sept., 1848; dep. registrar, Calcutta high court, Feb., 1851; registrar, Oct., 1862; is also keeper of records, taxing officer, acctnt.-gen., and sealer; author of 'Practice of the Civil Courts,' and other legal publns.

BELL, CHARLES ALFRED, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Winchester, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 3rd Nov., 1891, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. settl. officer.

BELL, HENRY JAMES, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen, Aberdeen Univ., and St. Peter's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 26th Nov., 1890, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.

BELL, HORACE, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. proby. asst. engr., 7th July, 1862; served in the Central Prov. and Central India, and was promoted to exec. engr., 1st grade, March, 1871; subsequently served on the Punjab, Rajputana, and Sindia and Nimach state railways; suptg. engr., Jan., 1880; transf'd. to Bengal, Nov., 1881; engr.-in-chief, Dacca and Maimansingh railway surveys, Dec., 1881; manager and engr.-in-chief, Tirhut state railway, Feb., 1884; acting dir., N.W. railway, July—Oct., 1887; engr.-in-chief, Great Western of India railway survey, Sept., 1888, and of the Moghal Sarai Howrah railway survey, Feb., 1889; consulting engr. to the govt. of India for railways, Aug., 1890; chief engr., Oct., 1890; offg. dir.-gen. of railways, and dep. sec., Jan. to April, 1893, when he reverted to his post of consulting engr.; ret'd., June, 1894; author of 'Laws of Wealth,' a work on pol. economy for native readers, and of 'Railway Policy in India.'

BELL, JAMES RICHARD, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 8th Oct., 1868, and served as asst. engr., exec. engr., and supt. of works in various projects; engr.-in-chief, Haidarabad-Umarkot railway survey, Jan., 1890; in charge of Mari extension of the Sind-Sagar branch of the N.W. railway, Feb., 1890; suptg. engr., Oct., 1890; engr.-in-chief of the Mari-Attock extension railway and frontier railway surveys, Dec., 1891; engr.-in-chief, E. Coast railway, May, 1893; consulting engr. to the govt. of India for state railways, June, 1894; rank of chief engr., July, 1894.

BELL, JOHN MACKENZIE, M.I.C.E., late Madras Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 23rd Dec., 1867, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Sept., 1877; ret'd., April, 1895.

BELL, MATTHEW DENNY, Indian C.S. (Madras).

—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1879; arrived, 25th Nov., 1881, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and joint mag., Dec., 1894.

BELL, WILLIAM, M.A., Educl. Dept., Punjab. —Joined the service, 16th Oct., 1885, as principal, central training coll., Lahore; prof. in the govt. coll. there, March, 1888; principal, April, 1892; author of a number of edul. works.

BELLASIS, EDWARD SKELTON, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1875, and employed on irrign. works in the Punjab; exec. engr., May, 1883.

BELLETTE, GEORGE, M.A., late Educ. Dept., Bengal.—Joined the service, 1st Feb., 1861; served successively as 2nd master, head master, and prof'r., Dacca coll.; inspr. of schools, Nov., 1868; prof'r., Presidency coll., Dec., 1873; principal, Berhampur coll., May, 1875; inspr. of schools, Nov., 1881; offg. dir. of public instrn., Jan., 1883; ret'd., April, 1893.

BELLINGHAM, HORACE COHEN, Finl. Dept., India Office.—Writer, India office, 18th May, 1875; clerk specially attached, Sept., 1892.

BENNETT, WILLIAM CHARLES, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 4th Dec., 1867, and served in Oudh as asst. commr. and asst. settlt. officer; manager of encumbered estates, Gonda, 1872-75; offg. sec. to govt., Oudh rev. dept., Sept.-Nov., 1879; dir. of agric. and commerce, N.W. Prov. and Oudh, April, 1891; sec., board of rev., May, 1886; sec. to govt., rev. and jud. depts., Nov., 1888; sec. in jud. dept., April, 1889; member of legisl. council, 1889-91; settlt. commr. and addl. commr., Oudh, July, 1894; author of 'History of the Chief Clans of the Rae Bareli District,' contributions to the Oudh gazetteer, and the Gonda settlt. report.

BENGER, ALFRED, India Audit Office.—Temporary clerk, 20th Nov., 1866; junr. asst., June, 1867; senr. examr., July, 1877; designation changed to senr. clerk, Oct., 1892.

BENNETT, JOHN, M.D., Brigade-Surgn., late Med. Dept., Punjab.—Joined the dept., 1865; from Sept., 1870, served in the Punjab as civil surgn. at various stations, jail supt., supt. of vaccn., and dep. sanitary commr.; med. adviser to the Maharaja of Patiala, Aug., 1885; ret'd., Sept., 1890.

BENSLAY, EDWIN CLEMENT, Surgn.-Major, late Bengal Med. Service.—First commn., 1859; served from 22nd July, 1863, as civil asst. surgn. at Midnapur and Rajshahi; civil surgn., Rajshahi, Oct., 1875; ret'd., Nov., 1885.

BENSON, CHARLES, M.R.A.C., Madras Rev. Dept.—Joined the service, 30th Jan., 1874, as asst. supt., Saidapet farms; asst. dir. of agric., April, 1884; asst. commr. of land records and agric., April, 1887; asst. dir. of that dept., Nov., 1887; dep. dir., Jan., 1892.

BENSON, RALPH SILLERY, M.A., LL.B., B.L., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Rathmines Sch., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 29th Oct., 1873, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; acted as head asst. collr. and mag. of Cuddapah during the famine of 1876-78; specially apptd. officer for the settlt. of lands in the Nilgiri districts; head asst., N. Arcot, June, 1882; sec.

to the local self-govt. committee, Oct., 1882; forest settlt. officer, May, 1883; acting under sec. to govt. in 1885 and 1887; acting sec. to the commrs. of land rev., 1887-88; sub-collr. and joint mag., Oct., 1887; district and sessions judge, Nov., 1889; collr. and mag., Feb., 1895; author of the settlt. registers and report, and descriptive memoirs, of the Nilgiri district.

BENSON, THOMAS, B.A., Indian C.S. (N. W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Tavistock Gr. Sch., Marlborough Coll., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 14th Nov., 1868, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., asst. settlt. officer, and manager of the Awa estate; asst. commr., Oudh, Oct., 1883; joint mag., Nov., 1883; district and sessions judge, April, 1887.

BENTINCK, BARON JOHN, late Punjab Commn.—Educ. at Leyden Univ. (doctor of Roman and Dutch law); joined the service, 6th Dec., 1869, as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Jan., 1892; ret'd., Dec., 1894.

BENTON, ALEXANDER HAY, M.A., late Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at the Univ. and King's Coll., Aberdeen; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 20th Feb., 1864, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Punjab as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; jud. asst., Nov., 1876; dep. commr., Jan., 1879; divl. judge, Nov., 1884; judge of chief court, Nov., 1889; ret'd., July, 1894.

BENTON, JOHN, India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1873, and employed on irrign. work in the Punjab; exec. engr., Oct., 1881.

BERESFORD, JOHN STUART, M.E., M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service as asst. engr., N.W. Prov., 26th July, 1867; exec. engr., Sept., 1872; personal asst. to chief engr., irrign., May, 1877, and 1884-85; suptg. engr., Dec., 1889; joint sec. to govt., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Aug., 1892; chief engr., Feb., 1895; sec. to chief commr., Central Prov., public works dept., June, 1895.

BERESFORD, LORD WILLIAM LESLIE DE LA POER, K.C.I.E., V.C., Col.—First commn., June, 1867; served in the Jowaki-Afridi expdn., 1877-78 (medal and clasp); also in the Zulu war of 1879 (V. C., medal, and clasp); and in the Afghan war, 1878-80 (medal and clasp); A.D.C. to viceroy of India, 1875-81; mil. sec. to viceroy, Dec., 1881 to Dec., 1893; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1894; served with the Burmese expdn. of 1886, as mil. sec. to govr.-gen. (clasp).

BERKELEY, JAMES CAVAN, C.I.E., Maj.-Gen., I.S.C., late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., Madras infantry, Oct., 1857; served from June, 1862, as asst. to the govr.-gen.'s agent in Central India; also as boundary settlt. officer, dep. opium agent in Malwa, asst. gen. supt. for thagi and dakaiti, and sessions judge and registrar for Mhow; pol. agent, Haraoti and Tonk, 1873; pol. agent, Baghclkhand, Jan., 1879; pol. agent, Jacobabad, 1882; offg. rest., Gwalior, March, 1883; on special duty, Kashmir, 1884; pol. agent, Bundelkhand, 1884; offg. rest., Nepal, 1885; rest. and govr.-gen.'s agent at Baroda, April, 1886; reverted to mil. dept., Nov., 1887.

BERNARD, SIR CHARLES EDWARD, K.C.S.I., late Bengal C.S., Rev. and Statistics Dept., India Office.—Apptd. to the Bengal civil service

from Haileybury; arrived, 12th Feb., 1858, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under sec., govt. of India, finl. dept., Aug., 1861; from Oct., 1862, served in the Central Prov. as judge of the small cause court, Nagpur, settl. officer, sec. to the chief commr. (July, 1864), commr. of Nagpur (Feb., 1869) and acting jud. commr.; from May, 1871, to Jan., 1875, served in Bengal as member of legisl. council, and acting sec. to govt., first in the jud., then in the rev., and lastly in the scarcity and relief dept.; from Nov., 1876, served as commr. of Nagpur divn.; sec. to Sir Richard Temple, depd. on a special mission to Bombay and Madras, Jan., 1877; jud. commr., Central Prov., Aug., 1877; addl. sec. to govt. of India in the dept. of rev., agric., and commerce, Aug., 1877, and in the finl. dept., Dec., 1877; sec. to govt. of India in the home dept., May, 1878, and in rev. and agricl. dept., July, 1881; from July, 1880, acted as chief commr. of British Burma; confd., April, 1882; rettd., March, 1888, and was apptd. sec. in the rev. and statistics dept., India office; in 1893 edited Sir George Campbell's 'Memoirs.'

BERNARD, JAMES HENRY, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 30th Dec., 1884; asst. mag. and collr., Midnapur, Jan., 1885; personal asst. to chief commr., British Burma, June, 1886; returned to Bengal, Feb., 1887, as offg. under sec.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1891.

BERRILL, EDWARD, Police Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Served in the Mutiny in Barrow's volunteer cavalry; adjutant, mil. police, Central Prov., 14th Aug., 1858; inspr. of police, N.W. Prov., Feb., 1861, and asst. inspr.-gen., Dec., 1861; district supt., May, 1867; dep. inspr.-gen. of police, March, 1893.

BEST, JAMES WILLIAM, B.A., B.L., late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Wimborne Gr. Sch., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 8th Dec., 1862; served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and sub-collr. and joint mag. in various districts; from Jan., 1875, served in the jud. dept. as district and sessions judge; judge of the high court, March, 1893; rettd., Nov., 1895, and was apptd. chief justice of Mysore.

BEVAN, JAMES FORBES, late India Telegraph Dept.—Joined the service as asst., 21st June, 1866; arrived 14th Sept., 1866, and served on various lines in Bengal and the Punjab; supt., June, 1880; offg. dir., construction branch, 1891-92; rettd., Sept., 1895.

BEVERIDGE, HENRY, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Queen's Coll., Belfast; apptd. after exam. of 1857; arrived, 20th Jan., 1858; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., and mag. and collr.; on special duty in Manipur, Nov., 1863; from Oct., 1874, served in the jud. dept.; district and sessions judge, March, 1875; rettd., Jan., 1893; author of a 'History of Backergunge,' 'The Trial of Nandakumar,' &c., &c.

BEVERLEY, HENRY, M.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Sedbergh Sch., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 31st Oct., 1861, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., dep. commr., and joint mag.; registrar-gen. of assurances, March, 1867; inspr.-gen. of registrn., Dec., 1870; suptd. the

first census of Bengal in 1872; also the census of Calcutta, 1876; district and sessions judge, and addl. judge, 24-Parganas and Hugli, Dec., 1877; census officer, Calcutta, Dec., 1880, and publ. a report on the census, 1881; member of Bengal legisl. council, Jan., 1884; judge of the high court, Calcutta, May, 1885; has publ. a 'Registration Manual' and 'The Land Registration Act.'

BHAGWAN CHUNDER CHAKARBUTTI, late Jud. Dept., Bengal.—From 9th Oct., 1861, served in the subord. jud. service, Bengal, and was apptd. subord. judge, 1st grade, May, 1884; rettd., Nov., 1891.

BIHARAT SINGH, Kunwar, Statutory C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Apptd. probr. under native civil service rules, 16th Feb., 1880; confd., March, 1882; served as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; joint mag., March, 1892.

BIDDULPH, CUTHBERT EDWARD, M.A., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—Educ. at Westminster Sch. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service as proby. asst. settl. officer, Sind, 19th Jan., 1878; services placed at disposal of agent to govr.-gen. for Baluchistan, Nov., 1878; served during the Afghan war of 1878-80 (medal); asst. to the pol. supt., Sawantwari, in charge of the Sir Dasai, Feb., 1880; asst. survey and settl. commr., Baroda, Nov., 1883; rev. settl. officer, Mewar, Dec., 1886; asst. commr., Berar, Feb., 1891; cantonmt. mag., Secunderabad, Jan., 1895; author of 'Our Western Frontier of India,' 'Afghan Politics,' 'Afghan Poetry of the 17th Century,' 'Army Reforms in Native States,' 'Four Months in Persia,' &c.

BIDDULPH, JOHN, Col., I.S.C., late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—Cornet, Bengal cavalry, Jan., 1858; arrived, 4th March, 1858, and served in the Mutiny campaign (medal); A.D.C. to the Viceroy, 1872-77; member of mission to York- and, &c., 1873-74; employed on a secret mission to the countries beyond Gilgit, 1876; on special duty in Gilgit, Sept., 1877; acting agent to govr.-gen., Baluchistan, May-Nov., 1882; pol. agent, Bhopawar, Dec., 1882; pol. agent in Haraoi and Tonk, April, 1886; rest. and commr., Ajmir, April, 1890; offg. agent in Baluchistan, July, 1891; offg. rest., Gwalior, April, 1892; agent, Baroda, Jan., 1893; reverted to mil. dept., July, 1895; author of 'Tribes of the Hindukush.'

BIDDULPH, THOMAS HENRY STILLINGFLEET, Finl.
