CANTOPHER, BERNARD WILLIAM, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. to the dept., 29th Aug., 1873, and posted to state railways; transf'd. to Bengal, Aug., 1881; again employed on railways, from Nov., 1883; exec. engr., May, 1884; services lent to Mahratta railway co., 1888-9; offg. engr. in-chief, Burma state railway, Sept.-Dec., 1893.

CAPPEL, SIR ALBERT JAMES LEPPOC, K.C.I.E.,

late India Telegraph Dept.—Apptd. to the dept., 10th Feb., 1857; 1st grade supt. and personal asst. to dir.-gen., Dec., 1866; dir. of traffic, May, 1869; dep. dir.-gen., Feb., 1879; dir.-gen. of telegraphs, June, 1883; K.C.I.E., Feb., 1887; ret'd., 1889.

CAPPEL, EDWARD LOUIS, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 28th Oct., 1878, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; from May, 1882, served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; dir. of land records and agric., June, 1891, to April, 1893.

CARD, HENRY CHARLES, Store Dept., India Office.—Educ. at Christ's Hosp.; clerk, 2nd class, India store depôt, 22nd Jan., 1875.

CARDEW, ALEXANDER GORDON, M.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at the Somersetshire Coll., Bath, and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1879; arrived, 7th Dec., 1882, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sec. to the Malabar land tenures committee, Dec., 1885; asst. sec., jud. and legisl. dept., Nov., 1888; inspr.-gen. of jails, Aug., 1892.

CARDEW, CORNELIUS EDWARD, A.M.I.C.E., M.I.M.E., India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. to the superior rev. establ., state railways, locomotive dept., March, 1876; locomotive supt., April, 1881; served on the Rajputana, Nagpur-Chhattisgarh, Rajputana-Malwa, and other railways.

CARDOZO, HENRY O'CONNELL, Rev. Survey Dept., Madras.—Served in the dept. from 21st March, 1859, and was promoted to dep. supt., 1st class, March, 1883, and to supt., July, 1887.

CAREY, ARTHUR DOUGLAS, late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at the City of London Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 9th Nov., 1865, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and asst. collr. of salt. rev.; collr. of salt. rev., April, 1881; from Feb., 1882, served under the govt. of India as offg. commr., inland customs; commr., salt. rev., N. India, Dec., 1882; commr. of customs, salt, opium, and abkari, and reporter-gen. of external commerce for Bombay, May, 1891; on special duty in Lisbon for Goa treaty negotiations, 1891-92; ret'd., March, 1893.

CAREY, HORACE RICHARD LE MARCHANT, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., India Public Works Dept.—First commn., 15th Jan., 1861; served in the Hazara campaign, 1868 (medal with clasp); apptd. to the dept., 24th Dec., 1870, as asst. engr., and employed on irrig. works in the Punjab; transf'd. to superior accts. dept., Sept., 1874; examr., Jan., 1882; employed on public works accts. in Burma, April, 1883; and in Bengal, June, 1888.

CAREY, HIRZEL DENIS DE MUSSENDEN, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Marlborough and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 25th Nov., 1891, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

CAREY, LIONEL SLADE, Indian C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at Elizabeth Coll., Guernsey, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 14th Dec., 1882, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; registrar, jud. commr.'s court, June to Dec., 1885; settl. officer, Oct., 1886; dep. commr. and rev. sec., Dec., 1891; acting commr. of settlts. and agric., May-Aug., 1893, and from Dec., 1894.

CARGILL, JAMES DUDLEY, Indian C.S.

(Bengal).—Educ. at the Brighton Gr. Sch., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 13th Nov., 1891, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

CARLYLE, ROBERT WARRAND, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Glasgow Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1878; arrived, 13th Dec., 1880, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt., Dec., 1888; joint mag. and dep. collr., June, 1890; has offd. also as under sec. to the govt. of India, home dept.; mag. and collr., June, 1894.

CARMICHAEL, CHARLES PAGET, C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Harrow and Haileybury; apptd., 1849; arrived, March, 1849, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. sec. to govt.; joint mag. and dep. collr., 1st grade, Jan., 1856; depd. as civil officer with the relieving force to Shahjahanpur, April, 1858; mag. and collr., Budaun, Feb., 1862; inspr.-gen. of police, N.W. Prov., Feb., 1870; commr. and agent to govr.-gen., Benares, March, 1877; also held the substantive appt. of district and sessions judge, Jan., 1877; member of the board of rev., 1882; ret'd., March, 1884.

CARMICHAEL, DAVID FREMANTLE, late Madras C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1851; asst. collr. and mag., Dec., 1851; head asst. registrar, court of Sadar and Foujdari Adalat, Feb., 1855; dep. sec. to govt., Oct., 1856; sec. to board of examrs., Oct., 1856; private sec. to govr. of Madras, March, 1859; fellow of Madras univ., Oct., 1859; collr. and mag. and agent, July, 1863; acting judge, high court, Madras, April to July, 1869; employed in preparation of Vizagapatam district manual; acting member, board of rev., Aug., 1871; inam commr., Aug., 1871; sec. to govt., rev. dept., Nov., 1872; member of legisl. council, Feb., 1874; on special duty at imperial assemblage, Delhi, Dec., 1876; chief sec. to govt., June, 1877; ordinary member of council, Madras, Dec., 1878; on tours as special commr. through hill tracts of Ganjam, Vizagapatam, and Godavari, Feb., 1881; ret'd., 1886.

CARMICHAEL, GEORGE, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at Aberdeen Gr. Sch., Univ. of Aberdeen, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; arrived, 4th Dec., 1886, and served in Bengal as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settl. officer; transf'd. to Burma as asst. commr., Aug., 1889; returned to Bombay as 2nd asst., Oct., 1894.

CARNDUFF, HERBERT WILLIAM CAMERON, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Edinburgh Coll. Sch., Univ. of Edinburgh, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 30th Nov., 1883, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt. of Bengal, May, 1888; registrar, appellate side, high court, Calcutta, Oct., 1890; acted as dep. sec. to govt. of India, legisl. dept., from Jan., 1895.

CARR, REGINALD CHILDERS CULLING, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Cheam Sch., the Charterhouse, and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 7th Dec., 1885, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; addl. special asst. to district mag. and collr., March, 1889; asst. sec., jud. and legis. depts., Sept., 1891; acted as private sec. to govr. in 1891, 1893, and 1895; under sec., rev. dept., Aug., 1892; under sec., in depts. under the chief sec., May, 1894.

CARR, WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at Lancaster Gr. Sch., and Christ's Coll.,

Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 14th Nov., 1893, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

CARSTAIRS, ROBERT, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Edinburgh Acad., and Edinburgh Coll. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 16th Nov., 1874, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1886; dep. commr., Aug., 1888; author of 'British Work in India.'

CARTER, ALBERT MICHAEL, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office.—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll.; temporary clerk, India office, 4th Nov., 1861; junr. clerk, March, 1866; senr. clerk, Aug., 1882; assessor of income tax, 1892.

CARTER, GEORGE MARKHAM STRANGE, Burma Commn.—Apptd. chief inspr. of police, June, 1875; asst. district supt., Feb., 1880, and district supt., Oct., 1882; apptd. asst. commr., March, 1886; dep. commr., Dec., 1890; acted as prest., Rangoon municipality, Dec., 1893, and Jan., 1894.

CARTER, HENRY VANDYKE, M.D. (Lon.), Dep. Surgn.-Gen., late Med. Dept., Bombay.—Joined the Indian med. service, 27th Jan., 1858; arrived, 29th March, 1858, and served on mil. duty and as prof'r. at the Grant med. coll., and asst. surgn., Jamsetji Jijibhow hosp.; also as civil surgn. and supt. of jail, Satara; surgn. to the Gokuldas Tejpal hosp., Bombay, Nov., 1879; principal and prof'r. of medicine, Grant med. coll., and first physician, Jamsetji Jijibhow hosp., 1886; ret'd., July, 1888.

CARTER, PHILIP JOHN, Burma Forest Dept.—Arrived in India, 27th Dec., 1875, and was apptd. to Burma as asst. conservator; dep. conservator, April, 1881; on duty at Port Blair, Nov., 1886; rejoined his Burma appt., June, 1889; conservator, Feb., 1892.

CARTER-CAMPBELL, THOMAS TUPPER, Col., R.E., late Survey of India.—First commn., Dec., 1857; arrived, Dec., 1859; served with the Sikkim field force, 1860-61, as survey officer; from May, 1861, served as asst. engr. in the public works dept., Punjab; apptd. to the great trigonol. survey of India, March, 1862, and served as asst. in the Kashmir and Kumaon and Garhwal surveys; served in the Ambela campaign, 1863 (medal and clasp); surveyor, April, 1866; in charge of the survey party with the Abyssinian expdn., 1867-69; dep. supt., 1st grade, March, 1886; ret'd., Jan., 1887.

CASEY, ARTHUR EDWIN COMERFORD, late Bengal C.S. (N. W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Portora Royal Sch., and Marlborough Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1874, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., Kumaon, May, 1882; ret'd., Oct., 1889.

CASPERSZ, CHARLES PETER, B.L., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Harrow and London Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 21st Dec., 1878, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and settl. officer; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1889; district and sessions judge, Nov., 1894.

CASSON, HERBERT ALEXANDER, B.A., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Newton Abbot Proprietary Coll., Marlborough Coll., and Hertford Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 15th Nov., 1889, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; on special duty with Waziristan delimitation commn., 1894-95.

CAVE-BROWNE, EDWARD RABAN, C.S.I., Acctnt.-Gen., India Office.—Educ. at the Coll. Sch., Taunton; junr. clerk in the treasury, East India house, 12th April, 1854, and from 1858 in the India office; acctnt.-gen.'s dept., 1860; senr. clerk, June, 1867; asst. in the accts. branch of the finl. dept., Jan., 1872; assessor of income tax, 1872; dep. acctnt.-gen., Feb., 1879; C.S.I., Jan., 1888; acctnt.-gen., June, 1893.

CAWLEY, GEORGE JOHN, late Police Dept., Bengal.—Joined the service, 1st May, 1862, as asst. supt. of police, Assam; district supt., Dec., 1876; transf. to Bengal, Dec., 1883; ret'd., Sept., 1894.

CAYLEY, HENRY, Dep. Surgn.-Gen., late Bengal Med. Service.—First commn., 1857; from April, 1872, served as civil surgn. and offg. supt. of vaccn., supt. of royal botanical gardens, ophthalmic surgn. and profr. of ophthalmic surgery, med. coll., Calcutta, supt. of the eye infirmary, and marine surgn.; med. board, India office, 1885; ret'd., April, 1887.

CENTER, WILLIAM, M.B., M.A., Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Med. Dept., Punjab.—Joined Indian med. dept. in 1865; arrived, 11th April, 1866; from March, 1871, served in the Punjab as chemical examr. and profr. in the Lahore med. sch.; offg. meteorological reporter in addn., May, 1872; served under the govt. of India, 1882, as statistical officer in sanitary dept.; civil surgn., Lahore, and supt., lunatic asylum, 1884; profr. Lahore med. coll., April, 1888; ret'd., April, 1893.

CHAMBERLAIN, SIR NEVILLE BOWLES, G.C.B., G.C.S.I., Gen.—Ensign, Bengal infantry, 1837; served through the Afghan campaigns of 1839-42 (medal); present with the govr.-gen.'s body-guard at the battle of Maharajpur, Dec., 1843 (bronze star); dep. asst. quartermaster-gen. to the army, 1843; hon. A.D.C. to Lord Dalhousie, 1848; served in the Punjab campaign of 1848-49, including the actions of Chilianwala and Gujrat (medal and two clasps); mil. sec. to the chief commr. for affairs of the Punjab, March, 1853; commandant, Punjab irregular force, Dec., 1854; adjutant-gen. of the Bengal army, July, 1857; C.B., 1857; A.D.C. to the Queen for services in the Mutiny; commanded the force on the N.W. frontier of India in various expedns. against the hill tribes (medal and clasp); commander-in-chief, Madras, and member of the govr.'s council, Feb., 1876, to 1881; was apptd. head of the special mission to Kabul, Aug., 1878, which, however, was not allowed to enter the Amir's territory.

CHAMBERLAIN, WILLIAM JOHN, India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. traffic supt., Dhond-Manmad railway, 29th Oct., 1878; served upon Berar, Punjab N., Rajputana-Malwa, and other railways; dep. traffic supt., N.W. railway, April, 1892.

CHAMIER, FRANCIS EDWARD ARCHIBALD, Major-Gen., I.S.C., late Oudh Commn.—Arrived, as ensign, 7th April, 1850; A.D.C. to Gen. Outram throughout the Oudh campaign; commanded the Kapurthala contingent during its service in Oudh, in 1858 (medal and two clasps); from March, 1858, served in Oudh as asst. commr., dep. commr., and offg. commr.; district judge, Rae Bareli, Feb., 1882, and afterwards at Faizabad; returned to Europe, 1888.

CHANNING, FRANCIS CHORLEY, late Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 25th Nov., 1870, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and settl. officer; settl. sec. to finl. commr., Jan., 1878; senr. sec. to the finl. commr., March, 1882; dep. commr., March, 1886; district judge, Jan., 1888, but to continue to act as divl. judge; divl. judge, May, 1890; acting judge, chief court, May-Dec., 1894; ret'd., Aug., 1895.

CHAPMAN, CHARLES, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 6th Aug., 1884.

CHAPMAN, EDMUND PELLY, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 12th Nov., 1889, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

CHAPMAN, REGINALD CORRIE, Fin. and Commerce Dept., Govt. of India.—Joined the service, 28th Sept., 1874, and was employed at Nagpur, and afterwards at Rangoon; asst. compr.-gen. at Calcutta, Feb., 1879; transf. to Madras as acting asst. acctnt.-gen., March, 1879; confd., July, 1880; in addn., inspr. of local fund accts., April, 1882; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, Nov., 1891.

CHAPMAN, ROBERT ALEXANDER BOSWELL, Indian C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at Haileybury Coll., and St. Peter's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 15th Nov., 1888, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Central Prov. as asst. commr., Nov., 1891.

CHAPMAN, ROBERT BARCLAY, C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1850; arrived, 6th March, 1850; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; under sec. to govt. of India, home, finl., and foreign depts., April, 1856, to April, 1860; called to the bar (Inner Temple), Jan., 1863; junr. sec., board of rev., Lower Prov., June, 1863; acting commr., Nuddea divn., Nov., 1866, to Jan., 1869; served subsequently as sec. to govt. of India, finl. dept., and govt. dir. of Bank of Bengal; ret'd., 1881.

CHARLES, FENDALL LOWIS, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Repton Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 13th Nov., 1869, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; on special duty, demarcation of forests in Nasik, July, 1875; forest settl. officer, Dharwar, Nov., 1881; acting municipal commr., Bombay, Oct., 1886; junr. collr. and mag., April, 1890; senr. collr. and mag., Nov., 1892.

CHARLES, GEORGE GWYTHER, M.I.E.E., India Telegraph Dept.—Joined the dept., 9th May, 1857; arrived, 3rd Oct., 1857, and held charge of various offices in Bengal and the N.W. Prov.; inspr., April, 1862; asst. supt., Feb., 1863; supt., April, 1880.

CHARLES, JOHN GEORGE, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 15th Dec., 1865, and served in Bombay as asst. mag. and collr., and offg. under sec.; member of cattle plague commn., June, 1870; offg. dep. collr. of customs, Calcutta, July, 1871; offg. dep. commr. of police, Calcutta, March, 1876; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1876; acted as presidency and as chief presidency mag., Calcutta; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1885; ret'd., Jan., 1893.

CHARLES, RICHARD HAVELOCK, M.D.,

M.CH., F.R.C.S.I., Surgn.-Major, Bengal Med. Dept.—Joined the service, 1st April, 1882, and served as profir. of anatomy, Lahore med. coll.; apptd. in similar capacity to Calcutta med. coll., April, 1894.

CHARLTON, ALFRED, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 8th April, 1882.

CHATFIELD, KYRLE MITFORD, M.A., Educ. Dept., Bombay.—Educ. at Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; served from 29th March, 1866, as principal and profir. of logic and moral philosophy at the Elphinstone coll., Bombay; dir. of public instrn., Nov., 1874.

CHATTERTON, FRANK WILLIAM, Col., Bengal Infantry.—Arrived in India as ensign, 4th Nov., 1859; employed under the mil. dept. till Feb., 1869, from which date he served in Jhansi and Kumaon as offg. asst. commr.; from May, 1873, served as cantonmt. mag. of Rurki; also small cause court judge, Rurki, March, 1879; cantonmt. mag., Agra, Dec., 1880, to Nov., 1886, when he was apptd. commandant of administrative battalion Bengal presidency volunteers; hon. A.D.C. to viceroy, Dec., 1888.

CHERRY, JOHN WILLIAM, Forest Dept., Madras.—Joined the service, 5th Feb., 1869, as asst. conservator; dep. conservator, Jan., 1872; conservator, Oct., 1891.

CHEVENIX-TRENCH, GEORGE FREDERICK, Capt., I.S.C., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—first commn., 22nd Jan., 1879; served in the Burma expdn., 1886; offg. asst. to govr.-gen.'s agent in Rajputana, June, 1888; on depn. in Kotah and Jhallowar, 1888-89; acting asst. district supt. of police, Quetta, Dec., 1889; also asst. commr., April, 1890; offg. first asst. to pol. rest., Persian Gulf, Aug., 1890; acting pol. agent, Basra, April, 1891; British joint commr. at Leh, Dec., 1891; asst. rest. for Leh, Kashmir, Dec., 1894.

CHEVIS, WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Lancing Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 4th Dec., 1885, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; district judge, April, 1893.

CHICHELE-PLOWDEN, TREVOR JOHN, C.S.I., Indian C.S.—Educ. at Winchester Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 31st Oct., 1868, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and offg. under sec.; offg. under sec., govt. of India, home dept., Feb., 1873; inspr.-gen. of police, Assam, Nov., 1876; sec. to the prison conference, Jan., 1877; sec., govt. of India, foreign dept., Dec., 1878; rest., and pol. agent, Turkish Arabia, March, 1880; consul-gen., Baghdad, June, 1880; commr. of Ajmir-Merwara, June, 1885; rest. in Mewar, Nov., 1885; rest. in Kashmir, Nov., 1887; commr., Haidarabad assigned districts, Nov., 1888; rest., Haidarabad, March, 1892; C.S.I., Jan., 1893; author of several reports on journeys in Kurdistan, &c.

CHIPPER, ERNEST, Registry and Record Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 3rd Dec., 1875.

CHISHOLM, JAMES WRIGHT, late Central Prov. Commn.—Served in the Central Prov. from 24th Oct., 1856, as dep. collr., asst. commr., dep. commr., and settl. officer; inspr.-gen. of registrn., supt. of stamps, and commr. of excise, Oct., 1871; inspr.-gen. of police and jails, Oct., 1876; sec. to the chief commr., June, 1880; commr. of the Chhattisgarh divn., March, 1883; ret'd., 1889.

CHISHOLME, JOHN JAMES SCOTT, Lieut.-Col., 5th Lancers.—First commn., 9th lancers, 10th Jan., 1872; served in the Afghan war, 1878-80; mil. sec. to govr. of Madras (Lord Connemara, G.C.I.E.), 10th Aug., 1888; reverted to mil. dept., Dec., 1890.

CHITPAL SINGH, Raja, Statutory C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Apptd. under native civil service rules, 12th Dec., 1881, and served in Oudh as asst. commr.; joint mag., Nov., 1893.

CHITTY, CHARLES WILLIAM, B.A., B.L., Bombay Jud. Dept.—Called to the bar (Inner Temple), 17th Nov., 1884; acting chief judge, Bombay small cause court, Feb.-May, 1891, and again from Oct., 1891; confd., July, 1892.

CHOLMELEY, NORMAN GOODFORD, B.A., Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at Charterhouse, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 22nd Dec., 1884, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Oct., 1890.

CHRISTIE, ARCHIBALD, B.L., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 7th Nov., 1871, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; on special duty, Kuram field force, Oct., 1878; offg. under sec. to govt., Aug. to Dec., 1884; district judge, Sept., 1887; divl. judge, April, 1893; acting judge, chief court, June, 1895.

CHRISTIE, CHARLES HOWARD PEREGRINE, Lieut.-Col., R.E., India Public Works Dept.—First commn., April, 1866; joined the dept., March, 1869; served as asst. engr. and asst. consulting engr. for railways; dep. controller, public works accts., Madras, irrign. branch, Jan., 1873; examr., Jan., 1880; transfed. to Central India, Sept., 1880; examr. of guaranteed railway accts., Calcutta, April, 1881; examr., public works accts., Madras, March, 1887; N.W. Prov. and Oudh, March, 1894.

CHRISTIE, GEORGE FRANCIS STEPHEN, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 14th Dec., 1891, and served in Burma as asst. commr., cantonmt. mag., and small cause court judge.

CHRISTIE, JAMES THOMAS, C.I.E., Police Dept., Punjab.—Entered the acctnt.-gen.'s office, Jan., 1858; served in the police dept. from March, 1861, and under the govt. of India in the foreign dept., Sept., 1887, and Feb., 1880 (in Afghanistan); C.I.E., March, 1881; district supt. of police, first grade, Dec., 1886; asst. inspr.-gen., railway police, April, 1893, to June, 1894.

CHRYSTIE, GEORGE, Col., Police Dept., Madras.—Joined the service, 12th Dec., 1857; arrived, 12th Feb., 1858, and served in the suppression of the Mutiny, &c.; from Aug., 1863, served in Madras as asst. supt. of police; supt., April, 1873; dep. inspr.-gen., Dec. 1891.

CHUCKERBUTTY, ALFRED WILLIAM GOOD-EVE, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch. and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 30th Dec., 1891, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

CHUCKERBUTTY, ARTHUR GOOD-EVE, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch. and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 25th Nov., 1886, and served as asst. mag. and collr. in Bengal; asst. compt., India treasuries, June, 1893; dep. acct. it.-gen., Bombay, May, 1894.

CHUNDER MADHUB GHOSE, Jud. Dept.,

Bengal.—Member of the legisl. council of Bengal, 1883-85; fellow, Calcutta univ., 1885; prest., faculty of law, 1886-88; prest., board of examrs. for pleaders, &c., 1892; puisne judge, high court, Calcutta, 12th Jan., 1885.

CHURCH, WILLIAM THOMAS, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Rugby, and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 30th Oct., 1863, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; supt., Dehra Dun, Jan., 1882; mag. and collr., July, 1885; offd. as commr., Rohilkhand, in 1889; ret'd., April, 1890.

CLARK, JOHN, M.A., Indian C.S. (Assam).—Educ. at Old Aberdeen Gr. Sch., Aberdeen Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 3rd Jan., 1887, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf'd. to Assam as asst. commr., April, 1890.

CLARK, WILLIAM OVENS, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Portora Sch., Ennis-killen, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 11th Nov., 1871, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; on famine duty, Bengal, Feb., 1874; on special duty in the public works dept., March, 1882, and in the secretariat, March, 1885; dep. commr., Jan., 1888; district judge, Sept., 1890; divl. judge, Nov., 1892.

CLARKE, SIR ANDREW, G.C.M.G., C.B., C.I.E., Lieut.-Gen.—First commn., royal engineers, June, 1844; in 1848 was employed on special service in New Zealand, and was A.D.C. to lieut.-govr., Tasmania, 1849-53; govr. and commander-in-chief, Straits Settlements, 1873-75; member of council of govr.-gen. of India in charge of public works, June, 1875, to 1880; commandant, sch. of mil. engrg., Chatham, 1881-82; inspr.-gen. of fortifications and dir. of works, ordnance dept., war office, 1882-86.

CLARKE, CHARLES, Funds Dept., India Office.—Writer, India office, 18th Nov., 1872; clerk specially attached, March, 1893.

CLARKE, C.B., M.A., late Educl. Service, Assam.—Apptd. to the Bengal educl. dept., 8th Dec., 1865; served as profir. at the presidency coll., and as inspr. of schools; offg. supt. of the Calcutta botanical garden, May, 1869; offg. dir. of public instrn., Dec., 1884; transf'd. to Assam as inspr. of schools, March, 1885; ret'd., June, 1887.

CLARKE, CHARLES MANSFIELD, C.B., Lieut.-Gen.—First commn., 1st March, 1856; served in the New Zealand wars, 1861-66 (medal), and S. African war, 1879 (medal and C.B.), and the subsequent operations in Basutoland; commandant-gen. of the colonial forces, Cape of Good Hope, 1880-82; asst. adjutant-gen., southern district, 1882, Aldershot, 1882-84, headquarters, 1884-86; dep. adjutant-gen., Ireland, 1886-88; in command of 3rd infantry brigade at Aldershot, 1889-92; dep. adjutant-gen., headquarters, 1892; commander-in-chief, Madras, and second member of council, Oct., 1893, till the abolition of that post, April, 1895; lieut.-gen. commanding the forces in Madras, April, 1895.

CLARKE, EDWARD HENRY SCAMANDER, Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—Held various non-gazetted appts. from 20th Sept., 1875; on depn. with Kabul mission, 1893; asst. sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., Oct., 1894.

CLARKE, HERVEY MORRIS STANLEY, Col., I.S.C.—Arrived in India as ensign, Madras

native infantry, 13th March, 1854; served with the Central India horse, 1858-60; from Sept., 1861, served in the N.W. Prov. police dept.; district supt., April, 1863; personal asst. to inspr.-gen. of police, Dec., 1871; dep. inspr.-gen., Oct., 1885; inspr.-gen. of prisons, N.W. Prov. and Oudh, May, 1886; reverted to mil. duty, 1890.

CLARKE, LEYCESTER ANNAND GREY, B.A., Indian C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., and Jesus Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 23rd Dec., 1893, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.

CLARKE, ROBERT, B.A., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Royal Sch., Raphoe, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 8th Dec., 1870, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. registrar, chief court, Punjab, May, 1881; offg. divl. judge, Oct., 1886; dep. commr., Nov., 1886.

CLARKE, ROBERT LUCAS HYRAPIET, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 25th Dec., 1893, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.

CLARKE, SYDENHAM CUBBON, Col., late Madras Public Works Dept.—Arrived, 9th Jan., 1862; apptd. asst. engr., Mysore, Oct., 1862; exec. engr., Sept., 1867; served at Chatham, March to Sept., 1874; acting profir. of surveying, Cooper's Hill Coll., Sept., 1874; returned to Madras, Nov., 1876; served under the govt. of India on the Peshawar-Jalalabad state railway, Nov., 1879, and in Baluchistan; temporary supt. of works, Madras, April, 1883; offd. for various periods as suptg. engr., and in May, 1889, was in charge of public works, stores, and workshops; ret'd., March, 1891.

CLARKE, TREDWORTH GEORGE, Col., I.S.C.—Ensign, Madras infantry, 1853; served in the Mysore commn. from Oct., 1857; dep. supt., Nov., 1862; dep. commr., 1873; sec. to chief commr., 1877; commr., Nagar divn., May, 1879; commr. of Coorg, 1881; offg. rest. in Mysore and chief commr. of Coorg, Dec., 1884, to March, 1885; and afterwards commr.; reverted to mil. duty, Jan., 1891.

CLARKE, WILLOUGHBY CHARLES STANLEY, Major-Gen., I.S.C., late Assam Commn.—First commn. as ensign, June, 1851; arrived, 15th Oct., 1851; was employed on mil. duty till 1862, including service in the Sonthal expdn., 1855, and in the China expdn., 1860-61; also served as supt. of police and mag., govr.-gen.'s camp, 1859-60; from June, 1862, served in the Assam commn.; dep. commr., July, 1867; returned to Europe, 1889.

CLAY, ARTHUR LLOYD, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Cholmeley Gr. Sch., Highgate, at Cologne, and at St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 29th Nov., 1862, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. and joint mag. and dep. collr.; from Sept., 1874, to June, 1877, served in Assam as dep. commr., and district and sessions judge of Sylhet; mag. and collr., July, 1879; dep. commr., Manbhum, Aug., 1879; mag. and collr., Bakarganj, March, 1886; offd. as commr. of various divns.; ret'd., July, 1890.

CLAY, WILLIAM MCKINLEY, B.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 28th Oct., 1862; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., asst.

commr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; on famine relief duty, 1873-74; mag. and collr., 1st grade, March, 1887; ret'd., 1888.

CLEGG, ROBERT BAILEY, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 12th Jan., 1885, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and head asst.

CLEGHORN, JAMES, M.D., Surgn.-Major-Gen., Indian Med. Dept.—Asst. surgn., March, 1865; arrived, 26th Sept., 1865; held various mil. med. charges, and offd. as civil surgn. at several stations; from Feb., 1870, served in the N.W. Prov., as civil surgn., at various stations; offg. surgn. supt., presidency gen. hosp., Calcutta, June, 1885, to March, 1886, when he was apptd. civil surgn. of Lucknow; inspr.-gen. of civil hosp., Punjab, Sept., 1891; in 1893 was employed on special duty in connection with the cantonmts. act; surgn.-gen. and sanitary commr. with govt. of India, March, 1895.

CLEMENTS, ERNEST, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1893, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

CLENCH, ROBERT CHERRETT, Registry and Record Dept., India Office.—Apptd. supplemental clerk, 1st Jan., 1893.

CLERK, GEORGE PERCY, Madras Rev. Dept.—Joined the service as proby. asst. dir., rev. settlt., 14th Sept., 1871; asst. dir., July, 1872; on special duty, N. Arcot, in connexion with famine relief works; dep. dir., Sept., 1880; dep. commr. of rev. settlt., April, 1887.

CLEVELAND, CHARLES RAITT, Indian C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at Christ's Coll., Finchley, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1887, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; asst. settlt. officer, Jan., 1890; asst. to commr. of settlt. and agric., Nov., 1890; settlt. officer, Oct., 1891; commr. of excise, &c., Nov., 1894.

CLIFFORD, SAMUEL LE POER TRENCH, Punjab Commn.—From 1st April, 1861, served in the subord. jud. dept.; in Jan., 1872, was apptd. registrar of the jud. commr.'s court in Oudh; dep. registrar of chief court, Punjab, Oct., 1872; offd. for various periods as judge, small cause court; jud. asst., Jan., 1884; author of 'Notes on the Code of Criminal Procedure.'

CLIFFORD, WILLIAM WIGRAM, Police Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Joined the dept., 10th Sept., 1868, as acting asst. district supt.; confd., Feb., 1871; district supt., March, 1875.

CLIFT, HENRY WILKINS, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. 8th Oct., 1868, and employed in buildings and roads branch in Mysore; exec. engr., Sept., 1871; served also in irrign. branch, and on famine relief works; transf. to state railways, July, 1879, and employed on Mysore and S. Mahrattra lines, and on Bengal-Assam railway survey; E. Coast railway, Nov., 1880; ret'd., Dec., 1895.

CLIFTON, CHARLES NAPIER, Bombay Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1876, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1886.

CLINTON (BARON), RIGHT HON. CHARLES HENRY ROLLE HEPBURN STUART TREFUSIS.—Educ. at Eton, and at Christ Church, Oxford; M.P. for N. Devon from 1857 to 1866; parly. under sec. of state for India, July, 1867, to Dec.,

1868; apptd. a charity commr. for England and Wales, 1874.

CLOETE, HENRY NEWMAN CUNNINGHAM, India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., and posted to Bombay, 10th Aug., 1868; exec. engr., March, 1873; transf. to state railways, Dec., 1873, and employed on W. Rajputana railway survey, and on the Punjab N. and Kandahar state railways; transf. to Bengal, May, 1881; suptg. engr., May, 1893.

CLOETE, JOSIAS GORDON, Lieut.-Gen., late Rev. Survey Dept., Madras.—Ensign, Madras infantry, Feb., 1857; arrived, May, 1857; served in the Madras police as asst. supt., June, 1861, to June, 1863, and from the latter date in the rev. survey; dep. supt., Jan., 1872; ret'd. from the dept., 1888.

CLOUGH, JOHN WILLIAM, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 25th July, 1892.

COBB, HENRY VENN, LL.B., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at King's Sch., Canterbury, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 18th Nov., 1886, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst. collr., Feb., 1892; acting asst. rest., Mysore, and sec. to chief commr., Coorg, Aug.-Nov., 1893, and from April, 1895; in 1894 acted as dep. dir., land records, and as dep. commr. of salt, &c., rev.

COBB, WILLIAM HEY, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at St. Peter's Sch., York, Winchester, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 25th Dec., 1884, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; city mag., Lucknow, July, 1889; also wasika officer, Oct., 1891; joint mag., Aug., 1893.

COCKERELL, HORACE ABEL, C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury; arrived, 31st Oct., 1853; served as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., and mag. and collr. also as supt. of survey, Sept., 1859, offg. chairman, justices of the peace, Calcutta, and commr. of police, March, 1869, and Dec., 1872; commr. of divn., June, 1872; sec. to the govt. of Bengal, jud. and pol. depts., April, 1878; member of Bengal legisl. council, 1878, 1880, and 1882; board of rev., March, 1883; offg. lieut.-govr. of Bengal, Aug., 1885; ret'd., Aug., 1887.

CODRINGTON, GEORGE HENRY FORBES, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., late Police Dept., Bombay.—Arrived, 4th May, 1857; served on mil. duty till Dec., 1865, and subsequently in the Bombay police dept. as asst. supt. and district supt.; 1st grade supt., April, 1883; reverted to mil. dept., 1892.

COGHLAN, ROBERT NESBIT, Customs Dept., Bombay.—Arrived, 23rd April, 1871; from March, 1872, served as gen. manager of the customs bonded warehouses, Bombay; from March, 1874, served as asst. collr. of salt rev.; collr. of sea customs, Karachi, and asst. commr. of salt rev. in Sind, Sept., 1882; collr. of customs and salt rev. in Sind, March, 1891.

COLDSTREAM, WILLIAM, B.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 31st Oct., 1861, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; jud. asst., July, 1876; dep. commr., March, 1877; acted as commr. in various divns. from 1880; ret'd., June, 1894; author of a work on the grasses of the Southern Punjab.

COLE, WILLIAM HAMMOND, M.A., late Survey

of India Dept.—Served in the educn. dept., N.W. Prov., April, 1862, to March, 1864, as profrr. of mathematics at the govt. coll., Agra; from Jan., 1867, served in the great trigon. survey; asst. supt. of surveys, Sept., 1869; dep. supt., July, 1882; apptd. to charge of head quarters office, Dehra Dun, and survey detachment in Kashmir, Jan., 1884; ret'd., April, 1892; author of various contributions to the survey publns., and of transls. relating to geodesy.

COLEBROOK, HENRY WILLIAM VAUGHAN, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1874, and employed on irrign. works in the Punjab; exec. engr., May, 1883; transfed. to Burma, April, 1894, and placed in charge of central irrign. divn.

COLERIDGE, FRANCIS ARTHUR, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Malvern Coll., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 5th Dec., 1894, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

COLES, GEORGE EDWARD, India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1874, and employed on irrign. works in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., May, 1883.

COLLEN, SIR EDWIN HENRY HAYTER, K.C.I.E., Major-Gen., I.S.C.—First commn., royal artillery, July, 1863; served with the Abyssinian expdn., 1868 (medal); commissary of ordnance, India, 1869-70; admitted to the Bengal staff corps, May, 1873; acted as asst. mil. sec. to govt. of India, 1870 and 1873-74; sec. to Indian ordnance commn., 1874-75; 3rd asst. sec. to govt. of India, mil. dept., March, 1875; 2nd asst. sec., April, 1876; 1st asst. sec., Dec., 1876; dep. asst. quartermaster-gen., imperial assemblage, Delhi, 1876-77; on special duty in intelligence branch, London, 1877-79; sec. to Indian army organisation commn., 1879-80; served during the Afghan war as asst. controller-gen. of supply and transport, 1880-81 (medal); dep. sec. to govt. of India, mil. dept., April, 1884; served in Egypt with Suakim field force and as mil. sec. to Sir G. Graham, 1885 (medal with clasp, and Khedive's star); acctnt.-gen., mil. dept., govt. of India, July, 1886; sec. to govt. of India, mil. dept., Nov., 1887; C.I.E., Jan., 1889; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1893; has been apptd. to succeed Sir H. Brackenbury as mil. member of govr.-gen.'s council.

COLLIER, FRANK ROBERT STANLEY, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at King's Coll. and London Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 6th Nov., 1872, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; on famine duty, March, 1874; offg. collr. of customs, Calcutta, May, 1884; joint mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1886; mag. and collr., April, 1889; member of Bengal legisl. council, July, 1893; publ. an annotated edition of the Bengal municipal act, 1884, another of the Bengal local self-govt. act and rules, 1886, and the 'Bengal Municipal Manual,' 1887.

COLLIER, GEORGE HERMAN, Store Dept., India Office.—Educ. at Uppingham Sch.; junr. clerk, India office, 17th Feb., 1876.

COLLIN, EDWARD WOODFIELD, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Eton, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1878, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; private sec. to finl. member of govr.-gen.'s council, Jan., 1880; served under

the foreign dept., Jan. to July, 1881, and in the finl. dept., March to Sept., 1885; settl. officer, Muzaffarpur, Jan., 1886; offd. as dir. of land records, Bengal, April, 1888; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1889; mag. and collr., Jan., 1894.

COLLINS, ARTHUR HERBERT, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 1st Jan., 1879, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. and joint mag. and dep. collr.; transfed. to Burma as asst. commr., Aug., 1894.

COLLINS, SIR ARTHUR JOHN HAMMOND, Kt., Q.C., B.L., Madras High Court.—Educ. at St. John's Coll., Cambridge, of which he was fellow; called to the bar (Gray's Inn), 1860; Q.C. and bencher of his inn, 1877; recorder of Poole, 1873-79, and of Exeter from 1879, until he was apptd. chief justice of the high court, Madras, 28th Nov., 1885; vice-chancellor, Madras univ., 1889.

COLLINS, GEORGE GOWER, Burma Commn.—Held subord. appts. from 25th July, 1879; apptd. to the Burma police dept. as asst. district supt., Oct., 1882; joined the commn. as supernumerary asst. commr., March, 1884; asst. supt., Rangoon jail, Oct., 1884; asst. commr., March, 1886.

COLLINS, SIDNEY ARTHUR, Store Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, India store depôt, 4th Dec., 1885.

COLQUHOUN, ARCHIBALD ROSS, A.M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Entered the dept. in Oct., 1871, and served as asst. and exec. engr. in Burma; on special duty as personal asst. to Major Street on his mission to Siam, Jan.-May, 1879; posted to special duty in Upper Burma as civil officer and apptd. a dep. commr., Feb., 1886; retrnsfcd. to public works dept., and posted to Baluchistan, Aug., 1889; services lent to British S. Africa Co., 1891-92; ret'd., Nov., 1893; author of 'Across Chryse,' 'Among the Shans,' 'The Truth about Tonquin,' 'The Opening of China,' 'Burma and the Burmans.'

COLVIN, SIR AUCKLAND, K.C.M.G., K.C.S.I., C.I.E., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Eton; apptd. from Haileybury, 1857; arrived, 17th Jan., 1858, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settl. officer; offg. under sec. to the govt. of India in the home dept., May, 1864, and in the foreign dept., Jan. to Nov., 1865; settl. officer, July, 1869; sec., board of rev., N.W. Prov., March, 1870; on special duty for revision of Allahabad settl., June, 1870; offg. sec. to govt., N.W. Prov., April, 1873, and again in 1875; offg. commr. of excise and stamps, and inspr.-gen. of registrn., June, 1873; mag. and collr., Basti, Feb., 1877, and afterwards at Bijnaur; offg. commr. of inland customs under the govt. of India, Nov., 1877; transfed. for employment in Egypt, Jan., 1879, and served as controller of Egyptian finance, and subsequently as finance minister to the Egyptian govt.; acted as consul-gen. in Sir E. Malet's absence; finl. member of council of the govr.-gen. of India, Oct., 1883; lieut.-govr. of the N.W. Prov., and chief commr. of Oudh, Nov., 1887, to Nov., 1892; ret'd., Dec., 1892.

COLVIN, BAZETT WETENHALL, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Eton; apptd. from Haileybury, 1850; arrived, 29th June, 1850; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. till Jan., 1854, and subsequently in the N.W. Prov.;

asst. commr., Kumaon, April, 1855; mag. and collr., Mainpuri, July, 1865; civil and sessions judge, Allahabad, July, 1873; on special duty at Agra during visit of H.R.H. the Prince of Wales, Feb., 1876; offg. sec. to govt., N.W. Prov., May, 1876; commr., Meerut, Jan., 1879; addl. member, govr.-gen.'s council, Jan., 1877, to May, 1881; member, board of rev., N.W. Prov., Jan., 1862; retd., April, 1885.

COLVIN, CLEMENT SNEYD, Public Works Dept., India Office.—Educ. at Brighton Coll., and by private tuition in England and on the Continent; temporary clerk, 1863; junr. clerk, mil. dept., 1864; funds dept., 1867; private sec. to sec. of state (the Duke of Argyll), 1873-74; private sec. to permanent under sec. of state, 1874-77, and accompanied Sir Louis Mallet on his depn. to India in 1875-76; senr. clerk, rev., statistics, and commerce dept., 1877; rest. clerk, 1877-85; public works dept., 1877; govt. dir. of Madras irrign. and canal cos., 1880-82; asst. sec. in the public works, railway and telegraph dept., Sept., 1882; asst. govt. dir. of Indian railway cos., 1882; sec., public works dept., March, 1892.

COLVIN, ELLIOT GRAHAM, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Charterhouse, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 4th Jan., 1883, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Assam as personal asst. to chief commr., May, 1884; asst. pol. agent, Rajputana, Sept., 1886; private sec. to lieut.-govr. of Bengal, April, 1887; 1st asst. to govr.-gen.'s agent in Baluchistan, April, 1889; served under finl. dept. of govt. of India from June, 1890; offg. postmr.-gen., Bengal, July, 1890; joint mag. and dep. collr., May, 1891; mag. and collr., March, 1895.

COLVIN, JOHN RUSSELL COLQUHOUN, Capt., I.S.C.—Joined the service, 13th Aug., 1879, and was employed under the mil. dept. to Aug., 1886, including service in the E. Soudan, 1885; apptd. asst. to the agent in Rajputana, Aug., 1886; private sec. to lieut.-govr., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Nov., 1887; govr. to the Nawab of Rampur, April, 1889; rest. pol. officer, Rampur state, April, 1894.

COMINS, DENIS WOOD DEANE, M.D., Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., Bengal Jail Dept.—Joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1881, and served on gen. and jail duty, and as offg. first rest. surgn., med. coll. hosp., Calcutta; protector of emigrants, Calcutta, Jan., 1889; from Jan., 1891, served under the govt. of India, fin. and commerce dept.; apptd. inspr.-gen. of jails, Bengal, March, 1892.

CÖNDER, JAMES, late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the public works dept., Sept., 1870; exec. engr., Oct., 1873; personal asst. to engr. in-chief, Punjab N. railway, June, 1875; provisional asst. dir. of state railways, western system, 1877; traffic supt., Indus valley state railway, May, 1878; manager, Holkar, Sindia-Nimach, and Nimach-Nasirabad railways, Feb., 1881; traffic supt., Rajputana-Malwa railways, Sept., 1884; retd., 1890.

CONDON, JAMES HUNT, M.D., Brigade-Surgn., late Med. Dept., N.W. Prov.—Asst. surgn., Bengal med. establ., Feb., 1859; arrived, 25th July, 1859; served under the mil. dept. till Oct., 1861, when he was apptd. civil asst. surgn. in Oudh; from April, 1868, served in the N.W. Prov.; civil surgn. and supt.

of jail, Cawnpore, March, 1871; retd., March 1891.

CONINGHAM, HENRY EVELYN, Major-Gen., I.S.C., late Police Dept., Madras.—Arrived, 18th March, 1855; served in the dept. from Dec., 1861, as asst. supt. and supt., and acted as dep. inspr.-gen., Sept., 1883, to May, 1884; retd., Feb., 1886.

CONNAN, WILLIAM, India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 22nd Dec., 1868, and posted to Bengal; exec. engr., Aug., 1879; offg. under sec. to govt. of Bengal, public works dept., 1888 and 1889; offg. suptg. engr., Orissa irrign. circle, April, 1891.

CONNAUGHT AND STRATHEARN (DUKE OF), H.R.H. ARTHUR WILLIAM PATRICK ALBERT, K.G., K.T., K.P., G.C.S.I., G.C.M.G., G.C.I.E., K.C.B., P.C., Gen.—First commn., 19th June, 1868; asst. adjutant-gen., Gibraltar, Oct., 1875; brigadier-gen., Aldershot, Sept., 1880; accompanied the expedy. force to Egypt, Aug., 1882 (medal with clasp, 2nd class Medjidie, and C.B.); commander-in-chief, and 2nd member of council, Bombay, Dec., 1886, to March, 1890; on return to England was apptd. to command of southern divn., Aug., 1890; commanding at Aldershot, Oct., 1893.

CONNEMARA (BARON), RIGHT HON. ROBERT BOURKE, G.C.I.E., P.C.—Educ. at Enniskillen Royal Sch. and at Trinity Coll., Dublin; called to the bar at the Inner Temple, Nov., 1852; M.P. for Lynn Regis from Dec., 1868; partly under sec. of state for foreign affairs, Feb., 1874, to April, 1880; a royal commr. on the laws relating to copyright, Oct., 1875; privycouncillor, April, 1880; again partly under sec. for foreign affairs, June, 1883, to Feb., 1886; apptd. govr. of Madras, Sept., 1886; took his seat, 7th Dec., 1886; created a peer, 12th May, 1887; resigned, 1st Dec., 1890.

CONNOLLY, JOHN FRANCIS, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Belvedere Coll., Dublin, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 6th Nov., 1890, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under sec. to govt. March, 1894.

CONNOLLY, EDWARD ROBERT, Col., I.S.C., late Punjab Commn.—Arrived, 9th April, 1858; from Sept., 1866, served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; on special duty as asst. to Major Cavagnari, Feb., 1879, and again at Khaibar, Feb., 1880; jud. asst., Sept., 1883; dep. commr., Bannu, Nov., 1884; also district judge; retd., Aug., 1890.

CONSTABLE, EDWIN THEOBALD, M.A., late Educl. Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Joined the service as mathematical profr., Bareilly coll., 25th Feb., 1862; inspr. of public instrn., 1st circle, Aug., 1870; inspr. of schools, Dec., 1877; offg. dir. of public instrn., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Feb., 1883, and again, April, 1890; retd., Sept., 1891.

CONSTABLE, WILLOUGHBY VERNER, Major, R.E., India Public Works Dept.—First commn., 12th Sept., 1872; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1876, and posted to state railways; asst. manager, Rajputana state railway, July, 1882; employed in mil. dept., on Suakim-Berber railway, 1885; dep. consulting engr. for railways, Bombay, Nov., 1886; exec. engr., Feb., 1887; offg. manager, Bhaunagar, Gondal and Morvi railways, April-June, 1887; dep. agent, Bengal-Nagpur railway co., 1887-89; offg. dep.

consulting engr. for railways, and under sec. to govt., public works dept., Madras, Feb., 1891; dep. manager, N.W. railway, Nov., 1893.

CONYBEARE, HENRY CRAWFORD ARTHUR, M.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Westminster Sch., King's Coll., London, and Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 3rd Nov., 1873, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Jan., 1885; dep. commr., Aug., 1890; mag. and collr., Aug., 1891; author of 'An Account of Pargana Dudhi, in S. Mirzapur,' and of contributions to the provl. gazetteer.

COODE, MONTGOMERY PENROSE, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1876, and posted to Haidarabad; exec. engr., Jan., 1885; transf. to Burma, Oct., 1889; asst. to chief engr. and under sec. to chief commr., Feb., 1895.

COOK, HENRY, M.D., Surgn.-Col., late Med. Dept., Bombay.—Joined the Indian med. service, 31st March, 1865; arrived, 31st Aug., 1865, and served as civil surgn. at various stations, supt. of vaccn., and supt. of jail; acted as surgn., Jamsetji Jijibhoj hosp., and prof., Grant med. coll., 1879-80, and 1881-82; civil surgn., Poona, with med. charge of the Deccan coll. and coll. of science, and supt. of lunatic asylum, April, 1883; also supt. and teacher of medicine and therapeutics at the med. school; acted as dep. sanitary commr., Oct., 1884; reverted to mil. dept., 1891.

COOK, HENRY, M.D., F.R.C.P., Dep. Surgn.-Gen., late Med. Dept., Bombay.—Joined the Indian med. service, 24th Jan., 1855; arrived, 23rd July, 1855, and served under the mil. dept. up to 1859, also holding the civil surgeney of Karachi in 1856; served with the Persian expedy. force in 1857 (medal); apptd. med. officer to pol. agency, Khalat, April, 1859; from April, 1861, served as civil surgn. in Ahmadnagar and Satara, and as supt. of Mahabal-eswar, and asst. collr. and mag. at that station; scientific officer, Abyssinian field force, Dec., 1867 (medal); physician, Jamsetji Jijibhoj hosp., and prof., Grant med. coll., May, 1873; retd., Nov., 1886; author of various papers publ. in the transactions of the med. and physical society of Bombay, on the geology, &c., of Baluchistan, and of other reports and papers on scientific and med. subjects.

COOK, HENRY DAVID, M.B., C.M., Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., Madras Med. Dept.—Joined the service as asst. surgn., 1st April, 1870; arrived, 22nd Dec., 1870, and served on mil., gen. and jail duty; surgn., 1st district, and med. inspr. of emigrants, Madras, May, 1888.

COOK, JOHN HOME, Madras Rev. Survey Dept.—Joined the service, 23rd Jan., 1868, as proby. asst. supt.; confd., July, 1876; dep. supt., May, 1887.

COOKE, ARTHUR GEORGE, B.A., Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at Bath Coll., and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 11th Jan., 1895, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

COOKE, CRAWFORD BOYD, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Burma Commn.—First commn., March, 1864; served under the mil. dept. up to Dec., 1868; and subsequently as asst. commr. in Burma; from Feb., 1874, to May, 1877, served

as pol. agent at Bhamo, and from May, 1879, as cantonmt. mag. in Nowgong; from Jan., 1884, served as dep. commr. in Burma, and was apptd. to special duty in Upper Burma, Dec., 1885; commr., Oct., 1890; acted as finl. commr. in 1894; author of the 'British Burina Manual.'

COOKE, FREDERICK JAMES, Indian C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at Bath Coll. and Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1886, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; transf. temporarily to Central Prov. as asst. commr., Nov., 1891.

COOKE, GORDON HENRY, Survey of India Dept.—Joined the dept., 6th Dec., 1866, and was apptd. asst. supt., May, 1871; in 1871-72, served with the Lushai expedy. force (medal); served in various districts, and at head-quarters, Calcutta, and was apptd. dep. supt., Oct., 1885; supt., Jan., 1895.

COOKE, HENRY GIRAUD, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 12th Nov., 1869, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; mag. and collr., June, 1886; commr., Orissa, and supt., tributary mahals, March, 1895.

COOKE, JOHN EDWARD, Finl. Dept., Govt. of India.—From Oct., 1863, to April, 1868, served as asst. sec. to the govt. of India in the finl. dept.; dep. acctnt.-gen., Central Prov., Sept., 1870, and in Madras, Jan., 1874; asst. compt.-gen., May, 1875; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, Oct., 1878; dep. acctnt.-gen., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, July, 1883; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, Oct., 1888; dep. auditor-gen., April, 1891.

COOKE, SAMUEL, M.A., F.I.C., F.G.S., A.M.I.C.E., Educl. Dept., Bombay.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; arrived, 27th Dec., 1868, and served as prof. of chemistry and geology at the civil engrg. coll. (now the coll. of science), Poona; apptd. principal, Oct., 1893.

COOKE, THEODORE, C.I.E., M.A., M.Eng., LL.D., late Educl. Dept., Bombay.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; arrived, Nov., 1860; from June, 1865, served as principal of the civil engrg. coll., Poona; acted as dir. of public instrn. and also as dir. of land records and agric.; retd., Oct., 1893, and was apptd. technical sub-dir., Imperial Institute.

COOPER, CHARLES PHILIP, late Police Dept., Bombay.—Arrived, 28th Jan., 1863; served from June, 1864, as examr. of the insolvent debtors' court, Bombay; police mag., Bombay, from Oct., 1871; acting first judge of the small cause court, Sept., 1873; senr. mag. of police and rev. judge, Bombay, June, 1874; chief presidency mag. and rev. judge, Bombay, June, 1878; retd., March, 1895.

COOPER, HARRY ALBERT, B.A., India Audit Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, acctnt.-gen.'s dept., India office, 3rd June, 1880; junr. examr., India audit office, Nov., 1881; B.A., London univ., 1887; designation changed to junr. clerk, Oct., 1892.

COPLESTON, FREDERICK SELWYN, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 23rd Oct., 1873, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Burma, as asst. commr., Sept., 1874; on forest demarcation duty, Tounghoo, Jan., 1880; dep. supt. of census,

Sept., 1880; judge under the land acquisition act, March, 1881; commr. of excise and stamps, March, 1885; addl. sessions judge, Tenasserim and Pegu, March, 1886; dep. commr., Jan., 1887; commr., Dec., 1890; offd. as jud. commr., Upper Burma, in 1894.

CORBETT, FRANK VINCENT, Lieut.-Col., R.E., India Public Works Dept.—Arrived, 13th Dec., 1866; asst. engr., Feb., 1867; exec. engr., March, 1871; employed chiefly on the Betwa canal; suptg. engr., Dec., 1889; chief engr. and sec. to govt. of N.W. Prov. and Oudh, irrign. branch, June, 1894.

CORDERY, JOHN GRAHAM, M.A., C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1855; arrived, 22nd Oct., 1856, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and dep. commr.; from Oct., 1864, to Sept., 1868, served in Berar as dep. commr. and asst. rest.; offg. dir. of public instrn., Punjab, 1872-73; offg. sec. to govt. of Punjab, April-Aug., 1878; commr. and supt., Multan, April, 1879; commr., Peshawar, April, 1881; rest., Haidarabad, April, 1883; C.S.I., Feb., 1887; rettd., July, 1888.

CORDNER, ROBERT ALLEN, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 20th Feb., 1868, and employed on irrign. works in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., March, 1871; on famine relief works in Bengal, 1873-74; member of accts. committee, Nov., 1888; suptg. engr., June, 1894.

CORNISH, ROBERT, M.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Lancing Coll., and Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 22nd Nov. 1870, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; on famine relief duty, 1874; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1882; mag. and collr., Dec., 1887; rettd., Aug., 1895.

CORNISH, WILLIAM HENRY, Bengal Police Dept.—Joined the service, 21st Jan., 1859; served in the mil. dept., to May, 1863, when he joined the police dept. as asst. supt.; district supt., April, 1870; served in Assam, 1870-74, and 1879-83.

CORNISH, WILLIAM ROBERT, C.I.E., F.R.C.S., Surgn.-Gen., late Madras Med. Service.—Studied at St. George's Hosp. (scholar and prizeman); apptd. to the Madras med. service, April, 1854; served under the mil. dept., and also as civil surgn. of Coimbatore, 1857-58; sec. to the dir.-gen. of the med. dept., Oct., 1858, to Feb., 1870; Madras member and sec. to the commn. for the reorganization of med. establs., 1865-66; sec. of Madras med. fund, 1864-70; sanitary commr., Madras, March, 1870, to April, 1880; during the famine of 1876-78, was specially engaged in organising a system of sanitary and med. inspection; supt. of Madras census, 1878; C.I.E., Jan., 1880; surgn.-gen. with the govt. of Madras, 1880-85; member of legisl. council, Madras, Dec., 1883; rettd., April, 1885; fellow of Madras univ., Feb., 1867, and president of the med. faculty, 1880-85; hon. physician to the Queen; author of various contributions to the med. press, and of the Madras census report, 1873; was for some time editor of the 'Madras Quarterly Journal of Medical Science.'

CORNWALL, WILLIAM WOLFAN GARDNER, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 17th Nov., 1861, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., and asst. settlt.

officer; mag. and collr., March, 1881; rettd., Aug., 1887.

COSTELLO, CHARLES PETER, F.R.C.S., Surgn.-Col., late Bengal Med. Dept.—Joined the service as asst. surgn., 10th Feb., 1859; served in the Ambela campaign, 1863-64 (medal and clasp), the Jowaki expedn., 1877-78 (clasp), and the Afghan war, 1878-80 (medal); sanitary commr., Assam, Sept., 1888; principal med. officer and sanitary commr., Assam, June, 1892; rettd., Jan., 1894.

COTGRAVE, THOMAS MANSON, Salt Dept., Bombay.—Joined the service as acting asst. commr. of customs, salt and opium, 30th Jan., 1866; opium inspr., Bombay, May, 1866; confd. as asst. commr., July, 1867; asst. collr., salt rev., Feb., 1874; dep. collr., April, 1890.

COTTON, SIR ARTHUR THOMAS, Kt., K.C.S.I., Gen., R.E.—Joined Addiscombe, 1818; arrived in Madras, Sept., 1821, as 2nd lieut. of engns.; asst. to suptg. engr., tank dept., May, 1822; served with the Burmese expedn., 1824-26; from June, 1826, served as suptg. engr. in the tank dept., and in 1828 was apptd. to the charge of the Cauvery irrign., being also employed in the survey and improvement of the Paumben pass; in 1844 was employed in carrying out the systematic irrign. of the Godavari delta; promoted to chief engr., 1852; in 1858 planned works for the irrign. of the Mahanadi delta; was afterwards employed under the govt. of Bengal, reporting upon water communication between the Ganges and Calcutta; resigned govt. service in 1862; in 1863, was employed under the Madras irrign. co., reporting on their works, and also inspecting the Ganges canal works, and proposed addl. works, which were afterwards carried out as the Lower Ganges canal; also projected works for the Sone irrign., which have since been partially executed; rettd. from the army in 1877.

COTTON, HENRY JOHN STEADMAN, C.S.I., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Magdalen Coll. Sch., Brighton Coll., and King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 29th Oct., 1867; served in the Bengal rev. and jud. dept., and as offg. under sec. to govt. and offg. registrar, high court; asst. sec., govt. of Bengal, June, 1875; junr. sec., April, 1876; mag. and collr., Nov., 1878; offg. commr., Chittagong, 1879, and 1884; sec., board of rev., Jan., 1882; port commr., Calcutta, May, 1887; sec. to govt. of Bengal, finl. and municipal depts., May, 1890; member of Bengal legisl. council, June, 1892; chief sec. to govt., July, 1892; author of a 'Memo. on the Rev. History of Chittagong,' 1880, 'Memo. on the Land Tenures of Bengal,' 1884, 'England and India,' 1883, and 'New India, or India in Transition,' 1885.

COTTON, JULIAN JAMES, B.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Sherborne Sch., and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 24th Dec., 1893, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

COTTON, WILLIAM BENSLEY, B.L., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Wellington Sch. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 12th Dec., 1894, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.

COUCH, RIGHT HON. SIR RICHARD, Kt.—Recorder of Bedford, 1858-62; judge of the high court, Bombay, 1862-66; chief justice, 1866-70;

chief justice of Bengal, March, 1870, to April, 1875, and was prest. of the commn. apptd. to inquire into the charges made against the Gaekwar of Baroda, Feb., 1875; privy councillor, Nov., 1875; member of the jud. committee, Jan., 1881.

COUCHE, THOMAS BENSON, Store Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, India store depôt, 22nd Jan., 1875.

COUCHMAN, MALCOLM EDWARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Leamington Coll., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 7th Dec., 1863, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

COUPER, SIR GEORGE E. W., Bart., K.C.S.I., C.B., C.I.E., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Sandhurst, and served in H.M.'s 15th regt. for two years before joining Haileybury; apptd. to the Bengal civil service, 1846; served in Bengal and the Punjab in various grades; under sec., govt. of India, home and fin. dept., 1853; under sec., foreign dept., 1854; sec. to chief commr. of Oudh, 1855; served through the siege of Lucknow, and was present at several engagements during the Mutiny as A.D.C. to Sir H. Lawrence and Sir John Inglis, and afterwards to Sir James Outram; commr. in Oudh, 1858; sec. to govt. of N.W. Prov., 1859; jud. commr. of Oudh., 1863; chief commr. of Oudh, 1874; lieut.-govr. of N.W. Prov., 1877, as well as chief commr. of Oudh; ret'd., 1882.

COURTENAY, ROBERT, B.A., late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 24th Nov., 1874, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; acted as asst. judge at intervals from Dec., 1883; ret'd., Sept., 1890.

COURTNEY, DAVID CHARLES, Major, R.E., Store Dept., India Office.—Entered the royal engrs., 17th April, 1866; served in the S. African war, 1879 (medal with clasp) asst. inspr. of works, royal arsenal, Woolwich, 1880-83; served in the Soudan expdn. of 1884-85 (medal with two clasps, bronze star); ret'd. from army, Nov., 1887; asst. supt. of the India store depôt, 1st April, 1887.

COWAN, SAMUEL HUNTER, Col., I.S.C., late Survey of India.—First commn., lieut., royal artillery, 9th Dec., 1859; arrived, 9th April, 1860; asst. supt., survey dept., Bengal, 19th Jan., 1867; held charge of the Cachar survey, 1878-79; and of the Hugli River survey, 1882-83; dep. supt., April, 1883; ret'd., Oct., 1891.

COWIE, DONALD WILLIAM GARDEN, M.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at the Charterhouse and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 30th Nov., 1886, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst. collr., Jan., 1892; acting registrar, high court (appellate side), Jan.-April, 1894, and from March, 1895.

COWIE, HENRY GEORGE, B.A., Finl. Dept., Govt. of India.—Educ. at Pembroke Coll., Oxford; from 25th Nov., 1868, served as asst. acctnt.-gen., asst. compt.-gen., and as asst. commr. of paper currency, in the N.W. Prov., and at Calcutta; from April, 1876, served in the Central Prov. as dep. acctnt.-gen. and dep. commr. of paper currency; compt., Assam, March, 1883; dep. acctnt.-gen., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, 1887; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, June, 1889; and Madras, Jan., 1890; dep. compt.-gen., March, 1893.

COWIE, THOMAS RENNY, Col., I.S.C., Oudh Commn.—Arrived as ensign, 12th Aug., 1860; employed under the mil. dept. till Aug., 1869, from which date he served in Oudh as asst. commr., sub-judge, and dep. commr.; cantonmt. mag., Feb., 1881; dep. commr., Oct., 1888; district and sessions judge, April, 1891.

COWLEY, FRANCIS WILLIAM RICE, M.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1863, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; employed in public works dept., March, 1873; dep. commr., wards' estate, Patna, June, 1875; joint mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1878; district and sessions judge, April, 1879; jud. commr., Chota Nagpur, Sept., 1887.

COX, ARTHUR FREDERICK, Indian C.S.—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch. and King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 3rd Nov., 1871, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; on special duty in connection with visit of H.R.H. the Prince of Wales, Nov., 1875; from Sept., 1882, served in the finl. dept.; asst. compt.-gen., July, 1883; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, April, 1885; sub-collr. and mag., Nov., 1887; district and sessions judge, April, 1889; acctnt.-gen., Bombay, Dec., 1889; offg. compt.-gen. and auditor-gen., March, 1894; acctnt.-gen., Bengal, April, 1895; compiled 'N. Arcot District Manual.'

COX, HARRY, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at St. Olave's Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 28th Dec., 1877, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1889; district and sessions judge, Nov., 1894.

COX, HENRY WILLIAM HOLMES, Col., I.S.C., Police Dept., Madras.—Ensign, Madras infantry, Nov., 1857; arrived, 26th Dec., 1857; served in the Madras police from April, 1861; supt., April, 1873; asst. inspr.-gen., Dec., 1881; presidency mag., Oct., 1883; pol. officer in charge of ex-King Thebaw of Burma, Dec., 1885, to April, 1886; dep. inspr.-gen. of police, Aug., 1886; commr. of police, Madras city, Dec., 1891.

COX, JOHN HUGH, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Clifton Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 21st Nov., 1890, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.

COXE, HENRY REYNELL HOLLED, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Winchester, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 1st Nov., 1884, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; employed in Burma, 1887-91, as asst. commr. and under sec. to chief commr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., March, 1893.

CRADDOCK, REGINALD HENRY, Indian C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at Reading Gr. Sch., Wellington Coll., and Keble Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 1st Dec., 1884, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr., asst. to commr. of settlts. and agric., and settlt. officer; commr. of excise, April, 1893; dep. commr., June, 1893.

CRAIG, JAMES, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 19th July, 1866; exec. engr., Sept., 1872; offg. suptg. engr. and sec. to the agents to the govr.-gen. for Rajputana and Central India, April-Oct., 1891;

offg. suptg. engr. and sec. to the agent for Baluchistan, April, 1892; suptg. engr., June, 1892; employed in Burma from March, 1893.

CRAMPTON, ARTHUR CECIL, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1874, and employed in the Central Prov. and the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., May, 1883; transfed. to Burma, Sept., 1890.

CRANBROOK (EARL OF), RIGHT HON. GATHORNE GATHORNE-HARDY, G.C.S.I., P.C.—Born, 1814; educ. at Shrewsbury Sch., and at Oriel Coll., Oxford; called to the bar at the Inner Temple in 1840, and practised as a barrister for several years; contested Bradford unsuccessfully in 1847; sat in the House of Commons for Leominster from 1856 to 1865, when he was returned for Oxford; under sec. of state for the home dept., 1858; prest. of the poor law board, July, 1866; sec. of state for the home dept., 1867-68; sec. of state for war, Feb., 1874; created Viscount Cranbrook, May, 1878; sec. of state for India, March, 1878, to May, 1880; lord prest. of council, 1885-86, and again, 1886-92; created Earl of Cranbrook and Baron Medway, Sept., 1892.

CRASTER, GEORGE AYTON, Col., R.E., late Public Works Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—First commn. as lieut., 11th Dec., 1849; from Nov., 1853, served as asst. and exec. engr. in Assam, Oudh, and the Punjab; transfed. to the N.W. Prov., March, 1869; offg. suptg. engr., and dep. sec. to govt., N.W. Prov., public works dept., April, 1872; suptg. engr., April, 1872; ret'd., 1886.

CRAWFORD, CLEMENT EDWARD, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Clifton Coll. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 10th Nov., 1892, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.

CRAWFORD, JAMES ADAIR, Indian C.S.—Educ. at Rugby; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 17th Sept., 1878, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; from Feb., 1881, served in the pol. dept., and acted as asst. to the agent for Rajputana, boundary settlt. officer in Mewar, and asst. to the rest. at Haidarabad; attaché, foreign dept., May, 1882; in charge of office of press commr., 1882-83; asst. commr., Ajmir, April, 1884; asst. sec., foreign dept., Jan., 1885; pol. agent, Quetta and Pishin, Nov., 1885; junr. under sec. to govt., foreign dept., March, 1888; acting first asst. and sec. for Berar to rest. at Haidarabad, May, 1890; under sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., April, 1892; on special duty in connection with the revision of Aitchison's 'Treaties,' Oct., 1892; and again with the Archduke of Austria during his tour in India, 1892-93; offg. pol. rest., Persian Gulf, July-Dec., 1893; rev. commr., Baluchistan, March, 1895.

CRAWFORD, RICHMOND IRVINE, C.I.E., Col., I.S.C., Sind Commn.—First commn., Oct., 1857; arrived, 6th Nov., 1857; served in the pursuit of the Gwalior rebels, &c., in 1858 (medal and clasp for Central India); from Sept., 1864, served in Sind as dep. collr. and mag., and supt. of police; pol. supt. of Thar and Parkar, May, 1875; dep. commr., Thar and Parkar, Aug., 1881; collr. and mag., Shikarpur, June, 1885; and subsequently at Karachi; C.I.E., Jan., 1896; has acted for various periods as commr. in Sind.

CRAWFURD, JAMES, B.A., B.L., late Indian

C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at the Coll. Sch. and Univ. of Glasgow, and Dublin Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 23rd Dec., 1868, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. under sec., govt. of India, home dept., Feb., 1875; under sec., govt. of Bengal, July, 1876; first inspr. of registrn. offices, Dec., 1877; registrar, high court, Nov., 1878; district and sessions judge, March, 1885; ret'd., Nov., 1894.

CRAWLEY, CHARLES EDWARD, Finl. Dept., Govt. of India.—Joined the dept., and was attached to the acctnt.-gen.'s office, Bombay, 25th Feb., 1874; asst. commr., paper currency, Bombay, March, 1878; asst. acctnt.-gen., Calcutta, Oct., 1883; Bombay, Aug., 1884; asst. comptr., India treasuries, Jan., 1889; asst. acctnt.-gen. and examr. of local accts., Bengal, March, 1889; on foreign service, as comptr.-gen. to the Nizam's govt., Aug., 1893.

CRAWLEY, GEORGE BURRIDGE, Capt., I.S.C., Jud. Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—First commn., 30th Jan., 1878; entered civil employ as cantonmt. mag., July, 1885.

CRAWLEY-BOEVEY, ARTHUR WILLIAM, M.A., B.L., late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at Rugby, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 21st Nov., 1868, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and special asst. to pol. agent; from May, 1873, to Dec., 1874, served in Baroda as acting asst. rest.; in special charge of Sachin state, Dec., 1874; on special duty at Baroda, Feb., 1875; on famine duty at Kaladgi, Aug., 1877; talukdari settlt. officer, Gujarat, June, 1879; special officer for the settlt. of claims to giras and watans in Baroda territory, 1883 and 1885; acting chief presidency mag. and rev. judge, Bombay, May, 1887; junr. collr. and mag., May, 1889; senr. collr. and mag., Nov., 1891; ret'd., Aug., 1893.

CREAGH, BOYLE PURDON, Commander, R.I.M., Marine Dept., Bengal.—Joined the service, 26th March, 1878; commander of 'Tenasserim,' Sept., 1880; transport officer, Suakim, March, 1885; captain in turn of 'Canning' and 'Clive,' 1885-91; acting port officer, Madras, Oct., 1891; and Bombay, April, 1892; apptd. port officer, &c., Calcutta, April, 1893.

CREGEEN, ARCHIBALD CRELLIN, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the dept., June, 1859, as asst. engr., Central Prov.; transfed. to Punjab, Feb., 1861; exec. engr., April, 1866; railway dept., Central India, Holkar state railway, Oct., 1870; engr.-in-chief, provl. light railway survey extensions, Lucknow, June, 1878; Nimach-Nasirabad survey, Nov., 1878; W. Rajputana state railway, Aug., 1879; Vizagapatam-Raipur railway survey, Oct., 1882; construction establt., Nagpur-Bengal railway, Oct., 1884; agent and chief engr., Indian Midland railway, Feb., 1886; ret'd., Feb., 1888.

GRESSWELL, CHARLES ARTHUR, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Burma Commn.—First commn., royal artillery, Jan., 1866; arrived, 12th Jan., 1867; joined the Burma commn., Feb., 1871, as offg. asst. commr. and cantonmt. mag. at Tounghoo; personal asst. to chief commr., Dec., 1875; boundary officer, Sandoway, March, 1881; prest., Sandoway town committee, April, 1884; deputy commr., March, 1885.

CROFT, SIR ALFRED WOODLEY, K.C.I.E., M.A., Educl. Dept., Bengal.—Apptd., 8th Dec., 1865; served as profr., presidency coll., principal,

Dacca coll., and inspr. of schools; dir. of public instrn., from Aug., 1878; prest. of the commn. to draw up a code for European schools, Oct., 1881; member of the educ. commn., 1882-83; C.I.E., May, 1884; K.C.I.E., Feb., 1887; member of the legisl. council of Bengal, Feb., 1887; on special duty for the preparation of a report on educn. in British India, 1887; fellow, member of the syndicate and prest. of the faculty of arts of Calcutta univ.; vice-chancellor, Dec., 1893.

CROFTON, JAMES, Lieut.-Gen., late R.E.—Second lieut., Bengal engrs., 9th Dec., 1844; joined the public works dept., Nov., 1847; asst. engr., Punjab circle, Dec., 1849; exec. engr., Punjab irrign., 1858; suptg. engr., Jan., 1862; supt., Sutlej canal surveys; chief engr., irrign., and under sec. to govt., Punjab, Sept., 1864; inspr.-gen. of irrign. and dep. sec. to govt. of India, public works dept., April, 1874, to Jan., 1882, when he resigned the service.

CROLE, CHARLES STEWART, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Edinburgh High Sch. and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 9th Dec., 1863, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and sub-collr. and joint mag.; district and sessions judge, Kistna, June, 1881; collr. and mag., Nov., 1882; district and sessions judge, Aug., 1886; member of board of rev., Dec., 1890; and of legisl. council, Dec., 1893; compiled the 'Chingleput District Manual.'

CROMBIE, ALEXANDER, M.D., Surgn.—Lieut.-Col., Bengal Med. Service.—Joined the service, 30th March, 1872; offg. rest. surgn., Calcutta med. coll. hosp., Jan., 1873; profir., med. coll., in addn., Oct., 1873; transid. to Burma, Feb., 1875, and apptd. junr. civil surgn., Rangoon, and health officer to the port; from April, 1877, served in Bengal as civil surgn.; also at Simla as joint med. officer, 1880-82; civil surgn., Dacca, with charge of the jail, &c., March, 1882; surgn. supt., presidency gen. hosp., Calcutta, June, 1889.

CROMER (BARON), RIGHT HON. EVELYN BARING, G.C.B., G.C.M.G., K.C.S.I., C.I.E.—Lieut., royal artillery, June, 1858; capt. in 1870, and major in 1876; A.D.C. to Sir Henry Storks, in Ionian Islands, in 1861, and sec. to him in 1865, during the enquiry into the outbreak in Jamaica; private sec. to the govr.-gen. of India (the Earl of Northbrook), 1872-76; commr. of the Egyptian public debt, 1877-79; comptr.-gen. in Egypt, Sept., 1879; finl. member of council of govr.-gen. of India, June, 1880; agent and consul-gen. in Egypt, and a minister plenipotentiary in the diplomatic service, Aug., 1883; finl. asst. at the conference in London on Egyptian finance, 1884; G.C.B., Jan., 1895; has 1st class of Medjidie.

CRONIN, JOHN JOSEPH, Capt., I.S.C., Burma Commn.—First commn., Aug., 1880; employed in Burma as asst. commr., July, 1886.

CROOKE, WILLIAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Erasmus Smith's Gr. Sch., Tipperary, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 2nd Nov., 1871, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; dep. commr., July, 1889; mag. and collr., Feb., 1890; author of a rural and agricl. glossary for the N.W. Prov. and Oudh, an 'Ethnographical Handbook for the N.W. Prov. and Oudh,'

'Popular Religion and Folklore of N. India,' and 'Gazetteer of Jalesar.'

CROSS (VISCOUNT), RIGHT HON. RICHARD ASSHETON, P.C., G.C.B., G.C.S.I.—Born, 30th May, 1823; educ. at Rugby Sch., and at Trinity Coll., Cambridge; called to the bar at the Inner Temple, 1849, and for several years went the northern circuit; M.P. for Preston, 1857-62, and S.W. Lancashire, 1868-86; sec. of state for the home dept., 1874-80; bencher of the Inner Temple, 1876; hon. LL.D. of Cambridge, Oct., 1878; G.C.B. in 1880; again sec. of state for the home dept., June, 1885, and was apptd. prest. of the royal commn. on the educn. acts; in Lord Salisbury's second administration, Aug., 1886, was apptd. sec. of state for India, and raised to the peerage under the title of Viscount Cross; G.C.S.I., Aug., 1892; lord privy seal in Lord Salisbury's third administration, July, 1895; author of two legal works.

CROSTHWAITE, SIR CHARLES HAUKE TODD, K.C.S.I., late Indian C.S., Council of India.—Educ. at St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1857; arrived, 29th Dec., 1857, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., and settl. officer; from April, 1877, served in the Central Prov. as commr. of the Nagpur and Jabalpur divns., and was apptd. jud. commr., Central Prov., Aug., 1881, and addl. member of the govr.-gen.'s council, Dec., 1881; acting chief commr., British Burma, March, 1883, to April, 1884; chief commr., Central Prov., Jan., 1885; member of the public service commn., Dec., 1886; C.S.I., Feb., 1887; chief commr. of Burma, March, 1887; K.C.S.I., May, 1888; member of council of govr.-gen., Oct., 1890, to Feb., 1891, and again from April, 1892; lieut.-govr., N.W. Prov., and chief commr., Oudh, Nov., 1892, to Jan., 1895; member of council of India, March, 1895; author of 'Notes on the N.W. Prov.' and 'Land Rev. Law.'

CROSTHWAITE, ROBERT JOSEPH, C.S.I., B.A., B.L., Indian C.S.—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 30th Oct., 1863, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr. and joint mag. and dep. collr.; asst. supt., sub-judge, and registrar of Dehra Dun, Dec., 1873; small cause court judge, Allahabad, March, 1875; registrar of the high court, N.W. Prov., Jan., 1878; acting jud. commr., British Burma, Dec., 1878; district and sessions judge, Allahabad, May, 1881; addl. sec. to govt. of India, legisl. dept., Jan., 1883; jud. commr. of the Central Prov., Jan., 1885; member of viceroy's legisl. council, June, 1889; offg. chief commr., Central Prov., July-Oct., 1889; rest. and agent to the govr.-gen., Central India, Jan., 1891; C.S.I., May, 1890; agent to the govr.-gen., Rajputana, March, 1895; author of 'The N.W. Prov. Rev. Law' (legal part).

CROW, WILLIAM, Store Dept., India Office.—Clerk for railway stores, 23rd Oct., 1883.

CROWDY, JOHN HENRY, Lieut.-Col., R.E., late Mil. Works Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn. as lieut., Dec., 1857; posted to Indian establt., Sept., 1870; from Dec., 1870, served as exec. engr. in the Punjab; transfed. to mil. works, 1872, and served in the Punjab, the N.W. Prov., and Bengal; suptg. engr., March, 1886; ret'd., March, 1890.

CROWE, WILLIAM HENRY, B.L., Indian

C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Godolphin Gr.
