HOLMES, WILLIAM, late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Windermere Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 21st Nov., 1868, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; in charge of Amir Yakub Khan, March, 1880; joint mag., April, 1881; private sec. to lieut.-govt., N.W. Prov., March,

1883; asst. commr., Oudh, Dec., 1883; joint mag., June, 1884; dep. commr., Aug., 1887; mag. and collr., Dec., 1888; acted as inspr.-gen. of police in 1893; ret'd., Sept., 1894.

HOLMES, WILLIAM CUTHBERT, P.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 7th Nov., 1877, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr., and in Bombay as offg. asst. acctnt.-gen.; head asst., Coimbatore, Dec., 1884; sub-collr. and joint mag., Jan., 1890; district and sessions judge, S. Kanara, Feb., 1893.

HOLMS, JOHN MITCHELL, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 26th Nov., 1883, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., April, 1889; junr. sec. to board of rev., May, 1889, and Feb., 1891; asst. commr., Oudh, March, 1892; joint mag., Sept., 1892; acted as registrar, high court, in 1894; offg. joint sec. to rev. board, June, 1895.

HOLMWOOD, HERBERT, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Uppingham; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 16th Dec., 1879, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1890; inspr.-gen. of registrn., Nov., 1890; district and sessions judge, Dec., 1894.

HOLROYD, WILLIAM RICE MORLAND, Col., I.S.C., late Educl. Dept., Punjab.—First commn. as ensign, Dec., 1853; served from 11th Aug., 1858, as inspr. of schools, and was apptd. dir. of public instrn., Punjab, Feb., 1868; under sec. to govt., educl. dept., Oct., 1884; reverted to mil. duty, Oct., 1890.

HOLT, JAMES RICHARD, B.A., late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Bective Coll., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1878; arrived, 18th Dec., 1880, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. under sec. to govt., 1884-85; joint mag., Nov., 1888; offd. as profir. in Muir coll., March, 1889; ret'd., Aug., 1892.

HOME, ALEXANDER LEFEVRE, Indian Forest Dept.—From 1st March, 1864, served in the dept. in the Punjab, Central Prov. and Bengal, and was apptd. dep. conservator, Oct., 1871; asst. comptr.-gen. of forests, April, 1877; conservator of forests, Bengal, April, 1883; Upper Burma, Oct., 1892; Assam, Dec., 1894.

HOME, FREDERICK JERVIS, C.S.I., Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., 11th June, 1858; arrived, 9th Sept., 1860; served in the public works dept., N.W. Prov., irrign. branch, from Feb., 1861, as asst. engr. and exec. engr.; suptg. engr., Jan., 1883; offg. chief engr., irrign. branch, and joint sec. to govt., N.W. Prov., 1885; on special duty in the Bheartpur state, Oct., 1886; chief engr. and joint sec., Punjab, Dec., 1889; inspr.-gen. of irrign. and dep. sec. to govt. of India, public works dept., Nov., 1890; C.S.I., May, 1892; reverted to mil. dept., Oct., 1894; ret'd., May, 1895.

HOME, ROBERT, C.I.E., Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., Bengal engrs., 1853; asst. engr., irrign. dept., N.W. Prov., Sept., 1856; transfed. to the Punjab, Nov., 1858; exec. engr., July, 1861; supt. of works, Sutlej canal, 1867, and afterwards on the Sirhind canal; suptg. engr., Sirhind canal circle, Dec., 1869; chief engr. and joint sec. for irrign., Punjab, 1882; inspr.-gen. of irrign., and dep. sec. to

govt. of India, public works dept., Nov., 1886; ret'd., Dec., 1889.

HOOKER, WILLIAM HENSLAW, Store Dept., India Office.—Supervisor, 2nd grade, India store depôt, Aug., 1877; 1st grade, July, 1888.

HOOPER, JOHN, B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., and Univ. of London; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 7th Dec., 1871, and served in Oudh as asst. commr.; settlt. officer, Basti, Nov., 1884; mag. and collr., April, 1890; sec., board of rev., Aug., 1890; author of Basti settlt. report.

HOOPER, WILLIAM ROE, Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., Med. Board, India Office.—Asst. surgn., Bengal med. establ., Feb., 1859; arrived, Aug., 1859, and served in Bengal, holding various mil. med. charges; from July, 1861, served in the N.W. Prov. as civil asst. surgn., jail supt., Benares, and civil surgn. at Benares and Lucknow; prest. of the India office med. board, Jan., 1895.

HOPE, CHARLES WILLIAM WEBLEY, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Mannedale Sch., Plymouth, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1885, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Aug., 1860; asst. commr., Oudh, June, 1891; settlt. officer, April, 1893.

HOPE, HUGH RICHARD, Brigadier-Gen.—Joined the service, 4th Nov., 1860; A.D.C. to Sir W. Denison, govr. of Madras, 1863-64; sec. to govt. of Madras, mil. dept., Oct., 1891, to 1st April, 1895, when appt. was abolished.

HOPE, JAMES, B.A., late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1861, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; served in Bombay, 1864-67; principal asst. collr., Ganjam, Sept., 1870; from Jan., 1877, served in the jud. dept. as district and sessions judge; ret'd., 1888.

HOPE, SIR THEODORE CRACRAFT, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., B.L. late Bombay C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury Coll.; arrived, 5th June, 1853, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and educl. inspr.; private sec. to govr. of Bombay, May, 1860; prest. of committee on finances of educl. dept., March, 1861; joint commr. to receive N. Kanara from the Madras govt., Feb., 1862; dep. commr. of customs, salt, and opium, Oct., 1862; collr. and mag., Jan., 1867; from July, 1875, served as addl. member of the govr.-gen.'s council, also acting as sec. to govt. of India in dept. of rev., agric. and commerce, and was apptd. addl. sec. for famine, Dec., 1876; reapptd. as addl. member of govr.-gen.'s council in 1877 and 1878; commr., N. divn., and agent to govr., Surat, Jan., 1878; on special pol. duty in England, 1878; provisional member of council, Bombay, Oct., 1880; sec. to govt. of India, fin. and commerce dept., Aug., 1881; ordinary member of govr.-gen.'s council in charge of public works dept., July, 1882; ret'd., Jan., 1888.

HOPKINS, EGBERT SILAS, Finl. Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, registry dept., 14th Nov., 1883; transfed. to finl. dept., Sept., 1891.

HOPKINS, HENRY MAYNE REID, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 24th Nov., 1887, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Aug., 1892; asst.

supt. and small cause judge, Dehra Dun, Feb.-Dec., 1893.

HOPKINS, JOHN ARTHUR, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 14th Oct., 1861, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., inspr. of schs., and offg. inspr.-gen. of registrn.; mag. and collr., March, 1878; offg. commr., June, 1888; opium agent, Bihar, April, 1893.

HOPKINSON, WILLIAM, Col., Bengal Infantry.—First commn., 1859; from 28th Nov., 1867, served in Bengal as asst. commr. and sub-registrar of assurances; cantonmt. mag., March, 1874; small cause court judge, Nov., 1875; dep. commr., April, 1886; again cantonmt. mag., April, 1887; ret'd., Jan., 1892.

HORDERN, PETER, B.A., late Educl. Dept., Burma.—Apptd. to the dept., 19th Nov., 1861; arrived, March, 1862; asst. prof., presidency coll., Calcutta, March, 1862; dir. of public instrn., British Burma, Nov., 1867; fellow of Calcutta univ., April, 1876; ret'd., 1889; author of a 'Handbook of Rules and Practice of the Educn. Dept., Burma.'

HORMASJI DADABHAI, Khan Bahadur, Bombay Jud. Dept.—Joined the service, 26th April, 1889; judge, Bombay small cause court, July, 1891.

HORN, DAVID BAYNE, F.C.H., A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1874, and employed on irrign. works in Bengal; asst. sec. to govt., irrign. branch, Feb., 1878; exec. engr., March, 1882; under sec., public works dept., 1885-86.

HORNE, WILLIAM OGILVIE, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 7th Dec., 1882, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and acting dep. registrar, high court; head asst., March, 1890; special asst., Ganjam, Feb., 1893; dep. commr. of salt, &c., rev., Nov., 1893; head asst., S. Kanara, March, 1894.

HORSFALL, JEREMIAH GARNETT, C.I.E., B.L., late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Giggleswick Gr. Sch., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1861; served in Madras, and was apptd. district and sessions judge, April, 1878; collr. and mag., Jan., 1879; ret'd., April, 1890; C.I.E., May, 1890.

HORSFALL, THOMAS MENDELSSOHN, B.L., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at the Wilhelms-Gymnasium, Berlin; apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 5th Dec., 1876, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., Madura, Dec., 1884; also forest settlt. officer, Nov., 1885; registrar of high court (appellate side), Sept., 1888; principal asst., Ganjam, Dec., 1890; collr. and mag., Dec., 1891; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1892.

HORSFORD, NORMAN MACLEOD THOMAS, Col., I.S.C.—Arrived, as ensign, 29th Dec., 1857, and served in the Mutiny campaign (medal and clasp); from Aug., 1861, served in the police dept., N.W. Prov., as asst. inspr.-gen., and district supt.; and from Dec., 1867, in Oudh as asst. commr., junr. sec., and offg. dir. of public instrn.; supt. of encumbered estates, Rae Bareli, 1874-76; offg. supt. of excise and stamps, and inspr.-gen. of registrn., April, 1876; cantonmt. mag., Lucknow, March, 1878; city mag. and wasika officer, Lucknow, April, 1883; district judge, April, 1886; dep. commr.,

March, 1887; chief commr., Andaman and Nicobars, April, 1892; reverted to mil. dept., Sept., 1895.

HORSLEY, WILLIAM HENRY, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at King's Sch., Canterbury, and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 31st Oct., 1870; served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and was in charge of the forests in Khandesh, 1872-74; on famine duty, 1874 and 1877; forest settlt. officer, Nov., 1878; second asst. collr., Sept., 1883; judge and sessions judge, May, 1887.

HORSPOOL, WILLIAM PADDISON, India Audit Office.—Educ. at the Newark Coll. Sch.; clerk, and class, 19th Aug., 1878.

HÖRST, HENRY, late Survey Dept., Govt. of India.—Librarian and accountant, Thomason coll., Jan., 1853; sub-assst., great trigonl. survey, Sept., 1854; in charge of picket which repulsed night attack on Murree sanitarium, 1857 (medal); transf. to topogr. survey, April, 1868; supt., Sept., 1882; ret'd., 1889.

HORST, WALTER PHILIPS VONDER, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd., 17th Oct., 1872, and employed on irrign. works in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; offg. asst. sec., public works dept., 1881 and 1882-84; exec. engr., Jan., 1883.

HOSE, JOHN WALTER, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Dulwich and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1886, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., May, 1891; under sec. to govt., April, 1893; registrar, high court, March, 1894.

HOSKING, EDWARD, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch., and King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 1st Feb., 1869, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., asst. judge and sessions judge, and registrar of the high court; judge and sessions judge, Jan., 1884; acted as jud. commr. and judge of the sadar court in Sind, in 1890-91 and 1892; jud. commr., Lower Burma, Nov., 1892.

HOSKINS, WILLIAM HENRY, Col., I.S.C., Police Dept., Madras.—Ensign, Madras infantry, July, 1858; arrived, 7th Sept., 1858; served in dept. from May, 1863, as asst. supt. and supt.

HOSKYN, CHARLES REGINALD, Major, R.E., India Public Works Dept.—First commn., 9th Jan., 1873; apptd., 29th Sept., 1876, and employed on the Punjab N. and Indus valley railways; transf. to superior accts. establ., and served as dep. examr. and examr.; dep. acctnt.-gen. for inspn. duty, March, 1894; examr., guaranteed railway accts., Bombay, Oct., 1894.

HOUGH, ALFRED LACEY, Burma Commn.—Joined the service, 25th Oct., 1870, as sub-assst. conservator of forests; apptd. to the commn. as extra asst. commr., Oct., 1874; dep. commr., Aug., 1891.

HOUGHTON, BERNARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at All Saints, Bloxham, Wellingborough Gr. Sch., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 23rd Jan., 1885, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; transf. to Burma, as asst. commr., Aug., 1886; dep. commr., March, 1891; author of an 'Essay on the Language of the S. Chins,' and other linguistic works.

HOUSE, HENRY FREDERICK, Indian C.S.

(N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Bristol Gr. Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 10th Nov., 1884, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag., asst. supt., Dehra Dun, and asst. commr.; settl. officer, Nov., 1893; has edited the N.W. Prov. rent act.

HOUSLEY, ARTHUR WILLIAM, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 19th Feb., 1872; junr. clerk, June, 1882.

HOUSTON, JOHN, M.D., Dep. Surgn.-Gen., late Madras Med. Dept.—Apptd., 4th Aug., 1855; arrived, 3rd March, 1856, and served in Mysore in various mil. charges, and also as civil surgn., chemical examr., and supt. of jail; from July, 1871, served as surgn. to the Mysore commn., and afterwards to the Mysoregovt.; ret'd., March, 1886.

HOWARD, EDWARD STAFFORD.—Educ. at Harrow, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; called to the bar at the Inner Temple, 1875; M.P. for E. Cumberland, 1876-85, and for the Thornburydivn. of Gloucestershire, 1885-86; under sec. of state for India, April-Aug., 1886; apptd. a commr. of woods and forests, Oct., 1893.

HOWARD, WILLIAM STUART, M.I.C.E., late Bombay Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 10th June, 1859; arrived, 20th Nov., 1859; exec. engr., Dec., 1865; chief engr., Aug., 1888; sec. to govt. of Bombay, public works dept., June, 1892; ret'd., Feb., 1894.

HOWELL, ARTHUR PEARSE, M.A., late Bengal C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1857; arrived, 27th Jan., 1858, and served in the N.W. Prov. and in Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; from April, 1864, to March, 1878, served in the secretariat of the govt. of India, in the finl., home, and rev. depts.; commr., Jabalpur, Central Prov., April, 1877; sec., famine commn., March, 1878; commr., Nerbada, Sept., 1878; acting jud. commr., Central Prov., 1879-80, and again in 1885; commr., Haidarabad assigned districts, June to Dec., 1882; member of educ. commn., 1882-83; offg. rest., Haidarabad, March, 1888; ret'd., Aug., 1889; author of notes on educn. in India, and on jails and jail discipline, and of a paper on educ. legislation.

HOWELL, MORTIMER SLOPER, C.I.E., LL.D., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Christ's Hosp., and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 10th Oct., 1862, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. inspr. of public instrn., April, 1867; asst. supt. and small cause court judge, Dehra Dun, March, 1868; joint mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1871; mag. and collr., Nov., 1879; from March, 1880, served as district and sessions judge in various districts; addl. jud. commr. in Oudh, Dec., 1891; jud. commr., Nov., 1892; author of an Arabic grammar.

HOWLETT, RICHARD ROSS, B.A., Store Dept., India Office.—Educ. at Westminster Sch., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge (scholar); junr. clerk, 16th April, 1895.

HUDSON, CHARLES WILLIAM MAITLAND, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Doveton Protestant Coll., Madras, Madras Univ., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 1st Nov., 1887, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., May, 1895.

HUDSON, WILLIAM HEBARD, B.A., late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 17th Nov., 1861; from Jan., 1863, served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; on famine duty in Behar, 1873-75; from May, 1880, served in the jud. dept., N.W. Prov., as civil and sessions judge; ret'd., Jan., 1890.

HUGHES, ARTHUR JOHN, C.I.E., M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 11th July, 1862, and arrived, 7th Dec., 1862; exec. engr., Dec., 1869; asst. sec. to govt. of Bengal, irrign. branch, May, 1872; on famine works, Salem district, Sept., 1877; served under the foreign dept. in Kashmir, 1884-85; suptg. engr., Nov., 1888; suptg. engr. for waterworks, N.W. Prov. and Oudh, 1888-93; offg. chief engr. and sec. to chief commr., Central Prov., April, 1895; author of a report on the feasibility of improved water communication between the Ganges and Calcutta.

HUGHES, DAVID ERSKINE, M.D., F.R.C.S., Surgn.-Col., late Bombay Med. Service.—Joined the service, March, 1866; arrived, 9th Nov., 1866; served under the mil. dept., and also acted as civil surgn.; civil surgn. and supt. of jail, Belgaum, from May, 1882; civil surgn., Poona, Sept., 1890; reverted to mil. dept., Sept., 1892.

HUGHES, EDWARD WILLIAM MACKENZIE, late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the dept., April, 1871, as asst. engr., Punjab irrign.; locomotive supt., Dhond-Mannad state railway, May, 1879; Indus valley railway, Feb., 1881; dep. locomotive supt., N.W. railway, Jan., 1886; on special duty in Mexico in 1887-88; locomotive supt., E. Coast railway, Jan., 1892; ret'd., Aug., 1893.

HUGHES, GEORGE, late Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at the Liverpool Inst., and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1873; arrived, 23rd Oct., 1875, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and offg. under sec.; district judge, Aug., 1890; asst. commr., 1st grade, Dec., 1890; ret'd., Dec., 1891.

HUGHES, JOHN WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at the City of London Sch., and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 25th Nov., 1892, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

HUGHES, RALPH BULLER, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Marlborough, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 4th Nov., 1892, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

HUGHES, THEODORE WILLIAM HUGHES A.R.S.M., F.G.S., late Geological Survey of India.—Apptd. asst., and joined, 11th Nov., 1862; 1st grade officer, April, 1883; suptd. Rewa coal explorations from March, 1883; designation changed to dep. supt.; supt., April, 1887; acted as dir. in 1892; ret'd., Oct., 1894; author of contributions to the survey publs.

HUGHES, WALTER CHARLETON, M.I.C.E., Bombay Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 23rd July, 1868; arrived, 3rd Oct., 1868, and served in various grades of the dept.; asst. sec. to govt., April, 1882; under sec., March, 1884; sec., Nov., 1888; chairman, Bombay port trust, June, 1892.

HUGHES, WILLIAM, B.A., M.I.C.E., Madras

Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 23rd July, 1863, as proby. civil engr.; asst. engr., Sept., 1870; exec. engr., Sept., 1877; suptg. engr., Feb., 1894.

HUGHES, WILLIAM GWYNNE, Col., I.S.C., late Burma Commn.—First commn. as ensign, June, 1858; arrived, 11th Sept., 1858, and was employed on mil. duty and in the police dept., Burma; transfed. to the Burma commn., June, 1864, and served as asst. commr., cantonmt. mag. of Rangoon, town mag. of Akyab, supt. of the hill tracts, Arakan, and dep. commr.; ret'd., June, 1889; author of 'The Hill Tracts of Arakan.'

HUGHESDON, JOSEPH CHARLES, B.L., late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Sutton Valence Gr. Sch., and King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1862; served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., small cause court judge, and sub-collr. and joint mag.; on famine duty, June, 1877; district and sessions judge, Tinneveli, March, 1880; ret'd., Aug., 1888.

HULLAH, ARTHUR, India Telegraph Dept.—Apptd. asst. supt., 20th Feb., 1869, and employed on various lines; supt., Aug., 1885; in charge, Rajputana divn., April, 1892; personal asst. to dir.-gen., Feb., 1893.

HUME, ALLAN OCTAVIAN, C.B., late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury in 1849; arrived, 7th March, 1849; served in N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; mag. and collr., Etawah, March, 1858; commr. of customs, N.W. Prov., Punjab, and Central Prov., July, 1867; offg. sec. to govt. of India, home dept., 1870-71; sec. to govt. of India in dept. of rev., agric., and commerce, June, 1871; member of board of rev., N.W. Prov., Oct., 1879; ret'd., 1882; author of several works on ornithology.

HUME, WILLIAM WHELER, Col., I.S.C., late Bengal Police.—First commn., 5th Aug., 1854; served in the Mutiny (medal and clasp); district supt. of police, Bengal, Aug., 1863; reverted to mil. duty, 1889.

HUMFRESS, GEORGE, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1873, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., May, 1883; employed on N.W. railway from Jan., 1886.

HUMFREY, JOHN, Lieut.-Col., Bombay Police Dept.—Joined the service, 2nd June, 1865, and arrived, 22nd Dec., 1865; served in the Abyssinian expdn. in charge of commissariat camel train, 1868 (medal); asst. supt. of police, Aug., 1873; supt. of police, Aug., 1875; inspr.-gen., Feb., 1890; on special duty to England with Thakur Sahib of Morvi's eldest son, in 1890; services lent to Morvi state in 1893; author of two works on horses and horse breeding.

HUMPHREYS, ROBERT, B.A., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at the Royal Univ. of Ireland and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 29th Dec., 1894, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

HUMPHRYS, ISAAC HUTCHINSON, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office.—Educ. at the Royal Sch., Raphoe, and Trinity Coll., Dublin (exhibr. and prizeman); clerk, 2nd class, 5th July, 1872; junr. clerk, Feb., 1876; senr. clerk, Sept., 1894.

HUNT, SAMUEL BRADSHAW, Surgn.-Col., Indian Med. Service.—Asst. surgn., 31st March, 1865; arrived, 10th Dec., 1865, and served under

the mil. dept., and also as civil surgn. in Madras; surgn., 4th district, and prof. of med. jurisprudence, med. coll., Feb., 1893; reverted to mil. dept., Aug., 1893.

HUNTER, FREDERICK MERCER, C.B., C.S.I., Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Pol. Dept., Bombay.—Arrived as ensign, Bombay infantry, June, 1861; asst. pol. rest. at Aden, Aug., 1871; first asst. there, Sept., 1880; also pol. agent for the Somali coast, and collr. of abkari and dep. collr. of salt rev.; pol. agent, Cutch, 1880-93 and 1894; pol. agent, Kolhapur and S. Maratha country, 1893-94 and 1894-95; transfed. to Mahi Kantha, March, 1895.

HUNTER, GEORGE YEATES, M.R.C.S., L.S.A., Brigade-Surgn., late Med. Dept., Bombay.—Joined the Indian med. dept., 23rd July, 1858; arrived, 9th Sept., 1858, and served in the mil. dept., and as civil surgn. in various stations; civil surgn., Karachi, Feb., 1882; presidency surgn., Bombay, in med. charge of jail, &c., May, 1884; ret'd., Oct., 1888.

HUNTER, JOHN MUIR, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Pol. Dept., Bombay.—First commn., royal artillery, 12th April, 1864; arrived, 14th Dec., 1865; boundary settlt. officer, Baroda and Mahi Kantha states, Oct., 1869; from June, 1872, served as asst. pol. agent in Mahi Kantha, Kolhapur, and Kathiawar; from Feb., 1883, served as supt. of surveys under the rajasthanik court, Kathiawar, and was employed in settling claims in dispute between native states, from Dec., 1884; prest., rajasthanik court, May, 1891; asst. agent, Baroda, Oct., 1891; first asst. pol. agent, Mahi Kantha, Jan., 1892; pol. agent, Mahi Kantha, July, 1893, to Jan., 1894, and Nov., 1894, to March, 1895; prest., rajasthanik court, Jan., 1894.

HUNTER, SIR WILLIAM GUYER, K.C.M.G., M.D., F.R.C.P., F.R.C.S., Surgn.-Gen.—Educ. at King's Coll., London, Aberdeen Univ., &c.; joined the Bombay med. service, 1850; served in the Burmese war and Indian Mutiny; principal of the Grant med. coll., Bombay, 1868; surgn.-gen., Bombay, Feb., 1877; vice-chancellor of the univ. of Bombay, 1879; ret'd., March, 1880; served on the cholera commn. in Egypt, 1883; British delegate to the international sanitary conference at Rome, June, 1885; M.P. for Hackney (central divn.), 1885 and 1886-92.

HUNTER, SIR WILLIAM WILSON, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., LL.D., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at the Univs. of Glasgow, Paris, and Bonn; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 10th Nov., 1862, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; supt. of labour transport, Kushtia, July, 1865; dir. and compiler of the statistical acct. of Bengal, July, 1869; compiled plan for a statistical survey of India, Jan., 1871; offg. under sec., govt. of India, home dept., Feb., 1871; dir.-gen. of statistics, govt. of India, Sept., 1871; on special duty to England for purpose of compiling statistical acct. of Bengal, and 'Imperial Gazetteer,' 1875 and 1876; on special duty in England, 1877-81; dir.-gen. of gazetteers, Oct., 1877; C.I.E., 1878; dir.-gen. of gazetteers and statistics, June, 1878; dir.-gen. of statistics, Dec., 1881; addl. member, govr.-gen.'s council, 1881, 1883, and 1885; prest. of the educn. commn. 1882-83; depd. to give evidence before the committee of the House of Commons on Indian railways, May, 1884; C.S.I., 1884; member of finance committee, Feb., 1886; K.C.S.I., Feb.,

1887; ret'd., Sept., 1887; author of 'The Annals of Rural Bengal,' 1868; 'A Dictionary of the Non-Aryan Languages of India and High Asia,' 1868; memo. on the older records of the board of rev., N.W. Prov., 1870; 'The Indian Mussulmans,' 1871; 'The Province of Orissa,' 1872; 'Famine Aspects of Bengal Districts,' 1874; a collected edition of 'Essays on the External Policy of India,' by the late J. W. S. Wyllie, 1875; 'A Life of Lord Mayo,' 1875; 'A Statistical Account of Bengal,' 1875-77; a 'Statistical Account of Assam,' 1879; 'The Imperial Gazetteer of India,' 1881; 'The Indian Empire,' 1881; 'England's Work in India,' 1881; 'A Brief History of the Indian People,' &c., &c.

HURRY, ARNOLD EARDLEY, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at the City of London Sch. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 25th Oct., 1882, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; from March, 1886, served in Burma as asst. commr. and dep. commr.; re-transfd., Dec., 1890; junr. sec. to govt., March, 1895.

HUTCHESON, GEORGE, M.D., C.M., Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service.—Joined the service as asst. surgn., 1st Oct., 1866; served on gen. and mil. duty, and in 1870-71 as chemical analyst; on special duty, cholera enquiry, 1872-73; sanitary commr. and supt.-gen. of vaccn., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, May, 1889; on depts. to mil. duty, April-Oct., 1895; author of 'Cholera, its Causes, and Mode of Dissemination,' and 'Cholera as a Preventible Disease.'

HUTCHINGS, FRANCIS SMITH, India Audit Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 23rd June, 1879.

HUTCHINS, SIR PHILIP PERCEVAL, K.C.S.I., B.L., late Madras C.S., Jud. and Public Dept., India Office.—Apptd. to the Madras civil service from Haileybury, 1857; arrived, 27th Feb., 1858; served as asst. collr. and mag., dep. sec. to govt., sec. to board of examrs., and as judge of the court of small causes, Chittur; private sec. to govr. of Madras, Nov., 1863; registrar, high court, March, 1865; district and sessions judge, Madura, April, 1873; called to the bar (Inner Temple), June, 1875; member of rent law committee, Sept., 1882; acting sec., rev. dept., Dec., 1882; on special duty in connection with reorganization of jud. estabts., March, 1883; board of examrs., May, 1883; judge of the high court, Madras, Nov., 1883; fellow of Madras univ., March, 1884; member of govr.'s council, Dec., 1886; C.S.I., Jan., 1888; member of the council of the govr.-gen., Nov., 1888; K.C.S.I., May, 1891; ret'd., Nov., 1893; sec., jud. and public dept., India office, Dec., 1893.

HUTCHINSON, FREDERICK THOMAS, India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 25th March, 1871, as asst. comptr.; dep. comptr., May, 1874; examr., Sept., 1881; examr., public works accts., Bombay, May, 1890.

HUTCHINSON, JAMES BRID, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Punjab Commn.—Arrived, 26th May, 1864; from March, 1868, served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; district judge, Nov., 1884; dep. commr., Dec., 1884; served under govt. of India, Aug. to Oct., 1887; commr. and supt., Lahore, March, 1894; has publ. a 'Guide to Dalhousie,' and a gen. index of regulations and acts in force in the Punjab.

HUTTON, EDWARD, late Postal Dept., Govt. of India.—Joined the service as supt. of mails,

Jabalpur, 10th Feb., 1858; supt., travelling post office, Nov., 1877; inspr., field force, Peshawar column, Feb., 1879; presidency postmr. at Calcutta, April, 1881; acted as postmr.-gen. at Lahore in 1890, at Bombay in 1891, and at Calcutta from April, 1892; ret'd., April, 1893.

HYDE, HENRY, Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Madras Med. Dept.—Arrived, 27th June, 1867; surgn., Oct., 1871; zillah surgn. and jail supt., April, 1872; civil surgn., Nov., 1884; ret'd., May, 1895.

HYNES, GEORGE JOHN, Postal Dept., Govt. of India.—Entered the dept., 4th May, 1868; asst. dir.-gen. of the post office of India, Oct., 1880; dep. postmr.-gen., Burma, June, 1887; postmr.-gen., Punjab, Jan., 1891; dep. dir.-gen. of post office of India, July, 1891; offg. postmr.-gen., Bengal, March-Aug., 1894.

IBBETSON, DENZIL CHARLES JELF, C.S.I., B.A., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Coll. Sch., Adelaide, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 8th Dec., 1870, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and settlt. officer; dep. supt. of census, Punjab, Aug., 1881; compiler, 'Punjab Gazetteer,' March, 1883; offg. dir. of public instrn., March, 1884; dep. commr., April, 1885; offg. supt., Kapurthala state, Nov., 1885; offd. as 2nd. finl. commr. in the Punjab in 1890; in charge of Gujranwala settlt., May, 1891; member of Deccan agriculturists relief commn., 1891-92; on special duty connected with the cantonmt. act, 1893; offg. sec. to govt. of India, dept. of rev. and agric., July, 1894; promoted to commr., retaining his offg. appt., April, 1895; C.S.I., Jan., 1896; has publ. 'Census Report of the Punjab (1881)'; 'Outlines of Punjab Ethnography,' the Karnal settlt. report, and contributions to the provl. gazetteers.

ILBERT, SIR COURTENAY PEREGRINE, K.C.S.I., C.I.E.—Educ. at Marlborough, and Oxford; scholar, and afterwards fellow of Balliol Coll.; Hertford scholar, 1861; Ireland scholar, 1864; Craven scholar, 1864; Eldon law scholar, 1867; called to the bar at Lincoln's Inn, 7th June, 1869; counsel to educn. dept., 1878-82; law member of the council of the govr.-gen. of India, 1st May, 1882, and was prest. of the council, 3rd Feb. to 8th March, 1886; resigned, 13th Nov., 1886; vice-chancellor of Calcutta univ., 1885-86; asst. parly. counsel to the treasury, 1886; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1895.

IMPEY, WILLIAM HENRY LOCKINGTON, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Wellington Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 7th Nov., 1878, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., Naini Tal, Nov., 1886; under sec. to govt. of N.W. Prov. and Oudh, May, 1888; settlt. officer, Oct., 1889; joint mag., April, 1892; sec. to govt., finl. dept., Aug., 1893; also member of legisl. council, Dec., 1893; mag. and collr., and acting sec. to govt., jud. dept., March, 1895; part author of Jhansi settlt. report.

INAM ALI, Maulvi, B.A., Punjab Commn.—Apptd. asst. prof., govt. coll., Lahore, 31st Oct., 1887; probr. under native civil service rules, 4th Feb., 1888; asst. commr., April, 1891; has publ. in Urdu a transln. and an epitome of Hamilton's lectures on psychology.

INCH, GEORGE FREDERICK, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 9th Oct., 1879.

INGLIS, THOMAS, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Wellington Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1878; arrived, 27th Nov., 1880, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., May, 1890; junr. sec. to board of rev., July, 1892.

INGLIS, WILLIAM ARBUTHNOT, India Public Works Dept. —Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1874, and employed on irrig. works in Bengal; exec. engr., May, 1883; offg. under sec., public works dept., Bengal, 1891-92.

INGRAM, GERALD CONSTANTINE WINNINGTON, B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Bromsgrove Sch., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 13th Dec., 1893, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.

INNES, JAMES JOHN McLEOD, Lieut.-Gen., V.C., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn. in Bengal engrs., 1848; served under the civil engr., Punjab, from 1852; asst. to chief engr., Oudh, Jan., 1857; served during the Mutiny (medal and two clasps, V.C.); exec. engr., Cawnpore, Dec., 1857; garrison engr., presidency, April, 1858; compt. and examr. of accts., Lahore, Feb., 1861; offg. acctnt.-gen., public works dept., 1865-66, and 1867-68; suptg. engr., Punjab, Oct., 1869; acctnt.-gen. and dep. sec., public works dept., June, 1870; chief engr., Sept., 1881, and dep. inspr.-gen., mil. works; inspr.-gen., 1882; ret'd., 1886.

INNES, LEWIS CHARLES, late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Charterhouse and Haileybury, and apptd. in 1844; served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., sub-judge, and civil and sessions judge; judge of the high court, Madras, Feb., 1865, to Sept., 1883, and offd. on several occasions as chief justice; ret'd., 1883; fellow of the univ. of Madras, 1865-83; vice-chancellor, 1874-80.

INNES, WILLIAM, India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. to superior rev. estblt., state railways, July, 1884; served on the E. Bengal and the Punjab N. state railways, and was apptd. traffic supt., Burma state railway, Nov., 1885.

IRELAND, WILLIAM DE COURCY, B.A., LL.D., late Burma Commn.—Joined the service as acctnt. in office of commr. of Pegu, April, 1859; asst. supt. of forests, Toungheoo, April, 1860; collr. of customs, Nov., 1860; from April, 1861, served in the commn. as asst. commr., town mag. and dep. commr.; acted also as inspr.-gen. of police; boundary officer, Akyab district, Sept., 1880; addl. sessions judge, Tenasserim, Jan., 1886; commr., Irawadi, July, 1886, and Arakan, Jan., 1890; ret'd., Dec., 1890.

IRVINE, GEORGE DUNCAN, late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1861; served in Madras from May, 1865, as asst. collr. and mag., sub-collr. and joint mag., and acting collr. of sea customs at Madras; district and sessions judge, July, 1882; ret'd., June, 1891.

IRVINE, ROBERT HAMILTON GRANT, late Bengal Police Dept.—Joined the service as dep. mag. and dep. collr., Aug., 1861; apptd. asst. supt. of police, April, 1863; district supt., July, 1869; ret'd., Sept., 1891.

IRVINE, WILLIAM, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at St. Anne's Sch., Brixton, and King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 12th Dec., 1863, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst.

mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; mag. and collr., Dec., 1881; ret'd., 1888; author of 'Canal Rates versus Land Revenue,' 'The Rent Digest: or the Law Procedure relating to Landlord and Tenant, Bengal,' 'Bangash Nawabs of Farukhabad,' and Ghazipur settlt. report.

IRVING, L. A., Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., Army Med. Dept.—Med. officer to govr. of Bombay (Rt. Hon. Lord Reay), 1885 to 1890.

IRWIN, ALFRED MACDONALD BULTEEL, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 24th Nov., 1876, and served in the Punjab till June, 1877, and subsequently in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Aug., 1886; offd. as commr. in 1894, and from June, 1895.

IRWIN, GEORGE ROBERT, B.A., Indian C.S.—Educ. at Uppingham, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 16th Nov., 1878, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr.; asst. sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., Dec., 1886; first asst. sec. for Berar to rest., Haidarabad, March, 1892; pol. agent, Jhalawar, April, 1893.

IRWIN, HENRY, C.I.E., M.I.C.E., Madras Public Works Dept.—Joined the service as asst. engr., Central Prov., 1st Feb., 1868; exec. engr., Sept., 1872; supt. of works, Simla imperial circle, Oct., 1881; C.I.E., June, 1888; acting public works sec. to agents for Rajputana and Central India, Nov., 1888; transf. to Madras public works dept. as suptg. engr., and apptd. consulting architect to govt., Sept., 1889.

IRWIN, HENRY CROSSLY, B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Blackheath Proprietary Sch., St. Edmund's Hall, and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 6th Nov., 1871, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr., joint mag. and dep. commr.; mag. and collr., Nov., 1889; acted as sec. to the board of rev. in 1893; author of 'The Garden of India; or Chapters on Oudh History and Affairs.'

ISAACSON, STEPHEN HENRY HOLDEN, Registry and Record Dept., India Office.—Writer, India office, 15th April, 1869; clerk, 2nd class, Sept., 1878.

ISMAY, STANLEY, B.L., Indian C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at Bromsgrove Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 14th Nov., 1871; served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr., and was on special duty in the Kalahandi state, 1882; registrar, jud. commr.'s court, May, 1884; offg. inspr.-gen. of police and jails, 1885 and 1890; judge, small cause court, Jabalpur, Dec., 1889; dep. commr., Jan., 1890; addl. sessions judge, Jan., 1892; offg. jud. commr., Feb., 1895; author of 'Rules for the Superintendence and Management of Jails in the Central Prov.' (1885).

IVENS, FLETCHER JAMES, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. locomotive supt., Indus valley railway, 3rd Aug., 1878; acted as locomotive supt., Punjab N. state railway, 1880, 1884, and 1885; district locomotive supt., N.W. railway, Jan., 1886; dep. locomotive supt., same line, Sept., 1893.

IVENS, THOMAS EDWARD, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 26th July, 1867, and posted to the Punjab; exec. engr., Sept., 1874; offg. under sec., Oct., 1886, and again, May, 1889; suptg. engr., Oct., 1892.

IZAT, ALEXANDER, M.I.C.E., late India Public

Works Dept.—Joined the dept., June, 1863, as asst. engr., Haidarabad; exec. engr., March, 1868; transf. to railway branch, 1870, and served on Nizam's, Holkar's, Dhond and Manmad, Kathiawar, and Bombay E. Deccan lines; suptg. engr., April, 1880; offg. under sec. to govt. of India, railway branch, Jan., 1883; services lent to Bengal and N.W. railway co. as agent and chief engr., Feb., 1883; ret'd., 1889.

JACKSON, ARCHIBALD, India Audit Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 24th Dec., 1892.

JACKSON, ARTHUR MASON TIPPETTS, M.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Westminster Sch., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 17th Dec., 1888, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. collr. and inspr. of factories, Bombay, Sept., 1892; 2nd asst., July, 1895.

JACKSON, EDWIN JAMES, Survey of India Dept.—Joined the service, 26th Sept., 1864; asst. supt., Nov., 1868; dep. supt., Oct., 1885; supt., March, 1895.

JACKSON, FRANK ERNEST, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at the City of London Sch., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 17th Nov., 1893, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

JACKSON, FREEMAN HENRY, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Pol. Dept., Bombay.—First commn., Bombay infantry, 1861; arrived, 10th Jan., 1862; from Jan., 1867, acted in charge of office of asst. rest., Amreli, and was apptd. to settle boundaries in E. Kathiawar, Jan., 1868; acting asst. rest., Dwarka, and commandant of the Gaekwar's battalion, March, 1869; asst. rest., Baroda, in charge Okhamandal, July, 1874; on duty in Baroda, Jan., 1875; extra asst. gen. supt., thagi and dakaiti dept., June, 1875; from May, 1876, served as asst. agent to the govr.-gen., Baroda, and from Feb., 1879, as supt. of the Gaekwar's contingent, Kathiawar, asst. in charge of Amreli Mahals, and supt. for the improvement of the breed of horses in Kathiawar; offd. for various periods as rest. and govr.-gen.'s agent, Baroda; pol. supt. of Palanpur, Aug., 1895.

JACKSON, HUGH MILBOURNE, Capt., R.E., Survey of India Dept.—First commn., 19th June, 1877; entered the dept. as asst. supt., June, 1883; dep. supt., April, 1889; served in the Burma expdn., 1886, the first S. Shan expdn., 1886-87, and with the N. Karen column, 1888-89.

JACKSON, WILLIAM GRIERSON, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch., and Univ. of London; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 12th Nov., 1874, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and in British Burma as asst. commr.; dep. supt. of family domains of Maharaja of Benares, March, 1882; joint mag., Nov., 1887; dep. commr., Aug., 1891; mag. and collr., Sept., 1892; author of Mirzapur volume of N.W. Prov. Gazetteer.

JACOB, EDWARD FOUNTAINE, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1876, as asst. engr.; district traffic supt., Feb., 1880; attached to public works secretariat, govt. of India, April, 1888; dep. traffic supt., N.W. railway, May, 1890; traffic supt., May, 1892.

JACOB, GEORGE, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch., and Highgate Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 21st Nov., 1879, and served as asst. collr. and mag.;

from May, 1883, served as asst. judge and sessions judge, and as asst. agent for sardars in the Deccan; joint judge and sessions judge, Nov., 1886; acting registrar, high court, appellate side, Feb., 1888; 1st asst. judge, Nov., 1891; judge and sessions judge, March, 1894.

JACOB, HERBERT BRUCE, Col., I.S.C., late Jud. Dept., Bombay.—First commn., Dec., 1857; served from 26th June, 1869, in the educt. dept., Central Prov., and was apptd. inspr. of schools, April, 1871; acting inspr.-gen. of educn., 1882-84; cantonmt. mag., Karachi, 1889-92.

JACOB, LIONEL MONTAGUE, India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1875, and employed on irrign. works in the Punjab; exec. engr., May, 1883; under sec. to govt., irrign. branch, Nov., 1894.

JACOB, SAMUEL SWINTON, C.I.E., A.I.C.E., Col., I.S.C., India Public Works Dept.—Apptd., 17th Nov., 1862, and posted to Bombay; services lent to Jaipur state, July, 1867; exec. engr., Sept., 1870; public works sec. to agent in Rajputana, 1886, 1887, and 1889; C.I.E., May, 1890; suptg. engr., Jan., 1893.

JACOB, STEPHEN, C.S.I., Indian C.S.—Educ. at Highgate Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 16th Nov., 1871, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr., and in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; on special duty, legisl. dept., govt. of India, 1879 and 1880; asst. compt.-gen., Nov., 1880; under sec., fin. and commerce dept., July, 1882; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, 1883-84, and 1885-86; dep. auditor-gen., 1884-85; sec. to the fin. committee, March, 1886; acctnt.-gen., and commr. of state paper currency, Bombay, May, 1888; compt. and auditor-gen. and head-commr. of paper currency, Nov., 1891; acted as sec. to fin. and commerce dept., 1892-93, and 1894; C.S.I., Jan., 1895.

JACOB, SYDNEY LONG, Lieut.-Col., R.E., India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 13th January, 1869, and served on irrign. works in Bombay and the Punjab as asst. engr. and exec. engr.; served through the Afghan war, 1878-79 (medal); suptg. engr., Oct., 1890.

JADU NATH ROY, Rai Bahadur, Bengal Jud. Dept.—Joined the service, 13th July, 1861, as offg. munsif; sub-judge, July, 1873; small cause court judge, June, 1883; addl. judge, Calcutta small cause court, Aug., 1890; 4th judge, Oct., 1893.

JAMES, ARTHUR HUTTON, Police Dept., Bengal.—Joined the service, 16th Feb., 1863, as asst. supt. of police, Assam; also asst. commr., Nov., 1866; district supt., Oct., 1873; transf. to Bengal, Dec., 1875; asst. inspr.-gen., railway police, Nov., 1884.

JAMES, HENRY EVAN MURCHISON, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at the Cathedral Sch., Carlisle, and Durham Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 14th Dec., 1865, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and as branch inspr.-gen. of registrn., and educt. inspr. in Sind; under sec. to govt. in the rev., fin., and gen. depts., Nov., 1875; postmr.-gen., Bombay, Nov., 1877; postmr.-gen., Bengal, March, 1882; acted as dir.-gen. of the post office in India, 1883-85; dep. dir.-gen., Nov., 1885; senr. collr. and collr. and mag., Ahmadabad, April, 1888; commr., June, 1890; commr.

in Sind, Nov., 1892; member of govr.-gen.'s legis. council, Feb.-March, 1895.

JAMES, STEPHEN HARVEY, Indian C.S.—Educ. at Probus Gr. Sch., and Shrewsbury Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 30th Oct., 1870, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; junr. sec., board of rev., May, 1875; under sec. to govt., N.W. Prov., Aug., 1877; registrar, high court, May, 1881; legal remembrancer, N.W. Prov., May, 1884; offg. dep. sec. to the govt. of India, legis. dept., March, 1885; sec. to the govt. of India, legis. dept., Sept., 1887.

JAMESON, ARTHUR SAMUEL, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 1st Jan., 1886, and served in the locomotive dept., N.W. railway; transfed. to E. Bengal state railway, Aug., 1888; district locomotive supt., July, 1889; locomotive supt., Oct., 1890.

JAMESON, ROBERT, M.D., Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., Sanitary Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Joined the Bengal med. service, Oct., 1865; arrived, 11th April, 1866, and was employed on civil and mil. duty; in 1868 was in med. charge at Calcutta of troops and coolies, en route to Abyssinia; served in Abyssinia with 12th Bengal cavalry; served in Bombay and Punjab, 1868 and 1869, and in the N.W. Prov. from 1870 on civil and jail duty; dep. sanitary commr., Nov., 1886.

JAQUET, ROBERT GLOVER, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office.—Junr. clerk, 11th May, 1877.

JARDINE, JOHN, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 30th Oct., 1864, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and asst. judge and sessions judge; acting under sec. to govt., pol., &c., depts., June, 1869; from May, 1871, served in Kathiawar as pol. asst.; also jud. asst., June, 1873; acting registrar of the high court, Bombay, Oct., 1873; sec. to the Baroda commn. for the trial of the Gaekwar, 1875; sec. to the delegates of the govt. of India in the negotiations for a commercial treaty with Portugal, 1877, also acting as dep. sec. to govt. of India, legis. dept.; acting sec. to govt. of Bombay, pol., &c., depts., Nov., 1877; from Feb., 1878, served in British Burma as jud. commr.; prest. of the educ. syndicate, Sept., 1881; acting chief sec. to govt. of Bombay, Oct., 1885; judge of the high court, Bombay, Nov., 1885; acted as chief justice in 1895; author of 'Notes on Buddhist Law'; editor of Moung Tet Pyo's Customary Law of the Chin Tribe, also of a reprint of Dr. Tandy's transln. of Father Sangermano's description of the Burman Empire, and a compilation of printed judgments of the jud. commr. of British Burma; wrote a preface to Dr. Forchhammer's 'King Wagaru's Dhamma-Satham.'

JARDINE, WILLIAM ELLIS, Indian C.S. (Haidarabad).—Educ. at Fettes Coll., Edinburgh, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 20th Feb., 1889, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; second asst. to rest., Haidarabad, Nov., 1893.

JARRETT, HENRY SULLIVAN, C.I.E., Col., I.S.C., late Legis. Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., Dec., 1856; served during the Mutiny (medal); also in the Mahsud Waziri expdn., 1860 (medal and clasp), and the 2nd Yusufzai expdn., 1863 (clasp); sec. to board of examrs., Fort William, March, 1871; in addn., asst. sec., legis. dept., govt. of India, from Aug., 1875;

reverted to mil. dept., June, 1894; C.I.E., Jan., 1895.

JEFFERY, THADDEUS JAMES PASSMORE, India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 15th July, 1869, and employed on irrig. works in Bombay and the Punjab; exec. engr., Feb., 1879; suptg. engr., Oct., 1894.

JEFFREYS, WILLIAM, Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., 8th June, 1854; arrived, 24th Jan., 1856, and served in the irrig. branch, Punjab, and in the N.W. Prov.; on duty in the public works secretariat, govt. of India, 1864-65; transfed. to Bengal, and depd. to investigate a project of irrig. and navigation for the river Gandak, Oct., 1867; offg. asst. to chief engr., and asst. sec. to govt., N.W. Prov., May, 1869; suptg. engr., April, 1872; acting chief engr. and joint sec., irrig. branch, March, 1887; ret'd., Nov., 1887.

JEHANGIR KAIKHASRO NAVROJI KABRAJI, Statutory C.S. (Bombay).—Apptd. prob. under native civil service rules, 14th Jan., 1888; asst. collr. and mag., March, 1888; confd., 24th Feb., 1890; 2nd asst., May, 1895.

JEHANGIR, PESTANJI, C.I.E., Khan Bahadur, late Bombay Uncov. Service.—Joined the service as asst. master, Elphinstone instn., Bombay, 10th Oct., 1853; served in the settlt. dept. from April, 1857; 1st grade dep. collr., Aug., 1869; C.I.E., Jan., 1882; talukdari settlt. officer, May, 1889; ret'd., 1889.

JENKINS, JOHN LEWIS, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Bristol Gr. Sch., and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 15th Jan., 1880, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and from June, 1881, in Sind; 3rd asst. collr. in Sind, June, 1885; manager of encumbered estates in Sind, and educ. inspr., Sept., 1885; 2nd asst. collr., May, 1889; 1st asst., Feb., 1892; asst. commr. in Sind, and Sindi translr. to govt., March, 1892; acting collr. of salt rev., Jan., 1893.

JENKINS, ROBERT GEDON, Col., I.S.C., late Madras Police Dept.—Ensign, Madras infantry, Dec., 1856; arrived, 8th Feb., 1857; served in the Madras police from July, 1863, and was promoted to supt., 1872; reverted to mil. duty, 1890.

JENKINS, THOMAS MORRIS, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Burma Commn.—Ensign, 2nd March, 1866; served in the Burma commn. as asst. commr. from 28th April, 1874; acted as port officer and collr. of customs, Bassein, in July, 1881; settlt. officer, 1884-85; dep. commr., Aug., 1886.

JENKINSON, SIR EDWARD GEORGE, K.C.B., late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Harrow and Haileybury; apptd. in 1856, and served in the N.W. Prov. as joint mag. and dep. collr. of Benares, and afterwards of Farukhabad; served through the Mutiny (medal); also as settlt. officer, mag. and collr., commr. of rev. in Jhansi, and commr. in Oudh; ret'd., 1880; was private sec. to Earl Spencer when lord-lieut. of Ireland, and asst. under sec. for police and crime, 1882-85; C.B. in 1883, and K.C.B. in 1888.

JENNINGS, ROBERT HENRY, Major, R.E., late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., 12th Sept., 1872; arrived, 1st Dec., 1875, and was employed as asst. engr. and in various acting appts. in the Punjab and Baluchistan; served through Afghan war, 1879-80, and the operations against the Maris, 1880; on boundary settlt.

duty in Central India, 1883-84; dep. asst. quartermaster-gen., Simla, July, 1885; district staff officer, Meerut, Sept., 1888; dep. asst. adjutant-gen., Meerut, April, 1890; exec. engr., mil. works dept., June, 1890; acting asst. pol. agent and consul, Bassora, Feb.-Aug., 1892; pol. officer with Sardar Ayub Khan, Feb., 1893; reverted to mil. works dept., Oct., 1894; author of an account of a journey through W. Baluchistan, Persia, &c., and of contributions to Persia and Baluchistan gazetteers.

JERVOISE, ALAN ARTHUR CLARKE, late Bombay C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1856; arrived, 23rd March, 1857, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., pol. agent and collr. of stamp rev., and acting dep. commr. of customs and opium; junr. collr. and mag., Sept., 1877; senr. collr. and mag., March, 1879; ret'd., April, 1887.

JEWETT, THOMAS HOPE, India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Calcutta Civil Engrg. Coll.; apptd. 22nd May, 1872, and posted to mil. works; on famine duty in Mysore, 1877-78; transfid. to Assam, Sept., 1880; exec. engr., April, 1886.

JOHNSON, SIR ALLEN BAYARD, K.C.B., Gen., I.S.C., late Mil. Dept., India Office.—Educ. at Winchester; joined the service (infantry), July, 1846; apptd. to 4th Sikhs as volunteer for service in Burma (medal and clasp for Pegu); in civil employ in Punjab, 1854; served in the Mutiny in 1858 (medal and clasp); asst. adjutant-gen., 1861; mil. dept., Calcutta, 1862; asst. sec. to govt. of India in the mil. dept., 1875; dep. mil. sec., 1876; sec. in the mil. dept., India office, from Jan., 1877; temporarily employed as mil. sec. to the govt. of India, 1879-81; C.B., 1881; ret'd. from India office and was made K.C.B., May, 1889.

JOHNSON, The Most Rev. EDWARD RALPH, D.D., LL.B.—Educ. at Rugby, and Wadham Coll., Oxford; ordained, 1851; vicar of Northenden, Cheshire, 1866-76; archdeacon of Chester, 1871-76; apptd. bishop of Calcutta and metropolitan of India, 14th Dec., 1876.

JOHNSON, EVANS CHARLES, late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 29th Nov., 1867, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; on special duty, Rumpi disturbances, 1879-80; sub-collr. and joint mag., S. Arcot, Jan., 1883; collr. and mag., May, 1886; district and sessions judge, Oct., 1886; collr. and mag. and agent, Ganjam, May, 1890; ret'd., March, 1893.

JOHNSON, JOHN, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 17th Feb., 1872; junr. clerk, June, 1882.

JOHNSON, JOHN WILLIAM, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 23rd Dec., 1867, and posted to Bengal; exec. engr., Jan., 1880; transfid. to Assam, April, 1892.

JOHNSON, THOMAS GEORGE, Capt., I.S.C., Burma Comm.—First commn., Aug., 1877; employed in Burma as asst. commr., March, 1884; dep. commr., March, 1891.

JOHNSON, WARWICK HUSON, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd., 1st Sept., 1865, and posted to the Punjab; transfid. temporarily to mil. works, April, 1872; and to Bengal on famine relief duty, Nov., 1873; exec. engr., March, 1874.

JOHNSTON, The Ven. CHARLES FRANCIS HARDING, M.A., late Ecclesl. Dept., Bombay.—Educ. at Christ's Coll., Cambridge; junr. chaplain,

13th Feb., 1869; senr. chaplain, Feb., 1879; archdeacon of Bombay, Oct., 1888; ret'd., Oct., 1890.

JOHNSTON, DAVID LANDALE, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Boston Gr. Sch., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 10th Nov., 1891, and served as asst. commr. and asst. mag. and collr.

JOHNSTON, JOHN CHARLES, A.M.I.C.E., Madras Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 11th Feb., 1873, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Jan., 1885.

JOHNSTON, JOHN LEECH, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at the Royal Sch., Raphoe, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 26th Nov., 1867, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and asst. judge; judge and sessions judge, Jan., 1884; jud. commr. and judge of sadar court in Sind, May, 1895.

JOHNSTONE, DONALD CAMPBELL, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Edinburgh Acad.; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 10th Nov., 1879, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; junr. sec. to finl. commr., Punjab, Nov., 1888; district judge, Dec., 1891.

JOHNSTONE, FREDERICK JOHN, C.I.E., M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Served the Central Prov., from July, 1861, and was apptd. asst. to the chief engr., Aug., 1873; offg. comptr. of public works accts., 1875; transfid. temporarily to Punjab, Oct., 1879; suptg. engr., Nov., 1880; posted to British Burma, Jan., 1881; transfid. to Assam as sec. in the public works dept., Jan., 1884; chief engr., June, 1888; chief engr. and joint sec. to the govt. of Bengal, June, 1891; sec., March, 1892; fellow of Calcutta univ., 1893; ret'd., Jan., 1896; C.I.E., Jan., 1896.

JOHNSTONE, HUGH, M.A., M.D., Brigade Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., Med. Dept., Burma.—Joined the Indian med. service, 30th Sept., 1867; arrived, 2nd May, 1868, and served in Calcutta at the gen. hosp. and as house surgn. at the med. coll. hosp.; in med. charge of the British residency, Mandalay, July, 1869; civil surgn., Akyab, March 1877; junr. civil surgn., Rangoon, April, 1880, and civil surgn., May, 1890; also port health officer and in charge of the lock hosp. and meteorological observatory.

JOHNSTONE, JAMES WILLIAM HOPE, Col., I.S.C., late Punjab Comm.—Arrived, 4th Feb., 1853; from March, 1858, served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and dep. commr.; offd. as commr. in 1880 and 1883; civil and sessions judge, Peshawar, Sept., 1881; divl. judge, Nov., 1884; ret'd., Feb., 1896.

JOLL, HENRY, late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 26th July, 1867, and served as asst. engr. and exec. engr.; suptg. engr., Nov., 1890; ret'd., March, 1892.

JOLLIFFE, EDWIN WILLIAM, Registry and Record Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 2nd April, 1885.

JONES, GEORGE ALFRED, Indian C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 26th Dec., 1894, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.

JONES, HOWELL HEWSON, Store Dept., India Office.—2nd class clerk, India store depôt, 14th July, 1888.

JONES, JOHN ALFRED, M.I.C.E., Madras Sanitary Dept.—Joined the service as exec. engr., public works dept., Madras municipal commn., Jan., 1877; acted as prest. of the commn. in 1886 and 1888; apptd. sanitary engr. to govt. of Madras, April, 1890; author of 'Switches and Crossings,' and of a manual for district and municipal boards.

JONES, OSWALD ROUTH, Jud. Dept., Madras.—Joined the service, 3rd Sept., 1870, under the mil. dept.; served subsequently in Natal, Mauritius, and in England; in April, 1879, was apptd. to Madras as asst. supt. of police; asst. inspr.-gen., Aug., 1886; supt., Aug., 1889; chief presidency mag., March, 1891.

JONES, THOMAS, B.L., late Bengal Jud. Dept.—Asst. sec., govt. of Bengal, 7th April, 1866; clerk and offg. 5th judge, Calcutta court of small causes, Jan., 1873; registrar and chief ministerial officer of the court, June, 1882; 4th judge, Oct., 1884; and 3rd judge, Oct., 1889; retd., 1892.

JONES, WILLIAM BRITTAIN, C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1855; arrived, 15th Sept., 1856, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Punjab as asst. mag. and asst. commr.; from April, 1865, served in the Central Prov. as dep. commr., sec. to the chief commr., and acting commr. in various divns.; on special duty for drawing up land rev. and tenancy bills for Central Prov., Sept., 1874; commr., Haidarabad assigned districts, April, 1877; rest., Haidarabad, June, 1882; chief commr., Central Prov., May, 1883; retd., Jan., 1885.

JONES, WILLIAM CHARLES NIGEL, M.I.E.E., India Telegraph Dept.—Apptd. asst. supt., 24th Feb., 1870; in charge, Jowaki field telegraphs, 1877-78 (medal); also employed in connection with Kuram valley field force; supt., Aug., 1887; personal asst. to dir.-gen., 1889-90.

JONES, WILLIAM RICHARD SUMPTION, late India Public Works Dept.—Dep. supt., Rurki workshops, Nov., 1867; joined the public works dept., Feb., 1873, as exec. engr., N.W. Prov.; transf. to Rajputana state railways, April, 1879, as dep. locomotive supt.; carriage and wagon supt., Ajmir, Jan., 1880; retd., Dec., 1893.

JONES - BATEMAN, HERBERT BURLETON, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Marlborough; apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 16th Oct., 1876, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr. and joint mag.; small cause court judge, March, 1892; district and sessions judge, April, 1892.

JOPP, CHARLES HENRY, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Winchester, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 187c; arrived, 19th Oct., 1873, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; joint judge and sessions judge, Ahmadabad, April, 1892; registrar, high court, Bombay, May, 1893; acting special judge under Deccan agriculturists relief act, Nov., 1893.

JOPP, JOHN, C.B., Col., late Pol. Dept., Bombay.—First commn., 20th Oct., 1856; served at capture of fort of Sheik Othman, near Aden, in 1858, in the Abyssinian war of 1868 (medal), and in the Afghan war, 1879-80 (medal); dep. quartermaster-gen., Bombay, May, 1888; pol. rest., &c., Aden, May, 1890; C.B., May, 1894; reverted to mil. dept., May, 1895.

JOSCELYNE, DOUGLAS, India Public Works

Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 11th Aug., 1870, and posted to Rajputana; employed in Madras on famine relief, 1878-79; exec. engr., Jan., 1880; transf. to Burma as asst. sec. in the public works secretariat, July, 1888; under sec., Nov., 1889, to March, 1890.

JOSEPH, GEORGE JOHN, India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Calcutta Civil Engrg. Coll.; apptd., 23rd May, 1872, and posted to the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., May, 1883.

JOSEPH, HUGH GORE, M.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at the Charterhouse, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 7th Nov., 1877, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and in Burma (1886-87) as dep. commr.; returned to Madras as head asst.; sub-collr. and joint mag., Feb., 1890; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1894.

JOUBERT, CHARLES HENRY, M.B., Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., Bengal Med. Service.—Joined the service, 30th March, 1872, and was in mil. employ until 1874; served as civil surgn., rest. surgn., presidency hosp., offg. supt. of emigration, offg. health officer for Calcutta, and offg. sanitary commr. for Bengal; profir., med. coll., June, 1890; fellow of Calcutta univ., Jan., 1893.

JOWERS, HAROLD, Indian C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at Shrewsbury, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 15th Nov., 1891, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.

JOYCE, ALFRED, M.I.C.E., Madras Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1874, as asst. engr., and was employed in the N.W. Prov.; transf. to Madras, April, 1883; exec. engr., Jan., 1886.

JOYNER, ROBERT BATSON, M.I.C.E., Bombay Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 8th Oct., 1868, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Jan., 1879; acted as sanitary engr. in 1892; suptg. engr., May, 1894.

JWALA PRASAD, Kunwar, B.A., Statutory C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Apptd. a prob. under the native civil service rules, Dec., 1884; confd., as asst. mag. and collr., Feb., 1887; asst. commr., Nov., 1891; has transf. Sir Alfred Lyall's 'Asiatic Studies' into Hindustani.

JWALA PRASAD, Pandit, M.A., Statutory C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Apptd. a prob. under native civil service rules, July, 1886; confd., 12th March, 1889, as asst. commr.; joint mag., Nov., 1893.

KALI PRASANNA MUKERJI, B.A., B.L., Bengal Jud. Dept.—Joined the service, 23rd Feb., 1866, as munsi; subord. judge, Feb., 1879.

KAVASJEE JAMASJEE BADSHAH, Statutory C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Elphinstone High Sch., Bombay Univ., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 12th Nov., 1879, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; postmr.-gen., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Feb., 1887; acting dep. dir.-gen. of post office, Jan., 1895.

KAYE, WILLIAM, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1858; arrived, 8th April, 1858; served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., and mag. and collr.; commr. of Agra, July, 1885, and of Rohilkhand, Nov., 1889; on special duty in connection with the police re-organisation commn., 1890-91; junr. member of board of rev., Nov., 1891; retd., April, 1893.

KAY-SHUTTLEWORTH, RIGHT HON. SIR
UGHTRED JAMES, Bart., M.P.—Educ. at Harrow,
and London Univ.; M.P. for Hastings, 1869-80;
served for two years on the London sch. board,
and was also a member of the royal comm. on
reformatory and industrial schools; from 1885,
M.P. for the Clitheroe division of N.E. Lancas-
hire; apptd. under sec. of state for India in Feb.,
1886, and shortly afterwards chancellor of the
duchy of Lancaster; parly. sec., admiralty, of
Aug., 1892, to Aug., 1895; was for some time
vice-prest. of Univ. Coll., London.

KEATINGE, GERALD FRANCIS, Indian C.S.
(Bombay).—Educ. at Sherborne Sch. and
Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of
1893; arrived, 10th Dec., 1894, and served in
Bombay.

KEATINGE, RICHARD HARTE, V.C., C.S.I.,
Lieut.-Gen., R.A.—First commn., Bombay artill-
ery, June, 1842; from 1847 was employed in
civil duties on the N.W. frontier, and was
apptd. asst. supt., Nimar, Dec., 1851; served
in the Mutiny (medal and clasp and V.C.), in-
cluding the pursuit of Tantia Topi, 1858-59;
pol. agent, Nimar, 1860; pol. agent, Gwalior,
and subsequently in Kathiawar, 1862; served
in Kathiawar against the rebel Waghirs in 1865;
agent to the govr.-gen. in Rajputana, 1868-74;
also ex-officio chief commr. of Ajmir, 1871-74;
chief commr. of Assam, Feb., 1874, to June,
1878.

KEDAR NATH ROY, M.A., B.L., Statutory
C.S. (Bengal).—From 4th April, 1878, served as
munisf., and on 27th July, 1888, was apptd. to
the statutory civil service as asst. mag. and collr.;
joint mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1893; author of
three legal works.

KEDAR NATH MOZUMDAR, B.A., B.L.,
Bengal Jud. Dept.—Served in the subord. jud.
dept. from 16th Dec., 1865, and was apptd.
subord. judge, Jan., 1879.

KEEFER, WILLIAM NAPIER, Surgn.-Major,
late Indian Med. Dept.—Asst. surgn., Bengal
med. establ., 1st Oct., 1860; served in the
Lushai campaign, 1871-72 (medal and clasp),
the Duffla expedn., 1874-75, the Jowaki cam-
paign, 1877-78 (clasp), the Afghan campaign,
1878-80 (medal and clasp), the Zaimusht expedn.,
1879, and the Egyptian campaign, 1882 (medal
and clasp, bronze star, and 4th class Osmanieh);
senr. med. officer, Port Blair, April, 1884; ret'd.,
Dec., 1889.

KEEGAN, DENNIS FRANCIS, M.D., Brigade-
Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Indian Med. Service.—
Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the
Bengal med. service in 1866; acted as residency
surgn., Indore, 1877-78; served in the Afghan
war, 1879-80 (medal and clasp, bronze star);
apptd. residency surgn., Indore, and civil med.
officer, Central India, June, 1881; ret'd., April,
1894; author of several articles and papers on
med. subjects.

KEELAN, BERNARD CALLAN, Surgn.-Major,
L.K. & Q.C.P., M.R.C.S., late Med. Dept.,
Bombay.—Joined the Bombay med. service, 1st
April, 1867; served under the mil. dept., and
also as civil surgn. and supt. of jail at various
stations; took part in the Abyssinian cam-
paign (medal); civil surgn., Sukkur, Jan., 1881;
civil surgn., &c., Haidarabad, April, 1884; ret'd.,
March, 1891.

KEELAN, EUGENE JOHN, A.M.I.C.E., India
Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason

Coll.; apptd., 3rd Oct., 1868, and employed on
irrign. works in Oudh; transf. to state railways,
Nov., 1879; exec. engr., Oct., 1880.

KEENE, CHARLES JAMES, India Public
Works Dept.—Joined the service, 29th Dec.,
1874, as asst. traffic supt., Punjab N. state rail-
way; served also on the N. Bengal state rail-
way, the N.W. and other railways; district
traffic supt., 1890.

KEENE, HENRY G., C.I.E., M.A. (Hon.),
late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Rugby, and Oxford;
apptd. from Haileybury, 1847; arrived in India,
3rd Oct., 1847; served in the N.W. Prov. as
asst. mag. and collr., asst. supt. of Dehra Dun,
and joint mag. and dep. collr.; supt., Dehra
Dun, May, 1856; mag. and collr., April, 1860;
civil and sessions judge, April, 1867; offd. as
commr. of Agra in 1872 and 1875, and of Allaha-
bad in 1874; ret'd., 1883; fellow of the Calcutta
univ.; author of 'Indian Administration,' 1867;
'Fall of the Mughal Empire,' 1876; 'History
of Hindustan,' 1885; 'Life of Madhava Rao
Sindhia,' 1892; 'History of India for the use of
Students,' 1893; and numerous contributions to
the periodical press.

KEENE, HENRY GEORGE HARRIS, Finl. Dept.,
Govt. of India.—Joined the dept., 14th March,
1874, as asst. acctnt.-gen., asst. compt.-gen.,
and asst. compt., Burma; compt., Haidarabad,
March-July, 1886, and from Oct., 1893.

KEESS, JAMES, M.D., M.R.C.P., L.M.,
M.R.C.S., Dep. Surgn.-Gen., late Madras Med.
Dept.—Served in the subord. med. dept. from
11th Sept., 1848, to May, 1855; asst. surgn.,
4th Aug., 1856; from May, 1862, served as civil
surgn. and jail supt., Karnul, fort surgn.,
Madras, profr. in the med. coll., med. inspr. of
seamen, &c.; fellow of Madras univ., 1882;
principal and profr. of medicine in the med.
coll., and senr. physician, gen. hosp., Aug.,
1883; ret'd., Jan., 1887; author of 'Syllabus of
Medicine for use of Medical Students,' 'Notes
on Zoology and Comparative Anatomy,' 'History
of the Madras Medical College,' &c.

KEITH, STANLEY, Finl. Dept., India Office.—
Educ. at Lancing Coll.; writer, India office,
6th June, 1873; clerk, 2nd class, April, 1879;
joint editor of the India List, 1885-95, and of the
India Office List from Oct., 1895.

KEITH, THOMAS W., late Acctnt.-Gen., India
Office.—Educ. at Highgate Sch.; East India
house writer, old establ., 20th Aug., 1845;
junr. clerk, treasury, 1848; senr. clerk, Sept.,
1858; acctnt.-gen.'s dept., 1860; acctnt., Jan.,
1872; assessor of income tax, 1872; acctnt.-gen.,
Feb., 1879; ret'd., June, 1893.

KELLAKKA COVILAGOM MANAVEDAN
RAJA, B.A., Statutory C.S. (Madras).—Educ.
at the Madras Univ.; apptd. probr. for the civil
service, 4th June, 1880; confd., Aug., 1882;
asst. collr. and mag., July, 1882; head asst.,
Aug., 1888.

KELLEY, WILLIAM, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept.,
India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 9th Oct., 1879.

KELLNER, EDWIN WELSH, C.I.E., late Finl.
Dept., Govt. of India.—Joined the dept., 29th
April, 1870, and served as dep. acctnt.-gen.,
acctnt.-gen., asst. and dep. compt.-gen., and
asst. commr. of paper currency; acctnt.-gen.,
Punjab, March, 1890; ret'd., March, 1894; C.I.E.,
May, 1894.

KELLY, CHARLES ARTHUR, M.A., late Bengal
C.S.—Educ. at Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after

exam. of 1860; arrived, 14th Nov., 1861; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., and asst. supt. of survey; small cause court judge, Jan., 1876; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1879; ret'd., Jan., 1888.

KELSALL, JOHN, late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1862; from March, 1865, to Jan., 1867, served as proby. asst. supt. of police, and subsequently as asst. collr. and mag., and principal asst.; district and sessions judge, Aug., 1880; ret'd., Nov., 1889; compiled the 'Bellary District Manual.'

KEMBALL, SIR ARNOLD BURROWES, K.C.B., K.C.S.I., Gen., late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—Educ. at Addiscombe; 2nd lieut., Bombay artillery, Dec., 1837; served in the Afghan war, 1838-39, and was present at the siege and capture of Ghazni (medal); apptd. asst. to the rest. at Bushire, Feb., 1842; pol. rest., Persian Gulf, May, 1852; consul gen., Bagdad, and pol. agent, Turkish Arabia, 1855 to 1873; served in the Persian expdn. of 1857 (medal and clasp, C.B.); served with the Turco-Persian boundary commn., 1873; during the Russo-Turkish war, 1876-77, was British mil. attaché at the headquarters of the Turkish army.

KEMBALL, CHARLES GURDON, B.L., late Bombay C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury in 1850; arrived, 25th Nov., 1850; served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., asst. judge and sessions judge, jud. asst. commr. (in Sind), and dep. mag.; collr. and mag., Karachi, Nov., 1861; served as judge and sessions judge, Surat, from Jan., 1866; judge of the high court, Bombay, Aug., 1871; acted as member of council, Bombay, in 1880 and 1881; ret'd., 1885.

KEMBLE, WILLIAM, late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury; arrived, 8th April, 1858; served in Bengal, and was apptd. mag. and collr., Oct., 1873; inspr.-gen. of jails, July, 1876; offg. opium agent, Benares, May, 1882; opium agent, Behar, May, 1884; offg. commr., Patna, May-Nov., 1890; acting member of board of rev., June, 1892; ret'd., April, 1893.

KENDALL, EDWARD AUSTIN, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at the Bedford Gr. Sch. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 22nd Oct., 1892, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.

KENNEDY, CHESSBOROUGH GORDON MACKENZIE, Assam Commn.—Educ. at Southampton Coll.; sub-lieut., H.M.'s 59th regt., Nov., 1873, to Jan., 1877; in 1879-80 served as transport officer, Naga Hills field force; apptd. extra asst. commr., Assam, 25th June, 1880; dep. commr., April, 1894.

KENNEDY, HARTLEY, Bombay Police Dept.—Joined the dept., 30th Oct., 1871; asst. supt. of police, Jan., 1871; supt., March, 1876.

KENNEDY, JAMES, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at the High Sch. and Univ. of Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 14th Nov., 1863, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., settlt. officer, and joint mag. and dep. collr.; mag. and collr., March, 1884; ret'd., April, 1890.

KENNEDY, JOSEPH, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at the High Sch. and Univ. of Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 23rd Oct., 1874, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and in Assam (1884-86) as asst. commr.

and dep. commr.; mag. and collr., Bengal, Nov., 1892.

KENNEDY, SIR MICHAEL KAVANAGH, K.C.S.I., Gen., R.E., late Public Works Dept., Bombay.—First commn., Bombay engrs., 11th June, 1841; arrived, Nov., 1842; asst. engr., roads and tanks, June, 1843; exec. engr. at Surat, Aden, and Satara, 1848-60; on special duty, Ceylon, Madras, and Calcutta, 1860-61; suptg. engr., S. divn., April, 1862; sec. to govt. of Bombay, public works and railway depts., July, 1863; resigned, 1880; was controller-gen. of supply, Kabul expdn.

KENNEDY, ROBERT GREIG, India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1873, and employed on irrign. works in the Punjab; exec. engr., Oct., 1881; transf. to Baluchistan, Oct., 1884; returned to the Punjab, Aug., 1890.

KENNEDY, ROBERT MUIRHEAD, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Rugby; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 16th Nov., 1877, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and railway mag., Bandra; 1st asst., May, 1891; in 1891 acted as pol. agent, Mahi Kantha, and as administrator, Rajpipla state; talukdari settlt. officer, Nov., 1891; on depn. to adjudicate on Cutch-Morvi, &c., disputes, June, 1894.

KENNEDY, THOMAS JOHN, B.A., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at the Liverpool Institute, Balliol Coll., Oxford, and London Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1878; arrived, 20th Nov., 1880, and served in the Punjab and Central Prov. (1885-87) as asst. commr., and settlt. officer; district judge, April, 1892; dep. commr., Dec., 1894.

KENNEDY, WILLOUGHBY PITCAIRN, Major, I.S.C., Pol. Dept., Bombay.—First commn., 8th Feb., 1870; from March, 1875, served as asst. to pol. agent, Mahi Kantha, Aden, Zanzibar, Baluchistan, and Kathiawar; asst. to the agent to the govr.-gen., Central India, Aug.-Oct., 1880; special pol. agent, Cambay, 1890-94; second asst. to pol. agent, Kathiawar, Sept., 1893; administrator, Rajpipla state, Oct., 1894; administrator, Navanagar state, July, 1895.

KENNEY - HERBERT, ARTHUR ROBERT, Col., Madras Cavalry.—Cornet, 20th Sept., 1859; arrived, 31st Oct., 1859; mil. sec. to the govr. of Madras, May, 1881, to April, 1884; offg. asst. sec. to govt. of India, mil. dept., Dec., 1881; sec. to the govt. of Madras, mil. dept., Dec., 1885; ret'd., April, 1892.

KENSINGTON, ALFRED, B.A., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Marlborough, and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 12th Feb., 1878, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., asst. settlt. officer, and offg. under sec.; settlt. officer, April, 1884; on special duty in Ambala settlt., Dec., 1889; under sec. to govt. of India, fin. and commerce dept., April, 1890; retransfd. to Punjab as dep. commr., Dec., 1894.

KER, THOMAS, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 16th May, 1874, and employed on state railways in Rajputana; exec. engr., May, 1883; ret'd., May, 1894.

KERNAN, JAMES, M.A., Q.C., late Jud. Dept., Madras.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; judge of the high court, Madras, 3rd May, 1870; fellow of Madras univ., 1871; vice-chancellor, 1885 and 1887; offd. as chief justice, 1885 and 1888; ret'd., July, 1889.

KERR, JOHN HENRY, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Glasgow Acad., Glasgow Univ., and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 29th Nov., 1892, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

KERR, THOMAS COULTER, Capt., Store Dept., India Office.—Educ. at Liverpool Coll.; served during China war, 1860-61, and Abyssinian war, 1868-69, in command of hired troopships; surveyor of shipping, India office, 1st April, 1881.

KERSHAW, LOUIS JAMES, Indian C.S. (Assam).—Educ. at Belfast Methodist Coll. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 31st Oct., 1890, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to Assam as asst. commr., April, 1892.

KETTLEWELL, ARTHUR BRADLEY, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 31st Oct., 1892, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

KEYS, ARTHUR INNES, Indo-European Telegraph Dept., India Office.—Apptd. clerk, London office, Indo-European telegraph dept., 7th Feb., 1880; transfed. to registry and record dept., India office, Jan., 1880; clerk specially attached, Aug., 1891; rejoined Indo-European telegraph dept., June, 1894.

KEYSER, ALFRED, late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at Guildford Gr. Sch., and Royal Acad., Gosport; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 29th Oct., 1865, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; junr. collr. and mag., and pol. agent, Surat, Dec., 1884; senr. collr., &c., April, 1887; acting commr., S. divn., March, 1890; ret'd., March, 1892.

KHANDERAO CHIMANRAO BEDARKAR, Rao Bahadur, B.A., LL.B., Jud. Dept., Bombay.—Joined the service, 25th April, 1863, as Gujarati and Marathi translr. and interpreter, high court; dep. registrar and sealer, Feb., 1867; services lent to the Maharaja Holkar, Oct., 1890; judge, Bombay small cause court, Dec., 1890, continuing to be in foreign service.

KHANDUBHAI GULABHAI DESAI, L.C.E., Bombay Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 11th April, 1870, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., April, 1884; is a fellow of the Bombay univ.

KHARSEDJI MANEKJI KHARSEDJI, Khan Bahadur, B.A., B.L., Jud. Dept., Bombay.—Served from 29th May, 1874, as judge of the court of small causes, Ahmadnagar, and subsequently at Ahmadabad; judge, Bombay small cause court, June, 1888.

KHARSEDJI RATANJI BAMANJI, Statutory C.S. (Bombay).—Probr. under the native civil service rules, and asst. collr. and mag., Nov., 1882; confd., 8th Jan., 1885; 1st asst., Jan., 1895.

KHUSHWAKT RAE, RAE, B.L., N.W. Prov. Administration.—Apptd. proby. asst. mag. and collr., under the native civil service rules, 8th April, 1890; dep. collr., April, 1894.

KIERNANDER, CHARLES ROBERT CAMPBELL, late Finl. Dept., Govt. of India.—Joined the dept. 5th Aug., 1864, and served as asst. and dep. acctnt.-gen., inspr. of local offices of acct., and dep. comptr.-gen.; asst. sec., govt. of India, finl. dept., 1884-85; comptr. of Indian treasuries, April, 1885; dep. auditor-gen., Oct., 1886; dep. comptr.-gen., April, 1891; ret'd., March, 1892.

KILBY, GEORGE CHARLES, B.L., Bengal Jud. Service.—Dep. supt. and remembrancer of legal affairs, 28th Feb., 1876.

KILBY, SANFORD JAMES, late Bengal Preventive Service.—Asst. collr. of customs, Chittagong, 30th June, 1865; dep. mag. and dep. collr., July, 1866; from Aug., 1868, served in the police dept.; on famine relief duty, 1873 and 1876; from Dec., 1876, served in the customs preventive service, and also in Madras as dep. commr., abbkari and salt dept.; ret'd., Nov., 1892.

KILGOUR, FREDERICK COLL., I.S.C., late Police Dept., Madras.—First commn., 11th July, 1857; proby. asst. supt. of police, Nov., 1865; asst. supt., Aug., 1873; supt., June, 1874; dep. inspr.-gen., May, 1892; reverted to mil. dept., July, 1895.

KIMBERLEY (EARL OF), RIGHT HON.
