Bedfordshire, 1892-95; under sec. of state for India, Aug., 1892; under sec. of state, home dept., March, 1894, to July, 1895; author of a 'Life of Mr. Gladstone,' and editor of 'The Letters of Matthew Arnold.'

RUSSELL, ROBERT VANE, Indian C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at Winchester, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 5th Sept., 1893, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.

RUSSELL, SAMUEL, B.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Santry Sch., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 7th Nov., 1877, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settl. officer; special asst., Ganjam, Feb., 1885; sub-collr. and joint mag., April, 1890; district and sessions judge, Chengalpat, Feb., 1893.

RUSTAMJI DHANJIBHAI SETHNA, B.A., LL.B., B.L., Bombay High Court.—Joined the service, 27th March, 1886, as acting profr., Bombay law sch.; confd., Feb., 1889; registrar of the high court, appellate side, July, 1895.

RUTHERFORD, DAVID, Bombay Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, July, 1867, as proby. asst. engr.; exec. engr., Feb., 1873.

RUTHERFORD, JAMES, Col., late Mysore Rev. Survey.—First commn., ensign, 4th Aug., 1859; joined the Mysore rev. survey dept., Jan., 1865; dep. supt., April, 1881; reverted to mil. dept., Nov., 1892.

RUTHERFORD, JOHN MURIC, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained in Europe for the telegraph dept., and apptd. asst. supt., Oct., 1869; arrived, 8th Jan., 1870, and served in the Punjab, and in Rajputana; transf. to state railways, March, 1880, and served as district traffic supt. on the Rajputana state railway; Indus valley state railway, March, 1882; dep. traffic supt., N.W. railway, July, 1886; traffic supt., Oct., 1888; ret'd., 1891.

RYLAND, WILLIAM HENRY, late Jud. Dept., Bengal.—From 10th Dec., 1852, served in the subord. exec. service, Bengal; offg. collr. of stamps, and supt. of abkari rev., March, 1868; offg. asst. sec., govt. of Bengal, May, 1869; from Dec., 1874, served in the jud. dept. and was apptd. judge of the small cause court at Sealdah, April, 1875; offg. supt. of stamps and stationery, April, 1880; ret'd., April, 1887.

RYVES, ROBERT HUGH, Indian C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 15th Nov., 1891, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr. and acting under sec.

RYVES, THOMAS JAMES, Local Capt., late Police Dept., N.W. Prov.—Served as a volunteer in the Mutiny (medal); adjutant of mil. police, Bijnaur, 1859; asst. inspr.-gen., Rohilkhand, April, 1861; from Aug., 1861, served as supt. of police in various districts; asst. inspr.-gen., railway police, June, 1883; dep. inspr.-gen., June, 1892; ret'd., Jan., 1895.

SABONADIERE, ALFRED, Indian C.S.

(N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Charterhouse and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 19th Jan., 1888, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr., and asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., March, 1893.

ST. CLAIR, THE HON. LOCKHART MATHEW, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1876, and posted to Central Prov.; exec. engr., Jan., 1884; services lent to Nepal state, 1889-92; transf. to Assam, April, 1894.

ST. QUINTIN, WILLIAM ASTELL, India Audit Office.—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Blackheath Proprietary Sch.; temporary clerk, 31st Dec., 1866; junr. examr., Dec., 1868; senr. examr., Nov., 1878; designation changed to senr. clerk, Oct., 1892.

SAIYID MAHMUD, B.L., late High Court, N.W. Prov.—Educ. at the Delhi Govt. Coll., Queen's Coll., Benares, and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service as district judge, Oudh, 1st Aug., 1879; served under foreign dept., 1881-82; district judge, Rae Bareli, Nov., 1883; judge of high court, N.W. Prov., May, 1887; ret'd., Dec., 1893; author of 'The Law of Evidence in British India' (in Hindustani).

SALAMAN, SELIM MYER, M.A., M.Ch., Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., Bombay Jail Dept.—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1869; civil surgn. and supt. of jail, Bijapur, Nov., 1870; from Feb., 1872, served under the mil. dept. until June, 1874, when he was apptd. offg. civil surgn. and supt. of jail, Shikarpur; supt., Yerauda central jail, May, 1878; in addn. supt., Poona city jail, Dec., 1878.

SALE, EDWARD LITTLE, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Bury St. Edmunds Sch., and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 22nd Dec., 1894, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

SALIG RAM, Rao Bahadur, late Postal Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Served in the dept. from March, 1847; inspr., post offices, Agra divn., July, 1876; supt., Agra divn., April, 1881; postmr.-gen., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, June, 1884; ret'd., 1887.

SALISBURY (MARQUESS OF), RIGHT HON. ROBERT ARTHUR TALBOT GASCOIGNE CECIL, K.G., P.C., D.C.L., LL.D., M.A.—Born in 1830; educ. at Eton and Christ Church, Oxford, where he graduated, and was elected a fellow of All Souls' Coll. in 1853; represented Stamford in the house of commons from 1853 to 1868, when he succeeded to the upper house; sec. of state for India, July, 1866, to March, 1867; chancellor of the univ. of Oxford, Nov., 1869; in 1871-72 acted with Lord Cairns in conducting the investigation into the affairs of the London, Chatham, and Dover railway company; sec. of state for India, second time, Feb., 1874; was sent as special ambassador to the Sublime Porte in 1876, and acted with Sir Henry Elliot as joint plenipotentiaries of Great Britain at the conference of Constantinople, Dec., 1876; was apptd. sec. of state for foreign affairs on the resignation of Lord Derby, April, 1878, and afterwards accompanied the Earl of Beaconsfield to Berlin, as representative of Great Britain at the congress held there; K.G., July, 1878; prime minister and sec. of state for foreign affairs, June, 1885, to Feb., 1886; prime minister and first lord of the treasury, Aug., 1886; prime minister and sec. of state for foreign affairs, Jan., 1887, to Aug., 1892;

and again from July, 1895; lord warden of the cinque ports, Oct., 1895; has served on the royal commns. on historical manuscripts, 1882, on the housing of the working classes, 1884, and for the Indian and Colonial exhibn., 1886; is hon. col., Bedfordshire regt., and an elder brother of the Trinity House.

SALMON, ALEXANDER McDONALD, India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Madras Civil Engrg. Coll.; apptd. 24th Sept., 1869, and posted to Bengal; employed in Bombay on famine relief works, 1877; district engr., Patna, 1889-95; exec. engr., Aug., 1893.

SALMON, WILLIAM ARTHUR, Col., I.S.C., late Pol. Dept., Bombay.—Arrived, 11th May, 1859; served from March, 1867, in the pol. dept.; asst. pol. agent in Kathiawar, June, 1870; from Feb., 1879, served as asst. pol. agent, Janjira; joint administrator of Rajpipla, July, 1884; and of Sangli, Feb., 1885; asst. to the govr.-gen.'s agent, Baroda, in charge of the Okhamand district, and commandant Waghir corps, Nov., 1887; pol. agent, Mahi Kantha, Jan., 1892; pol. supt., Palanpur, July, 1893; acting pol. agent, Kolhapur, June, 1894; reverted to mil. dept., Aug., 1895.

SAMMAN, HERBERT FREDERICK, Indian C.S. (Assam).—Educ. at Edward VI.'s Sch., Stratford-on-Avon, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 4th Nov., 1892, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to Assam as asst. commr., April, 1894.

SAMPSON, ARTHUR BISKS, B.A., late India Public Works.—Asst. sec., public works dept., govt. of India, May, 1859; under sec., Sept., 1869; ret'd., May, 1887.

SAMUELLS, CHARLES ARCHIBALD, B.L., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch., Blackheath Proprietary Sch., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 25th Nov., 1869, and served in Bengal as asst. mag., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; on famine duty at Champaran, March, 1874; offg. port commr., Calcutta, April, 1886; mag. and collr., Oct., 1886; offg. commr. of excise, Bengal, April to Aug., 1892; ret'd., March, 1895.

SAMUELLS, WILLIAM LEYCESTER, Col., I.S.C.—First commn., 20th Nov., 1857; served from 18th Dec., 1864, in the non-regulation commn., Bengal, as asst. commr. and dep. commr.; special boundary commr. (Rewa and Chota Nagpur), 1868-71; offg. cantonmt. mag. and small cause court judge, Barrackpur, April, 1873; offg. pol. agent, Hill Tipperah, April, 1875; ret'd., April, 1890.

SANDELL, JOSEPH WILLIAM HENRY, Indian Postal Dept.—Joined the service, 1st March, 1862; chief asst. to dep. acctnt.-gen., Assam, Dec., 1878; chief supt., fin. dept., Jan., 1881; transfed. to office of acctnt.-gen., Bombay, Sept., 1881; comptr., Calcutta post office, Jan., 1890.

SANDEMAN, JOHN EDWARD, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Survey of India Dept.—Joined at Adiscombe, 1860; ensign, Bengal infantry, 7th June, 1861; studied at Rurki Coll., and obtained certificate qualifying for engr., public works dept., Sept., 1864; apptd. to the survey dept., Oct., 1864; served with the Hazara expdn., 1868 (medal); dep. supt., Jan., 1879; supt. (new scale), Aug., 1892; dir. of Bengal surveys, April, 1892; promoted to dep. surveyor-gen. (in charge, rev. surveys), in addn., March, 1895.

SANDERS, EDWIN, Surgn.-Major, late Bengal Med. Dept.—Served under the govt. of Bengal from 1st Nov., 1869, to May, 1871; offg. civil surgn., Chittagong, March, 1881; civil surgn., Murshidabad, June, 1889; ret'd., Jan., 1891.

SANDERS, JAMES, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at the Coll. Sch. and Univ. of Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1879, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; offg. city mag., and wasika officer, Lucknow, 1886, 1888 and 1889; joint mag., March, 1888; dep. commr., April, 1894.

SANDERS, RICHARD CARELESS, M.D., F.R.C.S., Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., Bengal Med. Service.—First commn., 1869; in mil. employ till July, 1874, from which date he served as surgn. and civil surgn. at various stations in the N.W. Prov.; ophthalmic surgn. and profir., med. coll., Calcutta, Aug., 1886.

SANDHURST (BARON), RIGHT HON. WILLIAM MANSFIELD, G.C.I.E.—Born, 1855; educ. at Rugby; sub-lieut., Coldstream guards, 1875; succeeded his father (who had been chief of the staff during the Indian Mutiny, commander-in-chief, Bombay, 1860-65, commander-in-chief in India, 1865-70, and commander-in-chief, Ireland, 1870-75) in the title in 1876; resigned his commn., 1879; acted as a lord in waiting to H.M. the Queen from Sept., 1880, to June, 1885; under sec. of state for war, Feb.-Aug., 1886, and again from Aug., 1892; succeeded Lord Harris as govr. of Bombay, 18th Feb., 1895.

SANDIFORD, CHARLES THOMAS, M.I.C.E., M.I.M.E., India Public Works Dept.—Joined the dept. from Sind, Punjab, and Delhi railway as locomotive supt., N.W. state railway, Jan., 1886.

SANFORD, GEORGE EDWARD LANGHAM SOMERSET, C.B., C.S.I., Major-Gen., R.E., late India Mil. Works Dept.—Lieut., R.E., Oct., 1856; served in the China war, 1858 (medal and clasp); served in the public works dept. (mil. works), April, 1872, to Jan., 1878, as asst. and exec. engr., and in the Jowaki campaign, 1878 (medal and clasp); asst. quartermaster-gen., Bengal, 1878-80; during this period served in the Afghan campaign (medal and clasp); dep. quartermaster-gen., Bengal, 1880-85; commanded royal engrs. in Burmese expdn., 1885-86; C.B., Nov., 1886; inspr.-gen. of mil. works, March, 1886; chief engr., March, 1887; C.S.I., Jan., 1890; dir.-gen., mil. works, Sept., 1890; chief engr., mil. works, Bombay, April, 1893; commanding at Meerut, Sept., 1893.

SANKEY, SIR RICHARD HIERAM, K.C.B., Lieut.-Gen., late R.E.—Ensign, 11th Dec., 1846; arrived, Nov., 1848; offg. suptg. engr., Nagpursubsidiary force, 1850-53; supt. of the east coast canal, 1856-57; addl. under sec., govt. of India, public works dept., March, 1857; on outbreak of Mutiny proceeded with special appt. to Allahabad, acting for a time as suptg. engr. and consulting engr. for railways, N.W. Prov.; acted as field engr. at Cawnpore, and was subsequently senr. engr. officer with the Gurkha force under Jung Bahadur; exec. engr. and supt. of convicts, Maulmain, Jan.-Nov., 1859, and subsequently garrison engr., Fort William; asst. to chief engr., Mysore, June, 1861; chief engr., Mysore, 1866-77; services placed at disposal of govt. of Victoria for reporting on the

waterworks of the colony, 1871; dep. sec. to govt. of India, Sept., 1877; commanding engr. of the S. Afghanistan expedy. force under Lieut.-Gen. Stewart, C.B., 1878-79; sec. to govt. of Madras, public works dept., April, 1879; fellow of Madras univ., March, 1881; resigned the public works dept. in 1883; ret'd. from the army, Jan., 1884; chairman, board of works, Dublin.

SAPTE BRAND, C.B., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Winchester; apptd. from Hailebury, 1843; served in the rev. and jud. depts. in Bengal, the Punjab, and N.W. Prov.; mag. and collr., 1857; C.B. in 1859; from 1864 to 1865 served as judge of Ghazipur, and subsequently of Farakhabad; ret'd., 1871.

SARGEAUNT, CHARLES CHESTER, Col., I.S.C., late Rev. Survey Dept., Madras.—Ensign, 31st May, 1856; arrived, 25th Sept., 1856; served in the dept. from Feb., 1867; dep. supt., Dec., 1881; on special duty to revise and republish the survey manual, April, 1886; supt. of rev. survey, June, 1886; ret'd., July, 1887.

SARGEAUNT, RICHARD ARTHUR, Lieut.-Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., Dec., 1861; joined the Bombay public works dept., May, 1864; asst. consulting engr. for railways, Nov., 1864; served in the Abyssinian expdn. of 1868 (medal); examr. of railway accts., Bombay, April, 1872; dep. consulting engr. for guaranteed railways, Calcutta, May, 1877; sec. to railway conference, 1879; rev. asst., railway branch, public works secretariat, govt. of India, 1880; manager, N. Bengal state railway, July, 1882; offg. under sec. to govt. of India, railway branch, Feb., 1883; offg. manager, Punjab N. state railway, June, 1884; offg. manager of E. Bengal railway, June, 1887; offg. consulting engr. for railways, and joint sec., railway dept., Bombay, May, 1888; manager, Oudh and Rohilkhand railway, Jan., 1889; dir.-gen. of railways, and dep. sec. to govt. of India, public works dept., May, 1892; ret'd., Aug., 1895.

SARGENT, SIR CHARLES, Kt., M.A., B.L., late Jud. Dept., Bombay.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Cambridge; called to the bar (Lincoln's Inn), 1848; apptd. judge of the high court, Bombay, 29th April, 1866; chief justice, Aug., 1882; resigned, April, 1895.

SATYENDRANATH TAGORE, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Presidency Coll., and Calcutta Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 12th Dec., 1864, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., asst. judge, and judge and sessions judge; judge, Sholapur-Bijapur, Aug., 1883.

SAUNDERS, ARTHUR LESLIE, Indian C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at the Oratory Sch., Edgbaston, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1882, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; transfid. to the Central Prov., Oct., 1885; asst. commr., Oct., 1888; judge, small cause court, March, 1892; commr. of excise, April, 1892; dep. commr., Feb., 1893; also second sec. to chief commr., Feb., 1894; acting chief sec., Nov., 1894.

SAUNDERS, LESLIE HARRY, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 30th Nov., 1889, and served in Burma as asst. commr., and offg. under sec.; offg. supt., N. Shan states, Sept., 1893, to May, 1894.

SAVAGE, HENRY, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—

Educ. at Liverpool Inst.; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 23rd Oct., 1874, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Sept., 1885; mag. and collr., Jan., 1891.

SCANNELL, HENRY, Police Dept., Bombay.—Educ. at King's Coll., London; joined the service, 28th May, 1868, as acting asst. supt. of police; dep. conservator of forests, Feb., 1871; asst. supt. of police, Feb., 1872; supt., Jan., 1874; supt. of police, Great Indian Peninsula railway, Dec., 1890; offd. as inspr.-gen. in 1890 and 1893.

SCHÄRLIEB, WILLIAM MASON, B.L., late Jud. Dept., Madras.—Served from 25th March, 1847, in subord. posts, and was apptd. first uncov. asst. to the board of rev., June, 1862; resigned the service to practise at the bar, Oct., 1867; actd. as administrator-gen., and as govt. pleader; asst. sec. to govt., jud. and legisl. depts., Dec., 1874; acted as first judge of the court of small causes, Madras, and served from 1885 as presidency mag. for the town of Madras; ret'd., 1890.

SCHLICH, WILLIAM C.I.E., Ph.D., late Forest Dept., Govt. of India.—Apptd. asst. conservator of forests, Dec., 1866; conservator, Bengal, Dec., 1872; transfid. to the Punjab, 1880; inspr.-gen. of forests, April, 1883; ret'd., 1888, and was apptd. principal prof'r. of forestry at Cooper's Hill Coll.; author of 'A Manual of Forestry,' &c.

SCHNEIDER, SIR JOHN WILLIAM, K.C.B., Gen.—Educ. at Christ's Hosp., and afterwards at Addiscombe; ensign, and native infantry, Bombay, 20th Feb., 1841; served in the S. Maharratta campaign, 1845; second in command and commandant of the Kolhapur infantry, and also asst. pol. supt., 1852-59; in 1857-58, took part in the suppression of the Mutiny at Kolhapur (medal); served in the Abyssinian expdn. of 1867-68 (medal, and C.B.); judge advocate-gen., Bombay army, 1868-72; pol. rest. at Aden, 1872-77; commanded N. divn., Bombay army, 1877-81; K.C.B., May, 1889.

SCOBLE, SIR ANDREW RICHARD, K.C.S.I., Q.C., M.P.—Called to the bar at Lincoln's Inn, Jan., 1856; held the offices of clerk of the crown and commr. for taking accts. and for local investigations, under the high court, Bombay, 1863-66; advocate of the high court, 1866-76, and successively held the offices of sheriff, 1866-67, legal remembrancer, 1869-72, and advocate-gen., 1872-77; conducted the prosecution of the Gaekwar of Baroda, 1875; was also member of the legisl. council of Bombay, 1870-76; Q.C., 1876; legal member of the govr.-gen.'s council, 1886-91; C.S.I., Jan., 1889; K.C.S.I., May, 1890; M.P. for Central Hackney from 1892; member of royal commn. on Indian expenditure, May, 1895.

SCONCE, GIDEON COLQUHOUN, B.L., late Jud. Dept., Bengal.—Offg. mag., Calcutta, 12th Nov., 1866; clerk of the Calcutta court of small causes, Dec., 1870; judge of the court, Jan., 1874; chief judge, Oct., 1889; ret'd., Oct., 1893.

SCONCE, JAMES, Col., I.S.C., late Survey Dept.—Joined at Addiscombe, Feb., 1852; lieut., Bengal artillery, Dec., 1853; served during the Mutiny (medal and clasps); served from Oct., 1859, as asst. and rev. surveyor in Bundelkhand, Central Prov., and Bengal; dep. surveyor-gen. (rev. survey branch), Jan., 1879; offd. as surveyor-gen. of India; reverted to mil. duty, April, 1891.

SCOTLAND, SIR COLLEY H., Kt.—Educ. at the Royal Sch., Raphoe; entered as a student

at the Middle Temple in 1840, and was called to the bar in 1843; practised on the common law side of Westminster Hall, and on the Oxford circuit; apptd. chief justice of the supreme court, Madras, 1861, and of the high court of judicature on its establ. in 1862; resigned, 1871; vice-chancellor of the Madras univ., 1861-71; contributed to the Law Journal reports of cases in the court of Queen's bench, 1848-57, and is joint author of a work 'On the Crown Office Practice of the Court of Queen's Bench.'

SCOTT, ARTHUR GUILLUM, Dep. Acctnt.-Gen., India Office.—Educ. at the Royal Agricl. Coll., Cirencester, and at private schs.; East India house writer, old establ., 7th Nov., 1857; junr. clerk, acctnt.-gen.'s dept., India office, Aug., 1860; senr. clerk, Jan., 1872; assessor of income-tax, 1892; dep. acctnt.-gen., June, 1893.

SCOTT, BUCHANAN, C.I.E., Major, R.E., Bombay Mint.—Joined the service, 2nd Aug., 1871; from Oct., 1874, served as asst. engr. in the Punjab; on field service in the Afghan war, 1878-79 (medal); exec. engr., May, 1883; supt. of works and engr.-in-chief, Zhob valley survey, Aug., 1888; acted as mint master, Calcutta, 1891; mint master, Bombay, March, 1895.

SCOTT, EDWIN, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch. and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 15th Jan., 1887, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., Vizagapatam, July, 1893.

SCOTT, GEORGE BATLEY, Survey of India Dept.—Joined the service, 1st May, 1863; asst. supt., survey dept., Nov., 1884; dep. supt., April, 1890; supt. of land record surveys, N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Dec., 1894; served in the Black Mountain expedn., Jowaki-Afridi expedn., Afghan war, Zhob valley expedn., &c.; author of a Pushto vocabulary, 'Pathans of the N.W. Frontier,' reports on various parts of Afghanistan, &c.

SCOTT, JAMES GEORGE, C.I.E., Pol. Dept., Burma.—Apptd. asst. commr., 9th April, 1886; on duty in the Shan states, Dec., 1886; asst. supt., Shan states, Dec., 1888; on duty with Anglo-Siamese boundary commn., 1889-90; supt., N. Shan states, July, 1892; chargé d'affaires at Bangkok, Nov., 1893; on depn. with Anglo-French buffer state commn., Oct., 1894; author of 'The Burman, His Life and Notions,' 'France and Tong-king,' 'Burma as it was, as it is, and as it will be,' 'Burma, the Eastern Country, and the Race of the Bramahs,' and 'The Hill Slopes of Tong-king.'

SCOTT, SIR JOHN, K.C.M.G., M.A., B.L., late Jud. Dept., Bombay.—Judge of the high court, Bombay, 18th Nov., 1882; services lent to the Egyptian govt. from April, 1890; resigned, April, 1891; K.C.M.G., Feb., 1894.

SCOTT, MATHEW HENRY, late Bombay C.S.—Apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 16th Dec., 1863, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. and asst. judge and sessions judge; judge, Khandesh, Feb., 1881; judge and sessions judge, Ahmadnagar, May, 1882; district judge, Satara, April, 1892; ret'd., May, 1893.

SCOTT, ROSS, M.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh.).—Educ. at Omagh Acad., the Royal Acadl. Instn. and Seminary, Belfast, and Queen's Coll., Belfast; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 26th Oct., 1873, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Nov., 1886; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1890.

SCOTT, THOMAS AUGUSTUS, Col., I.S.C., late Central Prov. Commn.—First commn., June, 1857; arrived, 29th June, 1857; from Jan., 1862, served in the police dept., Central Prov., and was apptd. district supt., March, 1864; from June, 1865, served as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Dec., 1882; offd. as commr., 1890-91; reverted to mil. dept., Aug., 1894.

SCOTT-MONCRIEFF, SIR COLIN CAMPBELL, K.C.M.G., C.S.I., Col., R.E., ret'd.—Joined the service as 2nd lieut., Bengal engrs., 13th June, 1856; arrived, 10th June, 1858; served with the Bengal sappers and miners until Feb., 1859, through the campaign in Oudh (medal); joined public works dept., Feb., 1859, and served in the N.W. Prov. in the irrign. branch; principal, Thomsom civil engrg. coll., Sept., 1861; asst. to chief engr., irrign. branch, April, 1869; from Nov., 1869, served on the Ganges canal as exec. and suptg. engr.; in 1877 was sent on depn. to Mysore to serve as chief engr. and famine commr.; C.S.I., May, 1878; again joined the irrign. dept. of the N.W. Prov., Feb., 1879; offd. as sec. to the chief commr. of British Burma, May, 1881; ret'd., Jan., 1883; K.C.M.G., 1887; under sec., public works ministry, Egypt, to 1892, when he was apptd. under sec. for Scotland.

SCULLY, JOHN, Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Dept.—Joined the dept., 1872; served as med. officer, Kashgarh pol. agency, 1874-76; residency surgn., Nepal, 1876-78; med. officer, Gilgit, 1878-80; acting dep. assay master, Calcutta mint, 1882-83; dep. assay master of the Bombay mint, May, 1884; assay master of the Calcutta mint, May, 1886.

SEABROOKE, JAMES HERBERT, B.A., Mil. Dept., India Office.—Educ. at King's Coll., London (Jelf medallist and associate, 1871), and Queen's Coll., Oxford (scholar, 1871-74); junr. clerk, finl. dept., 13th Jan., 1876; mil. dept., 1877; senr. clerk, June, 1887.

SEALY, CHARLES WILLIAM HENRY, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Bombay Pol. Dept.—First commn., 17th July, 1866; cantonmt. mag., Aden, May, 1875; 2nd asst. to rest., Oct., 1885; asst. pol. agent, S. Mahratra country, May, 1889; 2nd asst. to pol. agent, Kathiawar, Sept., 1889; 1st asst. to rest., Aden, and pol. agent for the Somali coast, Aug., 1893; acting pol. rest., Aden, May-July, 1894; joint compiler of an account of the Arab tribes in the vicinity of Aden, and has transl. Van Den Berg's work on Hadthramuth, and part of Carsten Niebuhr's description of Arabia.

SEATON, WILLIAM JOHN, Col., I.S.C., late Forest Dept., Burma.—Ensign, Madras infantry, Sept., 1855; asst. supt. in the forest dept., Dec., 1860; dep. conservator, 1st class, April, 1865; conservator, Nov., 1867; reverted to mil. duty, 1892.

SECCOMBE, HENRY LAWRENCE, Finl. Dept., India Office.—East India house writer, old establ., 14th May, 1856; junr. clerk, finl. dept., Dec., 1866; senr. clerk, Dec., 1882.

SECCOMBE, SIR THOMAS LAWRENCE, G.C.I.E., K.C.S.I., C.B.—Entered the home service of the East India Co. in Jan., 1829, as a clerk in the dept. of auditor of India accts.; on the transfer of the govt. of India to the Crown in 1858 was apptd. asst. finl. sec.; finl. sec., 1859; dir. of mil. funds, and chairman of consulting committee, 1866; chairman of a joint

India office and war office committee for adjusting the charges for services of H.M.'s regts. on the India establ., 1869; and was a member of a similar committee in 1874 and 1879; C.B., Dec., 1869; acctnt.-gen., in addn. to other duties, Feb., 1872; asst. under sec. of state for India, July, 1872; K.C.S.I., June, 1877; apptd. one of three commrs. to represent H.M.'s govt. at the international monetary conference at Paris, 1878; continued asst. under sec. of state until 1881, but resigned his other offices on 31st Jan., 1879; member of a joint India office and admiralty committee on finl. questions connected with the India troop service, 1879 to 1882, when he finally ret'd. from the service; G.C.I.E., Aug., 1892.

SEDDON, CHARLES NORMAN, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Liverpool Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 25th Nov., 1891, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

SEDFIELD, ARTHUR ROBERT WYATT, M.B., Surgn.-Major, Bengal Opium Dept.—In mil. employ from 14th April, 1881; apptd. supt., Chenawan jail, Sept., 1887; civil surgn., Murree, March, 1891; supt., Benares opium agency, Oct., 1891.

SELBY, FRANCIS GUY, M.A., Educ. Dept., Bombay.—Educ. at Wadham Coll., Oxford; arrived, 7th Feb., 1877, and served from that date as prof'r. of logic and moral philosophy in the Deccan coll., Poona; principal, Deccan coll., Dec., 1890.

SELBY, HENRY OLIPHANT, Lieut.-Col., R.E., India Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 13th Jan., 1869; arrived, 26th Dec., 1871; on famine duty in 1874; served in Afghanistan (medal), 1879-81, the latter part of the time as supt., signalling lines of communications; dep. consulting engr. for railways, Bombay, Dec., 1883; under sec. to govt. of Bombay in the railway branch, March, 1892; dep. consulting engr., Nov., 1892; also under sec. (railway), Jan., 1894; sec. and consulting engr., Oct., 1894; rank of supt. engr., March, 1895.

SELLS, ARTHUR, B.A., late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1859; arrived, 28th Nov., 1860; from Nov., 1861, served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; mag. and collr., Muzaffarnagar, Aug., 1878, and civil and sessions judge, Cawnpore, April, 1882; district and sessions judge, Meerut, Oct., 1886; ret'd., May, 1890.

SEN, BIRENDRA CHANDRA, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Dacca Coll., Presidency Coll., and the Univ., Calcutta, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 17th Nov., 1893, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

SETON-KARR, WALTER SCOTT, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Rugby; apptd. from Haileybury in 1842; arrived, 12th Dec., 1842, and served in Bengal as asst. and joint mag. and dep. collr.; mag. of Jessore, 1847; under sec., govt. of Bengal, 1847-53; collr. of Tirhut, 1857; civil and sessions judge, Jessore, 1858-60; prest. of indigo commn., 1860; sec. to govt. of Bengal, 1860-61; legisl. council of India, 1861; commr. of rev. and circuit, Chittagong, and member of provl. legisl. council, 1862; judge, high court, Calcutta, 1862-68; foreign sec., govt. of India, 1868; ret'd., 1870; vice-chancellor of the univ. of Calcutta in 1868 and 1869.

SEWELL, EBENEZER JAMES, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 1st Nov., 1872, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; on famine duty, 1877-78; senr. asst., Ganjam, June, 1882; sub-collr. and joint mag., Dec., 1887; district and sessions judge, Oct., 1889; collr. and mag., Feb., 1890; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1891.

SEWELL, HENRY, M.A., late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Radley, and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1861; served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr., acting under sec., and sub-collr.; collr. and mag., Trichinopoli, May, 1875; district and sessions judge, Nov., 1886; collr. and mag., S. Arcot, Nov., 1887; ret'd., Jan., 1891.

SEWELL, ROBERT, late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at St. Peter's Coll., Radley; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 19th Dec., 1868, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; on famine duty, Oct., 1877, and on special duty in connection with the archaeological survey of the Madras presidency, Jan., 1881, and again, Jan., 1883; sub-collr. and joint mag., Tinnevelly, Sept., 1884; fellow of Madras univ., 1887; from Sept., 1888, served as district and sessions judge, and collr. and mag.; ret'd., Dec., 1894; author of 'A Report on the Amaravati Tope, and Excavations on its Site,' 'Chronological Tables for S. India from the 6th Century, A.D.,' 'A Sketch of the Dynasties of S. India,' vols. I. and II. of the 'Archaeological Survey Series of S. India,' 'Early Buddhist Symbolism,' &c.

SHADBOLT, ERNEST IFILL, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1874, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., May, 1883; services lent to Bhaunagar and Gondal states, 1887-91; engr.-in-chief, Bezvada-Madras railway survey, Feb., 1894; employed in same capacity on Madura-Pamban and Tinnevelly-Quilon railway surveys, Dec., 1894.

SHAKESPEAR, ALEXANDER MURISON WAKE, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Harrow; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 17th Nov., 1878, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Aug., 1887; private sec. to lieut.-govr., March, 1888; asst. commr., Jan., 1890; dep. commr., Feb., 1894.

SHAMS-UD-DIN ALI KHAN, SAIYAD, Khan Bahadur, Berar Administration.—Joined the service, 1st March, 1869; asst. commr., Jan., 1882; special asst. commr., Jan., 1893; dep. commr., May, 1894.

SHANKER MADHO CHITNAVIS, B.A., Statutory C.S. (Central Prov.).—Apptd. prob'r. for the civil service under the statutory rules, July, 1885; confd. as asst. commr., 5th Oct., 1887.

SHARP, FREDERICK, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1875, and posted to Mysore; on famine duty, 1877; transf'd. to Coorg, April, 1881; exec. engr., May, 1883; transf'd. to Central Prov., Dec., 1886; and to Burma, March, 1887.

SHARP, FREDERICK WILLIAM, Store Dept., India Office.—Educ. at Milton Coll., Ullesstorpe; clerk, 2nd class, India store depôt, March, 1885.

SHARP, HENRY GRANVILLE, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Horncastle Gr. Sch.,

Marlborough Coll., and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 27th Jan., 1868; served in the rev. and jud. depts. in Bengal; on famine duty at Madras, 1877; joint mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1878; mag. and collr., Oct., 1884; ret'd., Jan., 1894.

SHARPE, CHARLES FERGUSON, Major-Gen., I.S.C., late Police Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Arrived, as ensign, 1st Feb., 1851, and served under the mil. dept. till Sept., 1858, when he was apptd. to the Oudh mil. police (medal for Mutiny services); from Feb., 1859, served as district supt. in the police dept. in Oudh; reverted to mil. duty, March, 1891.

SHARPE, FREDERIC ROBERT, Registry and Record Dept., India Office.—Educ. at Christ's Hosp.; writer, India office, 7th Aug., 1877; clerk, 2nd class, Sept., 1878.

SHARPE, HENRY GRATTAN, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at Dublin High Sch., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 12th Nov., 1890, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

SHARPIN, VEN. FREDERICK LLOYD, M.A., late Ecclesl. Dept., Bombay.—Educ. at Exeter Coll., Oxford; joined the service, Aug., 1865; arrived, 13th Oct., 1865; served as chaplain at various stations in Bombay and Sind; senr. chaplain, Oct., 1875; senr. presidency chaplain, June, 1880; archdeacon and commissary, June, 1886, also chaplain of Satara; ret'd., Oct., 1888.

SHAW, ARTHUR TREVOR AMBROSE, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 13th Dec., 1883, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf'd. to Burma as asst. commr., Feb., 1886; dep. commr., Oct., 1890.

SHAW, GEORGE WATSON, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at Hertford Gr. Sch., and King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 20th Dec., 1879, and served in British Burma as asst. commr.; dep. commr., May, 1888.

SHAW-MACKENZIE, CHARLES FORBES HUDSON, B.L., late Bombay C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury; arrived, 21st Aug., 1850, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and acting collr. and mag. up to Feb., 1864, and subsequently as judge and sessions judge in various districts; ret'd., Sept., 1885.

SHAW-STEWART, JOHN HERON MAXWELL, Col., R.E., late Public Works Dept., Madras.—First commn. as lieut., Madras engrs., June, 1851; arrived, 31st Dec., 1853; served in the dept. from Jan., 1854, as asst. engr. and exec. engr.; served in the China campaign, 1860 (medal and two clasps); dep. sec. to govt., public works dept., Dec., 1863; under sec., Oct., 1866; comptr., public works accts., Madras, May, 1867; consulting engr. for railways, Madras, Oct., 1867; chief engr., Oct., 1870; joint sec. to govt., railway branch, Feb., 1877; sec. to govt., public works dept., Sept., 1883; member of legisl. council, Madras, Sept., 1883; ret'd., Sept., 1886.

SHEPHARD, HORATIO HALE, M.A., B.L., Madras Jud. Dept.—Profr. of law at Presidency Coll., Madras, 1st Oct., 1873; fellow of Madras univ., 1881; apptd. govt. pleader, June, 1881; member of Madras legisl. council, March, 1885; advocate-gen. of Madras and off. judge of high court, Jan., 1888; confd. as judge of high court, July, 1889.

SHEPHERD, CHARLES EDWARD, Col., I.S.C., India Public Works Dept.—Apptd., 26th Sept., 1864, and posted to Central Prov.; exec. engr., Sept., 1868; transf'd. to mil. works, Dec., 1872; and to state railways, Nov., 1874; dep. consulting engr. for railways, Lucknow, May, 1891.

SHEPHERD, GEORGE EDWARD, Registry and Record Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 4th Sept., 1803.

SHEPHERD, WALTER CURZON, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Sedbergh Sch. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 19th Nov., 1894, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

SHEPPARD, GEORGE FREDERICK, late Bombay C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1854, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; collr. of salt rev., Bombay, Dec., 1871; collr. and mag., Kaira, Feb., 1873; senr. collr. and mag. and pol. agent, Kaira, Nov., 1875; commr., Oct., 1882; ret'd., May, 1889.

SHEPPARD, WILLIAM DIDSBURY, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Hereford Cathedral Sch. and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 13th Dec., 1886, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst. and forest settl. officer, Nov., 1893.

SHERER, JOHN WALTER, C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1846; served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. and joint mag., and asst. sec. to govt.; on special duty to report on reh efflorescence, W. Jumna canal, 1856-57; mag. and collr. of Cawnpore, 1857; civil officer with Gen. Havelock's column in 1857, in the advance on Cawnpore; commr. to enquire into claims against ex-king of Oudh, 1864; judge of Allahabad, 1871, and afterwards of Azimgarh and Mirzapur; ret'd., 1877.

SHERIDAN, CHARLES JAMES, India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 1st April, 1872, and posted to N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., Feb., 1876; employed on state railways, 1876-78.

SHERIDAN, PHILIP, Post Office Dept., Govt. of India.—Served in the postal dept., Punjab, from March, 1861; chief supt., travelling post office, Allahabad, Dec., 1873; chief inspr. of post offices, Assam, Dec., 1873; asst. dir.-gen. of the post office of India, July, 1880; inspr. of railway and mail service, Nov., 1885; dep. dir.-gen. of the post-office of India, April, 1888; postmr.-gen., Lahore, July, 1891.

SHERLOCK-HUBBARD, ISAAC SHERLOCK, India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. compiler of telegraph accts., 1st March, 1872; asst. comptr., April, 1872; asst. examr. of telegraph accts., June, 1877; dep. examr., Jan., 1880; examr., Aug., 1885.

SHERMAN, WILLIAM HANBURY PETTINGALL, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 31st July, 1866, and employed in the Central Prov.; transf'd. to state railways, April, 1874; exec. engr., Jan., 1877; services lent to S. Indian railway co., Feb., 1889, and to Gwalior durbar for Bhopal-Ujjain line, Feb., 1891; suptg. engr., Nov., 1892.

SHERRING, CHARLES ATMORE, B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Westminster and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 11th Nov., 1889, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.

SHEWAN, ALEXANDER, M.A., Indian C.S.

(Bombay).—Educ. at the Gymnasium, Aberdeen, and Univ. of Aberdeen; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 23rd Oct., 1873, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. and asst. pol. agent; on famine duty in 1876 and 1877; from Jan., 1885, served as asst. judge and sessions judge, and acting under sec. to govt.; administrator of Rajpilla state, Nov., 1887; judge and sessions judge, July, 1894.

SHIPLEY, REGINALD HEBER, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Winchester and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 20th Dec., 1883, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; special asst., July, 1890.

SHIRRES, LESLIE PITTENDRIGH, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Aberdeen and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1879; arrived, 18th Dec., 1882, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt., Jan., 1888; offd. as under sec. to the govt. of India, dept. of fin. and commerce, in Nov., 1887, and again from April, 1888; joint mag. and dep. collr., Aug., 1890; author of 'An Analysis of the Ideas of Economics.'

SHORTREDE, ROBERT, India Audit Office. —Temporary clerk, 1st Feb., 1864; junr. asst., Sept., 1866; senr. examr., July, 1873; designation changed to senr. clerk, Oct., 1892.

SHRIRAM BHIKAJI JATAR, C.I.E., B.A., Rao Bahadur, late Haidarabad Administration. —Served in the Bombay educn. dept. from Jan., 1859, and from July, 1867, in Berar as dep. educn. inspr. and educn. inspr.; dir. of public instrn., Haidarabad assigned districts, Aug., 1884; ret'd., Sept., 1894; C.I.E., May, 1894.

SHURLOCK, HENRY JAMES, Store Dept., India Office. —Clerk, 2nd class, India store depôt, 5th Aug., 1886.

SHUTTLEWORTH, ALLEN THORNTON, Forest Dept., Bombay. —Apptd. to the Indian marine, Nov., 1855; served in the Persian war (medal), and in the operations against the Waghirs in Okhamandal; from Nov., 1863, served in the dept. as asst. and dep. conservator, and was apptd. conservator of forests and supt. of botanical gardens, Sept., 1869; while stationed at Alibag was instrumental in saving upwards of a hundred lives from different wrecks (Albert medal, medals of the Royal Humane Society, &c.).

SIBOLD, ERNEST ALFRED, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept. —Trained at Thomson Coll.; apptd., 23rd Dec., 1864, and employed on irrign. works in the Punjab; exec. engr., Sept., 1872; in 1874 served in Bengal on famine relief works; ret'd., Feb., 1895.

SIBTHORPE, CHARLES, Surgn.-Major-Gen., F.R.C.P.I., Madras Med. Dept. —Asst. surgn., 1st April, 1870; arrived, 27th Dec., 1870; civil surgn. and supt. of jail, Banda, July, 1874; from May, 1875, to Nov., 1880, served as rest. surgn., gen. hosp., Madras, and profir. in the med. coll.; also served in the Afghan war, 1878-79 (medal), and in the Burma expdn., 1885-86; from Oct., 1882, served as fort surgn., and profir. in the med. coll.; fellow of Madras univ., 1884; surgn., gen. hosp., and profir., med. coll., Jan., 1883; reverted to mil. duty, May, 1890; surgn.-gen., Madras, May, 1894; author of a 'Clinical Manual.'

SILBERRAD, CHARLES ARTHUR, B.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh). —Educ. at Aldenham Gr. Sch., St. Peter's Coll., Cambridge, and London Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1893;

arrived, 10th Dec., 1894, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.

SILCOCK, HENRY FOSTER, Indian C.S. (Bombay). —Educ. at Drogheda Gr. Sch. and Kingstown Sch., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 24th Nov., 1874, and served in Bombay as asst. mag. and collr., and forest settlt. officer; on famine duty in 1876 and 1877; 2nd asst., April, 1887; 1st asst., April, 1890; collr. and mag. Aug., 1893.

SILCOCK, JAMES GREER, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (Punjab). —Educ. at Armagh Royal Sch., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 16th Nov., 1872, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; district judge, June-Nov., 1888, and March, 1889; dep. commr., April, 1889.

SIM, HENRY ALEXANDER, Indian C.S. (Madras). —Educ. at Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 19th Nov., 1878, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; from Jan., 1883, served as dep. conservator of forests, Karnul, and forest settlt. officer; special asst., Malabar, Feb., 1886; employed under govt. of India as offg. dep. acctnt.-gen., Aug.-Nov., 1887; dep. acctnt.-gen., Madras, Jan., 1888; acting sec. to the commrs. of land rev., 1888-90, and again, 1890-92; sub-collr. and joint mag., July, 1890; collr. and mag., April, 1895.

SIMCOX, ARTHUR HENRY ADDENBROOKE, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bombay). —Educ. at Winchester and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 19th Nov., 1894, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

SIME, JOHN, M.A., LL.D., Educn. Dept., Punjab. —Profr., govt. coll., Agra, April, 1869; from Sept., 1870, served in the Punjab as principal, Delhi coll., till 1877, and subsequently as profir., Lahore coll.; inspr. of schs., Aug., 1887; tutor to the Maharaja of Patiala, May, 1885; dir. of public instrn., Punjab, and under sec. to govt. in the educn. dept., Oct., 1890; author of 'Man and his Duty,' and of works on arithmetic and mensuration.

SIMEON, LIONEL BARRINGTON, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. —Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1876, and posted to the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., Oct., 1886; personal asst. to chief engr. and under sec. to govt., 1892-93.

SIMPSON, SIR BENJAMIN, K.C.I.E., M.D., Surgn.-Gen., late Bengal Med. Service. —Joined the Indian med. dept., 1853; served in civil employ from 1860, as asst. surgn. in Bengal, and was in med. charge of the Bhutan mission in 1863; on special duty on N.E. frontier, 1867-68; civil surgn., Darjiling, April, 1868; from Jan., 1871, served as civil surgn. of Patna with charge of the Mitapur jail; on special duty to the cinchona commn., Feb., 1871; guardian to the Maharaja of Kuch Behar during his visit to Europe, March, 1878; dep. surgn.-gen., S. Afghanistan field force, 1881; principal med. officer, Quetta, 1881; dep. surgn.-gen., March, 1882; sanitary commr., Central Prov., 1882; surgn.-gen. in the Punjab, 1883-84; surgn.-gen., Bengal, 1884-85; sanitary commr. and surgn.-gen. with the govt. of India, March, 1885; K.C.I.E., Feb., 1887; ret'd., March, 1890.

SIMPSON, FRANK DOUGLAS, M.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh). —Educ. at Aberdeen Univ. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 10th Dec., 1894, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr.

SIMPSON, JAMES THOMAS, B.C.E., M.I.C.E., M.I.M.E., India Public Works Dept.—Apptd., 4th May, 1869, and posted to Bengal; exec. engr., March, 1880; transf. to Burma, June, 1887.

SIMPSON, JOHN HOPE, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at foreign schs. and at St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 30th Oct., 1889, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.

SIMSON, D., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at the Gr. Sch. and Marischal Coll., Aberdeen; apptd. from Haileybury in 1845; arrived, 5th April, 1846; served in the N.W. Prov. and in the Punjab as asst. mag. and collr., asst. commr., joint mag., and dep. commr.; commr. in Oudh, June, 1859; extra judge, court of sadar diwani and nizamat adalat, N.W. Prov., April, 1864; judge, chief court, Punjab, April, 1868; ret'd., 1872.

SINCLAIR, DAVID, M.B., C.M., Brig.-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., Jail Dept., Burma.—Joined the Indian med. dept., 1st Oct., 1869; arrived, 14th May, 1870; from 1871, served in British Burma, and was employed in the analysis of potable waters in cantonments, 1871-72; served subsequently as jail supt., offg. civil surgn., and offg. inspr.-gen. of prisons; inspr.-gen. of jails, sanitary commr., supt. of vaccn., and chief of civil med. dept., Burma, Jan., 1884.

SINCLAIR, DUNCAN EVANS, Store Dept., India Office.—Educ. at Westminster Sch., King's Coll. Sch., and Merton Coll., Oxford; writer in post office, British museum, and admiralty, 1873-80; supervisor, 2nd grade, India store depôt, 20th Aug., 1880.

SINCLAIR, WILLIAM FREDERICK, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Armagh Royal Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 21st Nov., 1868, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and was on special duty for demarcation of forests; on famine duty, 1877; first asst. collr. and forest settl. officer, Feb., 1883; junr. collr. and mag., and pol. agent, Cambay, March, 1890; senr. coll. and mag., and pol. agent, Thana, March, 1892; ret'd., June, 1892; author of 'Notes on Arboriculture in the Bombay Presidency.'

SITARAM VISHWANATH PATWARDHAN, Rao Saheb, B.A., Haidarabad Administration.—Joined the Bombay educt. service, 14th Jan., 1867; principal, Poona training coll., Dec., 1876; educt. inspr., April, 1885; dir. of public instrn., Haidarabad assigned districts, Sept., 1894.

SKINNER, JOHN ALLAN CLEVELAND, Indian C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at Wellington Coll., and Keble Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 28th Oct., 1885, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr., and registrar, jud. commr.'s court; dep. commr., Oct., 1894.

SKINNER, RUSSELL MORLAND, Col., I.S.C., late Police Dept., Bengal.—First commn., 7th Dec., 1855; served through the Mutiny; joined the dept. as asst. supt., April, 1862; district supt., Aug., 1863; ret'd., April, 1888.

SKIPWITH, GREY TOWNSEND, Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., 11th June, 1853; joined the dept., Oct., 1860; exec. engr., April, 1865; asst. sec. to chief commr., Central Prov., and asst. to chief engr., Feb., 1870; asst. sec. to govt. of India, public works dept., Feb., 1872; suptg. engr., Feb.,

1880; offd. as chief engr. and joint sec. to govt., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, public works dept. irrign., in 1889; ret'd., Jan., 1893.

SKRINE, FRANCIS HENRY BENNET, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Blackheath Proprietary Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1870, and served as asst. mag. and collr. in Bengal; on famine relief duty, 1874 and 1877-78 (in Madras); supt. of Salem central jail, Jan., 1878; joint mag. and dep. collr., May, 1884; mag. and collr., Aug., 1887; offg. commr., Bhagalpur, 1893-94; offg. collr. of customs, Calcutta, April, 1895.

SLACK, FRANCIS ALEXANDER, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Taunton Coll. Sch., Univ. Coll., Oxford, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 8th Dec., 1876, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; settl. officer of wards' and attached estates in Chota Nagpur; joint mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1888; offd. as commr., Chittagong, Sept., 1890; mag. and collr., May, 1892; acted as sec. to board of rev., 1893.

SLADEN, JOSEPH, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 10th Nov., 1888, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; and in Sind as asst. commr., and Sindhi translr.

SLATER, ALFRED WILLIAM, India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd., 6th Nov., 1872, and employed on irrign. works in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; on famine relief duty in Madras, 1877-78; transf. to buildings and roads branch, Feb., 1881; exec. engr., Nov., 1881.

SLATER, JOHN SAMUEL, Bengal Educl. Service.—From Sept., 1870, served in the public works dept., Punjab, on the Bari Doab and Indus canals; from Nov., 1875, served in the Bengal educn. dept. as profr., civil engrg. dept., presidency coll., and, from April, 1880, as profr. of the Howrah engrg. coll.; principal, Sibpur engrg. coll., Dec., 1891.

SLATER, JOHN SANDERS, B.A., B.L., Bombay Police Dept.—Joined the service, 8th Oct., 1894, as acting chief presidency mag. and rev. judge, Bombay; confd., March, 1895.

SLIGHT, ANDREW MURE, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Royal Coll., Mauritius, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 12th Nov., 1889, and served as asst. collr. and mag.

SLOCOCK, FRANCIS SAMUEL ALFRED, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Marlborough and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 10th Dec., 1891, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

SLOCOMBE, CHARLES, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 22nd July, 1892.

SLY, FRANK GEORGE, Indian C.S.—Educ. at Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 16th Dec., 1887, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr. and settl. officer; under sec. to govt. of India, dept. o rev. and agric., Aug., 1895.

SMALLMAN, HENRY FRANCIS, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd., 3rd Nov., 1868, and employed on irrign. works in the Punjab; exec. engr., Jan., 1880; services lent to Patiala state, 1885-87.

SMART, ALEXANDER WILLIAM, Lieut.-Col., R.E., Madras Publi. Works Dept.—Joined the

service, 15th July, 1868; from March, 1871, served in Madras as asst. engr. and exec. engr.; asst. chief engr. for irrig., and under sec. to govt., 1885-86; acting principal, coll. of engrg., 1891-92, and in 1894.

SMEATON, DONALD MACKENZIE, C.S.I., M.A., Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at the Abbey Park Inst., St. Andrew's, and St. Andrew's Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1867, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; from May, 1870, served in the settlt. dept.; transf. to British Burma as settlt. sec. to chief commr., April, 1879; sec. to chief commr., Burma, in the land rev. and agric. dept., and dir. of agric., May, 1882; dir. of agric. and commerce, N.W. Prov. and Oudh, May, 1886; offg. chief sec. to chief commr., Burma, April, 1887; commr., central divn., Upper Burma, May, 1888; finl. commr., March, 1891; in May, 1892, offd. for a short time as chief commr.; C.S.I., Jan., 1895; author of an annotated edition of the N.W. Prov. rev. act of 1873, 'The Currency of India,' 'The Karens of Burma,' &c.

SMEATON, ROBERT, M.A., LL.D., late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at the Abbey Park Instn., St. Andrew's, and St. Andrew's Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 5th Dec., 1868, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settlt. officer; first asst. sec. to govt., July, 1876; under sec., May, 1877; junr. sec., Aug., 1877; sec., finl. dept., May, 1884; member of provl. legisl. council, Dec., 1891; ret'd., April, 1894.

SMITH, ALBERT CHARLES, Lieut.-Col., R.E., late Madras Public Works Dept.—Arrived, 5th Jan., 1864; joined the dept., Sept., 1864, and was apptd. exec. engr., March, 1870; suptg. engr., Sept., 1889; offg. joint sec. in the public works dept., April-July, 1891; ret'd., Jan., 1894.

SMITH, ALEXANDER, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Marischal Coll., Aberdeen; apptd. after exam. of 1856; arrived, 16th Dec., 1856, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag., and mag. and collr.; senr. supt. of survey, Aug., 1872; commr. of Orissa, and supt. of tributary mehals, Cuttack, Oct., 1879; commr., presidency divn., July, 1884; ret'd., Dec., 1891.

SMITH, ARNOLD CREMER, Store Dept., India Office.—Temporary clerk, 23rd June, 1865; junr. clerk, store dept., Nov., 1866; senr. clerk, Nov., 1876.

SMITH, CHARLES JOHN, Col., R.E., late Madras Public Works Dept.—First commn., Madras engrs., Dec., 1855; asst. engr., Jan., 1858; exec. engr., Nov., 1865; dep. consulting engr. for railways, 1870-83; under sec. to govt., public works dept., May, 1872; suptg. engr., 1st class, Sept., 1883; consulting engr. and joint sec. to govt., railway branch, 1883; chief engr., Dec., 1885; re-apptd. consulting engr., July, 1889; reverted to mil. duty, July, 1891; ret'd., May, 1892.

SMITH, FRANCIS WHITMORE, Mil. Dept., India Office.—Educ. at Bedford Sch. and Cheltenham Coll.; temporary clerk, 23rd Jan., 1864; junr. clerk, mil. dept., July, 1866; senr. clerk, Jan., 1880; asst. sec., mil. dept., May, 1895.

SMITH, GILBERT LESLIE, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Twyford Sch. and St. Andrew's Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 9th Nov., 1871, and served in the Punjab as asst.

commr., and settlt. officer; on famine duty in Bengal, 1874; district judge, Jan., 1887; divl. judge, Nov., 1889.

SMITH, HENRY ANDREW FELTON, Police Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Joined the service, 7th Aug., 1861; inspr. of police, April, 1866; asst. district supt., July, 1867; district supt., March, 1876.

SMITH, HENRY BABINGTON, M.A., Private Sec. to the Viceroy.—Educ. at Eton and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; examr. in the educ. dept. (England), Dec., 1887; private sec. to the chancellor of the exchequer (Mr. Goschen), May, 1891; clerk in the treasury, Aug., 1892; sec. to the British delegates at the Brussels monetary conference, Nov., 1892; private sec. to the viceroy, Feb., 1894.

SMITH, HENRY SCOTT, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Stratford-on-Avon, and Keble Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 4th Dec., 1886, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

SMITH, JOHN MACDONALD, Col., I.S.C., late Jail Dept., Madras.—First commn., Madras infantry, 20th April, 1855; served in the China expdn.; asst. to the agent in Vizagapatam, 1861-68; junr. asst. collr., Ganjam, 1870-78; supt. of jails, Rajamahendri, 1880-82; from June, 1882, served as paymaster of pensioners, Bangalore, and also acted as district mag. and prest. of the municipality; chief presidency mag., Madras, Dec., 1885; inspr.-gen. of jails, Mar., 1891; reverted to mil. dept., Aug., 1892; author of a hand-book in the Khond language.

SMITH, JOHN MANNERS, C.I.E., V.C., Capt., I.S.C., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—Joined the service, 10th March, 1883; attaché in the foreign dept., Nov., 1887; on special duty to Sikkim, in connection with the negotiations with the Chinese Amban, Nov., 1888; asst. to the officer on special duty at Gilgit, June, 1889; asst. to agent, Rajputana, Sept., 1892; on depn. to Kotah state, Oct., 1892; on duty under the orders of the British agent at Gilgit, April, 1893; on depn. with Kabul mission, Aug.-Dec., 1893; C.I.E., Jan., 1894; pol. agent, Bundelkhand, July, 1894; first asst. to agent in Baluchistan, Nov., 1894.

SMITH, JOSEPH, B.A., late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 25th Sept., 1861; from Sept., 1862, served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; mag. and collr., Aug., 1881; offg. district and sessions judge, July, 1886; ret'd., April, 1887.

SMITH, SIR ROBERT MURDOCH, K.C.M.G., Major-Gen., R.E., late Indo-European Telegraph Dept.—Apptd. to the dept., Aug., 1863; dir. of the Persian section from June, 1865; dir.-in-chief, March, 1887; ret'd., Feb., 1888.

SMITH, SAMUEL GEORGE, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office.—Junr. clerk, 6th Jan., 1883.

SMITH, SIDNEY, Lieut.-Col., R.E., India Public Works Dept.—First commn., 13th Jan., 1869; served in Egyptian expdn., 1882 (medal, clasp and star); joined the dept., 27th July, 1883, as exec. engr. and offg. dep. consulting engr., Madras; acting under sec. to govt., May, 1888; apptd. dep. consulting engr. and under sec. to govt., Feb., 1890; dep. consulting engr. for railways, Assam, Feb., 1893; in same capacity, Lucknow, Jan., 1895.

SMITH, THOMAS, B.A., late Bengal C.S.—

Educ. at Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 31st Oct., 1861; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., asst. commr., and dep. commr.; apptd. district and sessions judge, Tipperah, Dec., 1877, and served subsequently in the jud. dept.; retd., Jan., 1888.

SMITH, THOMAS WILLIAM, Store Dept., India Office.—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, India store depôt, 23rd May, 1888.

SMITH, TURTON, Police Dept., Punjab.—From 26th March, 1863, served in the dept. as asst. supt. and district supt.; between Dec., 1871, and Sept., 1878, also served in the postal dept. as offg. asst. dir.-gen. of post offices, and as offg. postmr.-gen. at Bombay, Aug., 1876, and at Madras, May, 1878; dep. inspr.-gen. of police, Aug., 1892; acted as inspr.-gen. in 1893-94.

SMITH, VINCENT ARTHUR, B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 2nd Nov., 1871, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; from Nov., 1874, served in the settlt. dept.; on special duty in the Allahabad district, Nov., 1880; served subsequently as asst. mag. and joint mag., and was also settlt. officer of Basti, 1883-84; mag. and collr., June, 1889; district and sessions judge, March, 1895; author of 'The Settlement Officer's Manual,' 'Coinage of the Early Gupta Dynasty,' 'Graeco-Roman Influence on the Civilization of Ancient India,' &c., and editor of Sleeman's 'Rambles and Recollections.'

SMITHE, EDMUND DU CANE, India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1875, and employed on irrig. works in the Punjab; exec. engr., Dec., 1883; transf. to buildings and road branch, Oct., 1894; has acted as under sec. to govt. of India, and to govt. of Punjab.

SMITHER, MAURICE, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Aldenham Gr. Sch. and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 9th Jan., 1895, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and offg. cantonmt. mag.

SMYTH, JOHN WATT, B.L., late Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Queen's Coll., Belfast; apptd. after exam. of 1857; arrived, 29th Dec., 1857, and was apptd. asst. mag. and collr., Agra; transf. to Punjab, May, 1859, and served as asst. commr., and as personal asst.; registrar of the chief court, Feb., 1866; dep. commr., Oct., 1869; judge, chief court, March, 1880; retd., 1885.

SMYTH, RALPH GRAHAM, Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Lieut., Bengal engrs., 7th Dec., 1855; from 6th Nov., 1857, served in the dept. in the Central and N.W. Prov. as asst. engr.; served in the Mutiny (medal and clasp); offg. garrison engr., Lucknow, June, 1859; on mil. duty, May, 1861; re-apptd., April, 1862, and served in Bengal as asst. engr. and exec. engr.; suptg. engr., July, 1876; transf. to Haidarabad, Dec., 1881, as sec. to rest. in the public works dept.; offg. chief engr. and sec. to chief commr., Burma, 1885; retd., Dec., 1889.

SMYTH, THOMAS WATT, M.A., B.L., late Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Queen's Coll., Belfast; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 2nd Feb., 1862, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and small cause court judge; registrar of the chief court, 1867-70; dep. commr., April, 1877; commr., April, 1883; divl. judge, Nov.,

1884; judge, chief court, Jan., 1887; retd., April, 1889; author of 'Acts and Regulations applicable to the Punjab.'

SMYTH, WILLIAM JOHN, B.A., Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at Bath Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 1st Jan., 1894, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

SNOW, PHILIP CHICHELEY HYDE, B.L., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Haileybury and Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 11th Nov., 1876; asst. commr., Delhi, Nov., 1876; from March, 1877, served in British Burma as asst. commr., asst. cantonmt. mag., &c.; from Nov., 1881, served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and district registrar; forest settlt. officer, Dec., 1885; acting under sec. to govt., pol., &c., depts., Sept., 1886; junr. collr. and mag., June, 1894.

SORABJI PADAMJI PESTANJI, Statutory C.S. (Bombay).—Probr. under the native civil service rules, 14th Jan., 1880; confd., March, 1882; served as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settlt. officer; 2nd asst., May, 1889; 1st asst., March, 1893.

SPANKIE, GEORGE THEOPHILUS, B.L., Oudh Jud. Commn.—Called to the bar (Lincoln's Inn), 6th June, 1871; apptd. addl. jud. commr. in Oudh, 11th Nov., 1892.

SPANKIE, JOHN PATRICK WALTER, Major, I.S.C., Jud. Dept., N.W. Prov.—First commn., 9th Aug., 1873; served during the Afghan war, 1877-78 (medal); offg. cantonmt. mag., Oct., 1881; confd., Sept., 1882.

SPEARMAN, HORACE RALPH, Col., I.S.C., Burma Commn.—Ensign, 91st Highlanders, 18th Dec., 1857; proby. asst. engr., public works dept., 1862; from Dec., 1862, served in the Burma commn. as asst. commr. and asst. sec. to the chief commr.; offg. asst. pol. agent, Bhamo, March, 1871; dep. commr., Jan., 1877; prest., Rangoon municipal committee, 1883, and 1886, and of the Akyab municipal committee, 1884; commr., April, 1890; author of 'British Burma Gazetteer.'

SPEDDING, ROBERT DECV, M.A., late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Queen's Coll., Belfast; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 17th Nov., 1861, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; dep. commr., Jhansi, April, 1879; mag. and collr., Dec., 1879; commr., Oct., 1889; retd., July, 1891.

SPENCE, JAMES KNOX, Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll. and Royal Mil. Acad., Woolwich; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 29th Oct., 1865, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; from May, 1878, to Oct., 1880, served in the postal dept.; junr. collr. and mag., and pol. agent, Kolaba, June, 1885; senr. collr. and collr. of land rev., &c., Bombay, Nov., 1888; acting commr. of customs, &c., Dec., 1889; also British delegate for the Portuguese treaty; commr., Nov., 1891; member of legisl. council, July, 1895.

SPENCER, CHARLES GORDON, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Marlborough and Keble Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 26th Nov., 1890, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

SPENCER, HUGH, Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Bristol Gr. Sch., and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 17th Dec., 1888, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Nov. 1893.

SPENCER, LIONEL DIXON, M.D., Surgn.-Col., Indian Med. Dept.—Asst. surgn., Bengal med. establ., March, 1865; served under the civil dept. in the Central Prov., and with the Central India horse; from Oct., 1871, served as agency surgn., E. states, Rajputana, on famine duty, 1877, and acted as pol. agent, Karauli, 1877, and Bhartpur, 1878; in 1879 was in med. charge of the Marwar and Jaisalmer agency; from March, 1881, served as agency surgn., Rajputana, and supt.-gen. of dispensaries and vaccn.; reverted to mil. dept., July, 1892.

SPILSBURY, EDGAR CHARLES, Major, R.E., India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. to Bombay public works dept. as asst. engr., 17th Aug., 1876; served in the Afghan war, 1879-80 (medal); employed on Bombay defences, March, 1883; exec. engr., March, 1886; transf. to Burma, Dec., 1893.

SPRING, FRANCIS JOSEPH EDWARD, C.I.E., M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. 4th Aug., 1870, and employed on irrign. works in the Punjab; transf. to state railways, May, 1873; exec. engr., Jan., 1880; under sec. to govt. of Bengal, railway branch, Sept., 1884; employed on N.W. line, March, 1888; engr.-in-chief, E. Coast railway, Nov., 1890; offg. consulting engr. for railways, Bombay, and sec. to govt., Aug., 1893; employed on Bezwada-Madras railway survey, Nov., 1893; consulting engr. to govt. of India for railways, Assam, Jan., 1894; C.I.E., May, 1894; rank of suptg. engr., July, 1894.

SPRING-BRANSON, JAMES HENRY, B.L., Madras Jud. Dept.—Crown prosecutor and public prosecutor for town of Madras, 4th July, 1885; advocate-gen., Madras, Sept., 1889; member of legisl. council, 1887, 1892, and 1894.

SPRY, ARTHUR HUME, late Bombay C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1857; arrived, 22nd Oct., 1857, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., special officer of income tax, and asst. pol. agent; junr. collr. and mag., Dhwarwar, Dec., 1878; senr. collr., Kaira, Oct., 1882; on special duty in the Cambay state, Feb., 1883; retd., 1886.

SRI LAL, Pandit, B.L., Statutory C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Apptd. by the govt. of India a prob. under the statutory civil service rules; asst. mag. and collr., 30th March, 1889; confd., 21st May, 1891; asst. settl. officer, Jan., 1894.

SRINAVASA RAGHAVA AIYANGAR, SESHAYANGAR, C.I.E., B.A., Dewan Bahadur, Madras Registrn. Dept.—Joined the service, Nov., 1870; head acctnt., rev. board's office, March, 1878; 2nd asst., Nov., 1879; and 1st asst., Aug., 1880; inspr.-gen. of registrn., Madras, Sept., 1889; C.I.E., June, 1893; member of legisl. council, Nov., 1893.

SRINIVASA RAO, PATEKI, Dewan Bahadur, Jud. Dept., Madras.—Served in the jud. dept. in various capacities from 16th Dec., 1844; apptd. police mag., Madras, May, 1871; fellow of Madras univ., 1875; small cause court judge, Madras, April, 1880; judge, Madras city civil court, Dec., 1892.

STACE, EDWARD VINCENT, C.B., Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Pol. Dept., Bombay.—Arrived, 13th Nov., 1862; from June, 1871, served in the Bombay rev. survey dept. as asst. supt., and as boundary settl. commr.; from May, 1873, acted as asst. pol. rest., Aden, and from April, 1875,

as asst. pol. agent, Kathiawar, &c.; second asst. rest. at Aden, Sept., 1880; asst. pol. agent, Kathiawar, Dec., 1881; joint administrator, Rajpipla, Feb., 1885; first asst. pol. rest. at Aden, and pol. agent for the Somali coast, Feb., 1889; acted as rest. at various periods; pol. agent and collr. of stamp rev., Cutch, Aug., 1893.

STACK, GEORGE AUGUSTINE, Bengal Educl. Dept.—Served from 1st Jan., 1868, as prof. at the Agra, Bareilly, and Dacca colls.; prof., Patna coll., Jan., 1883; presidency coll., Feb., 1888.

STACK, OLIVER STOKES, late Police Dept., Bengal.—From 8th June, 1859, served as dep. mag. and dep. collr.; from May, 1862, served in the police dept., and was apptd. district supt., 1st grade, Oct., 1878; dep. inspr.-gen., July, 1885; offg. inspr.-gen., June, 1886; retd., Oct., 1890.

STALEY, ALFRED EVELYN, B.L., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., Concord, U.S.A., and St. Peter's Coll., Radley; apptd. after exam. of 1873; arrived, 20th Oct., 1875, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Bengal, Jan., 1880; offg. under sec. to govt., 1884 and 1885; joint mag., Aug., 1886; acting junr. sec. to rev. board, March, 1890; district and sessions judge, Dec., 1891.

STANDEN, BERTRAM PRIOR, Indian C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at Uppingham, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 5th Dec., 1888, and served in Burma and the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; registrar, jud. commr.'s court, Nagpur, March, 1891; settl. officer, Dec., 1894.

STANSFELD, RIGHT HON. SIR JAMES, G.C.B., LL.B., B.L.—Born in 1820; educ. at Univ. Coll., London; called to the bar at the Inner Temple, 1849; M.P. for Halifax, 1859-95; a lord of the admiralty, 1863-64; under sec. of state for India, Feb. to June, 1866; third lord of the treasury, Dec., 1868; joint sec. to the treasury, Oct., 1869; prest. of the poor law board, March, 1871; prest. of the local govt. board, 1871-74, and again in 1886; G.C.B., 1895.

STANTIALL, WILLIAM, Public Works Dept., India Office.—Educ. at the City of London Sch.; clerk, 2nd class, 25th Sept., 1882.

STAVELEY, SIR CHARLES WILLIAM DUNBAR, G.C.B., Gen.—Educ. at the Scottish Naval and Mil. Acad., Edinburgh; 2nd lieut., 87th foot, March, 1835; asst. mil. sec. to Col. W. Staveley, in Mauritius, 1842, and at Hong Kong, 1847-50; A.D.C. to lieut.-gen., Earl Cathcart, in Canada, 1845-47; served in the Crimean campaign of 1854-55 (medal, C.B., Sardinian and Turkish medals, and 5th class Medjidie); brigadier-gen. in N. China, 1860-62 (medal), and was in command of the troops during the operations against the Taepings, 1862-63; K.C.B., March, 1865; from March, 1865, to Sept., 1867, served as brigadier-gen. in the Bombay presidency; second in command in the Abyssinian expedn., 1867-68 (thanks of parliament and medal); major-gen. on the staff, western divn., Devonport, 1869; commander-in-chief, Bombay, and member of the govr.'s council, Oct., 1874, to Oct., 1878; G.C.B., May, 1884.

STEAD, VEN. SAMUEL, M.A. (Oxon.), late Ecclesi. Dept., Bombay.—Joined the service, March, 1863; arrived, 24th April, 1863, and

served as chaplain at Karachi, Sholapur, Belgaum, and Poona; senr. chaplain, April, 1873; archdeacon and commissary, Nov., 1878; held charge of the diocese of Bombay, 1877-78, and 1883-84; ret'd., June, 1886.

STEANE, WILLIAM COPLE, Registry and Record Dept., India Office.—Temporary clerk, 9th Nov., 1860; clerk, 2nd class, Aug., 1878.

STEAVERSON, JOSEPH, B.A., Jud. Dept., Madras.—Served from 25th Feb., 1878, as acting registrar of the high court (original side), Madras, and was confd. in this appt., Feb., 1880.

STEDMAN, EDWARD, C.B., Major-Gen., I.S.C.—First commn., 19th Dec., 1860; served in the Hazara campaign, 1868 (medal with clasp); also in the Afghan war of 1878-80 (medal with clasp and bronze star); served in the Burma expdn. of 1886-87, and commanded the operations in the Shan states (two clasps; C.B.); inspr.-gen. of police, Upper Burma, May, 1887; and of Burma, Oct., 1888; apptd. quartermaster-gen. in India, March, 1892.

STEEDMAN, EDWARD BRITTAIN, Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Shrewsbury; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 31st Oct., 1871, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and settl. officer; dir. of settlts. and rev. records, May, 1887; designation changed to dir. of land records and agric.; dep. commr., Dec., 1889; in 1892 offd. as dir. of land records and agric., and rev. sec. to govt.; author of 'Report on the Settlement of the Jhang District.'

STEEL, CHARLES DENTON, B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at the Wesleyan Connexional Sch. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 13th Nov., 1876, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; asst. supt. and judge, small cause court, Dehra Dun, in 1888-89; asst. commr., April, 1889; joint mag., Jan., 1890; district and sessions judge, April, 1891; dep. commr., April, 1892; mag. and collr., March, 1894; author of 'Allahabad Gazetteer.'

STEEL, HENRY WILLIAM, late Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 10th Nov., 1862, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and dep. commr.; employed in connection with Punjab N. state railway, July, 1874; jud. asst., Dec., 1878; dep. commr., Nov., 1884; ret'd., May, 1889.

STEEL, JOHN PHILIP, Lieut.-Gen., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Lieut., Bengal engrs., Dec., 1855; joined the dept., March, 1860, as asst. engr., Bengal; exec. engr., April, 1868; offg. under sec., govt. of India, public works dept., 1873-75, and again Feb., 1877; suptg. engr. and sec. to agent, Rajputana, May, 1879; offg. chief engr. and sec. to govt., Punjab, March, 1887; offg. sec. to chief commr., Central Prov., Oct., 1887; chief engr. and joint sec. to govt., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, 1888; sec., Dec., 1889; ret'd., March, 1892.

STEELE, CHARLES EDWARD STAFFORD, Sind Commn.—Educ. at Victoria Coll., Jersey; joined the service, 11th June, 1869, and served in Sind as supernumerary asst. settl. officer; supernumerary dep. collr., Dec., 1871; employed under the Transvaal govt. as fin. and rev. commr., 1879-81; served subsequently as asst. collr. and mag., manager of encumbered estates, acting city mag., Karachi, and acting conservator

of forests; head asst. collr., Feb., 1892; dep. commr., upper Sind frontier, Feb., 1894.

STEINBELT, JEAN MARI CONRAD, late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 31st Oct., 1861; from Jan., 1863, served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., asst. commr., and joint mag.; from April, 1881, served as civil and sessions judge in Banda, and afterwards at Azamgarh; ret'd., April, 1887.

STEINBERG, ALFRED FREDERICK, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1879; arrived, 12th Dec., 1881, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; transfd. to Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., Jan., 1892; joint mag., March, 1892; district and sessions judge, March, 1895.

STENT, WILLIAM KITSON, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Apptd., 14th April, 1871, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., April, 1880; engr.-in-chief, Bellary-Kistna railway, in 1883 and 1886; services lent to S. Indian railway co., 1891; engr.-in-chief, Delhi-Minchinabad railway survey, Nov., 1892; suptg. engr., Oct., 1894; engr.-in-chief, Kotri-Rohri railway, Oct., 1894.

STEPHEN, ARTHUR, M.A., M.B., Surgn.-Col., Med. Dept., Punjab.—Joined the service as prob. at Netley, 30th Sept., 1867; served under the mil. dept. in Abyssinia and the Punjab as asst. surgn. and surgn.; acted at various periods as civil surgn., jail supt., and chemical examr. to the Punjab govt.; supt. of jail, Lahore, Sept., 1875; statistical officer to govt. of India, sanitary and med. depts., Nov., 1880; sanitary commr., Punjab, Nov., 1886; acting principal med. officer and sanitary commr., Assam, March, 1894.

STEPHEN, CARR, B.L., late Punjab Jud. Dept.—Served in the Punjab from 11th June, 1856, as extra asst. commr. and small cause court judge; extra jud. asst. commr., 1st grade, Oct., 1875; offg. divl. judge, 1885-86; ret'd., 1890; has publ. 'Indian Registration Act,' 'Handbook for Delhi,' and 'Archæology of Delhi.'

STEPHEN, KENT HUME, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd., 24th Sept., 1878, and employed on irrign. works in Bengal; exec. engr., March, 1889; under sec. to govt. of Bengal, public works dept., March, 1892, to March, 1895.

STERNDALE, R. A., late Finl. Dept., Govt. of India.—Served during the Mutiny as a volunteer, 1857 (medal); from Aug., 1859, served in the Central Prov. as dep. collr. and asst. settl. officer; from June, 1864, served in the finl. dept. in various capacities in Nagpur, Calcutta, and the Punjab; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, Jan., 1884, and Madras, Nov., 1887; ret'd., 1890; author of 'Mammalia of British India and Ceylon,' 'Denizens of the Jungles,' and 'Account of the District of Seoni,' 'Turkey and India; or Our Indian Moslems,' and other works.

STEVENS, CHARLES CECIL, C.S.I., B.A., Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Univ. of Melbourne; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 2nd Feb., 1862; served in Bengal, and was apptd. mag. and collr., Oct., 1873; offg. sec., board of rev., April, 1882; commr., Aug., 1885;

offg. chief sec. to govt. of Bengal, 1890; member of rev. board, Dec., 1891; member of supreme legisl. council, Jan., 1893; C.S.I., Jan., 1895.

STEVENS, JOHN FOSTER, Indian C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at Birmingham Gr. Sch., at Versailles, and at King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 16th Oct., 1865, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., May, 1876; district and sessions judge, Sept., 1884; jud. commr., Central Prov., April, 1894; acting judge, high court, Calcutta, Feb., 1895.

STEVENSON, FRANCIS, Registry and Record Dept., India Office.—Apptd. supplemental clerk, 1st Jan., 1893.

STEVENSON, GEORGE, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at King's Coll., Aberdeen, and Glasgow Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 11th Nov., 1872, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and in Assam as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Sylhet, Jan., 1885; returned to Bengal, June, 1889, as joint mag.; mag. and collr., July, 1889; acted as commr., Orissa, in 1895.

STEVENSON - MOORE, CHARLES JAMES, Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Felsted Sch., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 10th Dec., 1887, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Aug., 1894; also asst. settl. officer, Dec., 1894.

STEWART, ARTHUR BENNET, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Rugby and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 5th Nov., 1870, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; second asst. collr., Dec., 1882; acting postmr.-gen., Bombay, 1884-85; acting asst. judge, Poona, and asst. agent for sardars in the Deccan, Oct., 1885; jud. asst. to pol. agent, Kathiawar, Nov., 1887; judge and sessions judge, May, 1891.

STEWART, ALFRED JOSEPH, Registry and Record Dept., India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, pol. dept., 27th Sept., 1892; transfld. to registry and record dept., Jan., 1896.

STEWART, SIR DONALD MARTIN, Bart., G.C.B., G.C.S.I., C.I.E., Field-Marshal.—First commn., Bengal army, 12th Oct., 1840; served against the hill tribes on the Peshawar frontier in 1854-55, and against the Bussee Kheyl tribes in 1855 (medal and clasp); commanded volunteers in Aligarh district, May and June, 1857; dep. asst. adjutant-gen., Delhi field force, June, 1857; asst. adjutant-gen. of army, Dec., 1857; was present at siege and capture of Lucknow, and the operations in Rohilkhand (medal with two clasps); dep. adjutant-gen. in India, Feb., 1863; brigadier-gen., Abyssinian expedy. force, 1868 (medal and C.B.); in command of Peshawar district, July, 1869; chief commr. of the Andaman and Nicobar Islands, 1871; in command of Lahore divn., April, 1876; commanding Kandahar and S. Afghanistan force, 1878-80; commanding at Kabul and in N. Afghanistan, 1880, till withdrawal from that country (thanks of both houses of parliament; K.C.B., G.C.B., bart., and medal with clasp); mil. member of govr.-gen.'s council, 1880; commander-in-chief in India, and extraordinary member of the govr.-gen.'s council, 1881; member of council of India, Dec., 1885; on expiration of his term of office in Dec., 1895, was re-apptd. for a further period of five years; field-marshal, May, 1894; member of royal commn. on Indian expenditure, May, 1895.

STEWART, ROBERT BARTON, B.A., Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Dulwich and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 27th Dec., 1886, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., Feb., 1893.

STEWART, WILLIAM HALL MACKINTOSH, Capt., I.S.C., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., 25th May, 1878; from June, 1889, acted as attaché, foreign dept., and asst. sec. to govt.; confd. in former post, Oct., 1890; asst. agent, Indore, Jan., 1891; asst. rest., Kashmir, July, 1891; asst. pol. agent, Gilgit, April, 1893.

STEWART-WILSON, CHARLES, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Edinburgh High Sch., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 23rd Dec., 1886, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Dec., 1890; under sec. to govt., Aug., 1891; from April, 1892, served as offg. postmr.-gen., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Bengal (1893-94), and Bombay (1894); offg. dep. dir. of post office, Oct., 1894.
