Sandeman, 1875-77; asst. to govr.-gen.'s agent, Baluchistan, 1877; served in the Afghan campaign, 1878-80 (medal); C.S.I., Feb., 1881; asst. to the rest. in Mysore, and sec. to chief commr., Coorg, 1882; pol. agent, Bhopawar, Feb., 1885; Jhalawar, April, 1885; Bhopal, March, 1887; rest., Nepal, Oct., 1893.

WYLLIE, WILLIAM HUTT CURZON, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—Ensign, 106th Foot, Oct., 1866; arrived, Feb., 1867; served in Oudh, 1870-79, as asst. commr.; acted as inspr.-gen. of registrn. in 1875, and as junr. sec. to govt., 1876-77; from Jan., 1879, served under the foreign dept., as cantonmt. mag., Nasirabad, asst. commr. in Merwara and Ajmir, and as asst. to govr.-gen.'s agent, Baluchistan; served in Madras, 1880-81, as mil. sec. to the govr., and acted as private sec.; C.I.E., May, 1881; pol. agent, in charge of ex-Gaekwar of Baroda, Mulhar Rao, 1881; asst. rest., Hyderabad, 1882; asst. commr., Ajmir, Nov., 1883; 1st asst. agent, Rajputana, 1884; addl. pol. agent, Kotah, April, 1885; boundary settl. officer,

Mewar-Marwar border, Nov., 1886; pol. agent, Kotah, Jan., 1889; offg. commr., Ajmir, July, 1891; offg. pol. agent, Jhalawar, in 1891 and 1892; offg. rest., states of Rajputana, May, 1893; rest., Mewar, March, 1894.

WYNCH, LIONEL MALING, B.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 7th Dec., 1885, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sec. to land rev. commrs., Feb., 1894.

WYNDHAM, PERCY, B.A., Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Giggleswick Gr. Sch. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 13th Dec., 1889, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr.; joint mag., Aug., 1893; dep. supt. of family domains of Maharaja of Benares, April, 1894.

WYNNE, SYDENHAM HENRY, B.A., Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at the Royal Naval Sch., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 21st Oct., 1873, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst. to the collr. and mag., S. Arcot, Oct., 1882; sub-collr. and joint mag., Dec., 1887; collr. and mag., Jan., 1890.

YATE, CHARLES EDWARD, C.S.I., C.M.G., Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., 9th Nov., 1867; arrived, 27th Feb., 1868; from Aug., 1871, served under the foreign dept.; on special duty, Sind border, 1872 and 1873; boundary settl. officer, Mewar-Marwar border, Sept., 1874; and Mewar-Tonk border, Dec., 1875; offd. for various periods as pol. asst., asst. to supt. for suppression of thagi and dakaity, &c.; supt. of operations for control of Moghias, Sept., 1879; served in Afghanistan, 1880-81 (medal and clasp, and bronze star); subsequently served in the pol. dept. in Bhopal, Central India, and Bhopawar; attaché with Afghan boundary commn., July, 1884; on duty at Herat in connection with the fortifications, 1885; employed on various special services in connection with the Afghan boundary commn.; pol. agent, Thal Chotali, March, 1890; offg. rev. commr., Baluchistan, Oct., 1892; pol. agent, Zhob, March, 1893; on special duty on Russo-Afghan frontier, April, 1893; acting govr.-gen.'s agent and consul-gen., Khorassan and Seistan, Sept., 1893; author of gazetteers of Mewar, Banswara and Partabgarh, 'Northern Afghanistan,' &c.

YELD, HORACE PARR, Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Bengal Med. Service.—Joined the Indian med. dept., 1874; was employed in the mil. dept. up to Aug., 1884, when he was apptd. prob. in the assay dept., Bombay mint; transf. to Calcutta mint, Sept., 1884; dep. assay-master, Bombay mint, May, 1886, and Calcutta mint, Jan., 1888; dep. assay-master, Bombay mint, May, 1892; ret'd., Nov., 1894.

YOUNG, GEORGE EDGAR, Registry and Record Dept., India Office.—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 22nd May, 1888.

YOUNG, GEORGE GORDON, Col., I.S.C., late Punjab Commn.—Arrived, 23rd Oct., 1856; from June, 1858, served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and asst. settl. officer; dep. commr.,

Jan., 1873; commr. and supt., Jan., 1881; offg. 2nd. and finl. commr., 1888 and 1890; reverted to mil. dept., 1892.

YOUNG, JAMES, Bombay Public Works Dept.—Joined the service, 21st Dec., 1868, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., July, 1879.

YOUNG, MATTHEW, Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 25th Nov., 1892, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

YOUNG, WILLIAM, C.S.I., B.A., B.L., late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Heidelberg Univ. and Univ. Coll., London; called to the bar at the Middle Temple; apptd. after exam. of 1856; arrived, 16th Dec., 1856, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag., and mag. and collr.; civil and sessions judge, Oct., 1880; jud. commr., Oudh, Aug., 1886; acting judge of high court, April-Dec., 1890; C.S.I., Jan., 1891; ret'd., Dec., 1891.

YOUNG, WILLIAM MACKWORTH, C.S.I., M.A., Indian C.S.—Educ. at Eton, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 12th Dec., 1863, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., asst. settl. officer, and offg. sec. to finl. commr.; offg. under sec. to govt., Oct., 1873; settl. sec. to finl. commr., March, 1875; dep. commr., Jan., 1878; offg. supt., Kapurthala state, Feb., 1878; sec. to govt., Punjab, Jan., 1880; commr. and supt., May, 1887; offg. sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Jan., 1888; commr., Jalandhar divn., April, 1888; 2nd finl. commr. of the Punjab, April, 1889; 1st commr., Feb., 1893; C.S.I., Jan., 1890; member of viceroy's legisl. council, June-Nov., 1893; prest. of the Indian hemp drugs commn., Oct., 1893; rest., Mysore, and chief commr. of Coorg, Sept., 1895.

YOUNGHUSBAND, ARTHUR DELAVAL, Indian C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at Rugby, and Clifton Coll.; apptd. to the Bombay civil service after exam. of 1875; arrived, 9th Dec., 1877, and served as asst. collr. and mag., talukdari settl. officer, and asst. pol. agent; acted as private sec. to govr. in 1890; and as administrator, Rajpipla state, in 1888 and 1890-91; transf. to Central Prov. as asst. commr., Nov., 1891; dep. commr., Jan., 1892; also pol. agent, Chhattisgarh feudatories, Nov., 1893.

YOUNGHUSBAND, ROMER EDWARD, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Rugby, Clifton Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 14th Dec., 1879, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and settl. officer; junr. sec. to finl. commrs., March, 1886; under sec. to govt., Jan., 1888, to April, 1890; offg. junr. sec., May, 1893.

YUSUF, MOHAMMAD, Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at the Patna Coll. Sch., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 12th Nov., 1892, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

ZORAB, JOHN MANOOK, M.B., C.M., Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., Bengal Med. Service.—Joined the service, 30th March, 1872; served in the mil. dept., and as civil surgn., Balasor, March, 1887, and Cuttack, March, 1891.

LIST OF CASUALTIES.

In addition to those mentioned in the casualty lists given in the earlier part of the book, the following officers have died since the last issue.

BATESON, ROBERT SANDER, Dep. Surgn.-Gen., Med. Dept., Punjab (ret'd.).—Died, 24th Oct., 1895.

BROWN, FRANCIS DAVID MILLETT, V.C., Col., I.S.C., India Public Works Dept. (ret'd.).—Died, 21st Nov., 1895.

CARSWELL, EBENEZER ERSKINE ANDERSON, India Public Works Dept.—Died, —, 1894.

CHESNEY, SIR GEORGE TOMKYNS, K.C.B., C.S.I., C.I.E., Gen., R.E.—Died, 31st March, 1895.

CHURCHILL, RIGHT HON. LORD RANDOLPH SPENCER.—Died, 24th Jan., 1895.

CLOETE, HENRY DANIEL, Major-Gen., I.S.C., Madras Police Dept. (ret'd.).—Died, 2nd June, 1895.

COATES, JOHN MARTIN, M.D., Brigade-Surgn., Bengal Med. Dept. (ret'd.).—Died, 10th July, 1895.

CONWAY-GORDON, LEWIS, C.I.E., Col., R.E., India Public Works Dept. (ret'd.).—Died, 25th June, 1895.

DALY, SIR HENRY DOMINICK, G.C.B., C.I.E., Gen.—Died, 21st July, 1895.

HARMAN, CHARLES, India Audit Office.—Died, 5th March, 1895.

HODGSON, JOSEPH, India Audit Office.—Died, 13th Feb., 1895.

JACKSON, CHARLES J. J., M.D., Dep. Surgn.-Gen., Bengal Med. Dept. (ret'd.).—Died, 16th Jan., 1895.

JOBBINS, WILLIAM HENRY, Bengal Educn. Dept.—Died, 18th Sept., 1895.

JOHNSTONE, SIR JAMES, K.C.S.I., Col., Assam Commn. (ret'd.).—Died, 14th June, 1895.

LA TOUCHE, HENRY CHRISTOPHER DIGGES, India Public Works Dept. (ret'd.).—Died, 16th Feb., 1895.

LAWRENCE, RICHARD CHARLES, C.B., Gen.—Died, 24th Jan., 1896.

LESMOND, WILLIAM ALOYSIUS, India Public Works Dept.—Died, 1st Aug., 1895.

LIMOND, DAVID, C.B., Major-Gen., R.E., India Public Works Dept. (ret'd.).—Died, 10th July, 1895.

MACKENZIE, SIR WILLIAM, K.C.B., C.S.I., Insp.-Gen. of Hosps.—Died, 29th Oct., 1895.

MAYO, JOHN HENRY, Mil. Dept., India Office.—Died, 29th Sept., 1895.

MILES, THOMAS WILLIAM, India Public Works Dept.—Died, 11th June, 1895.

MORRIS, MATTHEW, Punjab Police Dept. (ret'd.).—Died, 8th Feb., 1895.

MUNRO, WELLESLEY HERBERT, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept., India Office.—Died, 25th Jan., 1896.

MUTHUSWAMI AIYAR, SIR TIRUVARUR, K.C.I.E., Jud. Dept., Madras.—Died, 25th Jan., 1895.

NASH, ALFRED MOSES, Educn. Dept., Bengal.—Died, 3rd June, 1895.

NAYLOR, FREDERICK ALEXANDER, Police Dept., Central Prov.—Died, 1st Nov., 1894.

PELLY, SAVILLE MARRIOTT, C.B., Insp.-Gen. of Hosps.—Died, 3rd April, 1895.

RAJENDRA CHANDRA CHANDRA, Brigade-Surgn., Bengal Med. Dept. (ret'd.).—Died, 14th Dec., 1895.

RAWLINSON, SIR HENRY CRESWICK, Bart., G.C.B., Major-Gen.—Died, 5th March, 1895.

SCORGIE, JAMES, Educ. Dept., Bombay (ret'd.).—Died, 15th July, 1895.

STRATTON, JOHN PROUDFOOT, M.D., Brigade-Surgn., Bombay Med. Dept. (ret'd.).—Died, 8th Aug., 1895.

WHISHAW, JOHN CHARLES, M.D., Surgn.-Major, N.W. Prov. Med. Dept. (ret'd.).—Died, 20th Jan., 1895.

WILKINSON, ALFRED REGINALD, Lieut.-Col., Bengal Police Dept. (ret'd.).—Died, 17th June, 1895.

CORRECTION IN LIST OF CASUALTIES IN 1895 EDITION.

For ROSS, ALEXANDER, read ROSS, ANDREW.
